name: Single Leg Hip Thrust
instructions: 1) Grab a bench or step and lay down on your back (face up). Have your torso flat on the bench with your arms by your sides. One of your feet should be planted on the floor with your knees bent at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees), the other should be extended towards the ceiling as vertical as possible. This is your starting position.

2) To initiate the movement, exhale and drive through your heel to lift your hips toward the ceiling by squeezing your glutes. As you do so, lean back onto your shoulders and keep driving upward until your glutes are fully contracted.

3) After a brief pause, inhale and gently relax your glutes to drop your pelvis towards the floor (the resistance should be placed through your heel). Continue to drop your pelvis until your glutes touch the floor.
4) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Lateral Squat to Cross Body Row
instructions: 1) Grab a resistance band.
2) Have a wide leg stance.
3) Place one end of the band under one foot.
4) With the other hand grab the other end of the band.
5) Transfer weight over towards the foot with the band under and reach the arm towards the foot then come back up to standing and pull the band into a high row position with elbow leading the move.
6) Repeat as many repetitions as prescribed.

name: Banded Squats
instructions: 1) Place a small band around the thighs just above the knee.
2) Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

3) Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

4) After a brief pause, exhale and return to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
5) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Banded Squat Jump
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knee.

2) Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your toes pointing slightly outward. Allow your arms to hang comfortably by your sides at this point. Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

3) Inhale and descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Bring your arms out in front of you so that they are also parallel to the floor at this point. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

4) Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, exhale and push hard through your heels to drive your hips forward. As you extend at your knee and ankle joints in order to jump as high as you can, pull your arms down towards the sides of your torso at the top of the jump (this will help to generate more height), before landing into your next rep.
5) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Banded Russian Twists
instructions: 1) Grab a band and place it round the bottom of the feet.
2) Sitting on the floor holding onto the other end of the band, lift the feet off the ground and lean back slightly keeping the head and chest lifted, back straight and abs engaged.
3) Slowly rotate to each side drawing the band towards the hips. After each rep return to the middle and then to the other side.
4) Repeat for the prescribed repetitions.

name: Banded Reverse Lunge
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knees whilst in a standing position. This will be your starting position.
2) Take a step backwards (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Whilst maintaining a straight torso.
3) Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
4) As you exhale, use mainly the heel of your front foot and push off the floor towards the starting position again.
5) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and then change sides.

name: Banded Lateral Step Out Squat
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knees.

2) Starting with feet hip distance apart brace the abs and step out laterally dropping into a squat position by bending the knees and hinging at the hips. Keep the chest lifted and sit back into the heels whilst pushing your knees into the band.
3) Step back to the starting position by pushing out of the ground and then repeat on the other side.
4) Repeat for prescribed repetitions.

name: Banded Kickbacks
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it round the ankles.
2) Take a small step back.
3) Raise the leg squeezing the glutes as you lift and slightly lean forwards.
4) Keep abs engaged and chest lifted throughout.
5) Return the leg back to the start position and then repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
6) Do the same on the other leg.

name: Banded Glute Bridge Pulses
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it around the thighs just above the knees.
2) Lay on your back with knees bent and feet hip distance apart, knees directly over ankles.
3) Pushing knees into the band and keeping abs braced squeeze your glutes and drive hips up until you have full glute contraction at the top.
4) Pulse up and down for 3 reps driving heels into the floor and then return all the way down to the floor and repeat for prescribed repetitions.

name: Banded Fire Hydrant
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it round the thighs just above the knees.
2) Adopt an all fours position on the floor with hands directly under the shoulders and knees directly under the hips.
3) Brace abs and keep hips as stable as possible whilst keeping the leg bent and lifting it up out to the side, squeezing the glutes and leading with the knee.
4) Return to the starting position and then repeat for prescribed repetitions, then do the same on the other leg.

name: Banded Face Pull
instructions: 1) Grab a band and tie one end around something stable at around shoulder height.
2) Take hold of the other end of the band with both hands and step slightly back so there is some tension in the band.
3) With palms facing down, leading with the elbows move the band towards your face keeping the elbows high. Squeeze the shoulder blades together.
4) Return back to the start position and repeat for the prescribed repetitions.

name: Banded Duck Walks
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it round your ankles, feet just wider than hip distance.
2) Squat down with hips below knees and chest up.
3) Walk forward in this low position keeping abs braced and chest up.
4) Keep distributing weight through the feet evenly.
5) Repeat for prescribed repetitions.

name: Banded Donkey Kicks
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place one side round one ankle and the other side round the sole of the other foot.
2) Adopt an all fours position on the floor with hands directly under the shoulders and knees under the hips.
3) Brace abs and keep hips as stable as possible.
4) With the leg that has the band around the sole of the foot, flex the foot and push the heel towards the ceiling until you get full glute contraction without moving the hips.
5) Come back down until the knee is nearly back on the floor and repeat for the prescribed repetitions.
6) Complete on the other leg.

name: Banded Crab Walks
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it around both ankles, hip distance apart.
2) Squat down bending the knees and hinging at the hips.
3) Sit into your heels and keep chest lifted and abs braced throughout.
4) Move laterally by pushing against the band and taking small steps.
5) With the other leg resist the band as you step in.
6) Maintain the position and squeeze glutes throughout.
7) Repeat for the prescribed repetitions on one leg and then complete on the other side.

name: Banded Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: 1) Grab a small band and place it around the thighs just above the knees.
2) Set up a bench or a step and barbell.
3) Take a seat on the floor with your legs underneath the bar, your back should be supported by a bench or an elevated step with your shoulder blades being the point of contact.
4) Roll the bar up and over your legs until it is directly above your hips, bend at your knees and walk your feet in towards your glutes.

5) Whilst exhaling, push your knees into the band, squeeze your glutes and drive your heels to lift your hips towards the ceiling. As you do so, keep your chin on your chest keep driving upward until your glutes are fully contracted and your body is parallel with the floor.
6) Inhale and gently relax your glutes to return the barbell back along the same path to the starting position.
7) Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Banded Abductors
instructions: 1) Stand with feet hip-width apart and a resistance band looped around both ankles.
2) Engage your core and maintain a slight bend in your knees.
3) Lift one leg out to the side squeezing the glutes, stretching the band, then return to the starting position. Repeat for prescribed repetitions and then repeat on the other side.

name: Russian Twist with Kettlebell
instructions: 1) Sit on the floor with your knees bent, holding a kettlebell with both hands close to your chest.
2) Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
3) Twist your torso to one side, lowering the kettlebell towards the floor beside your hip.
4) Return to the centre and twist to the opposite side, alternating sides for each repetition.
5) Focus on controlled movements keeping the chin off the chest and engage your core throughout.
This exercise targets your obliques and improves core strength and rotational stability.

name: Plate Bicep Curl
instructions: 1) Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight plate with both hands, palms facing in.
2) Keep elbows close to your sides, curl the plate towards your shoulders.
3) Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the plate back down.
4) Repeat for desired reps.
5) Focus on controlled movements and engage your biceps throughout. This exercise targets your biceps and improves arm strength.

name: Kettlebell Bicep Curl
instructions: 1) Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at arm's length, palms facing forward.

3) Pause briefly at the top, then lower the kettlebells back down with control.
4) Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and engaging your biceps throughout.

name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
instructions: 1) Sit on the ground with your upper back against a bench and a dumbbell across your hips.
2) Hold the dumbbell securely and plant your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
3) Press through your heels to lift your hips up until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
4) Pause briefly at the top, then lower your hips back down.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
6) Focus on squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.

name: Alternating Reverse Jump Lunges
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
2) Step back with one leg into a reverse lunge, lowering your body until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
3) Push through your front heel and jump immediately switching legs, performing a reverse lunge on the other side.
4) Continue alternating legs with each repetition.
5) Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout the movement.
This exercise targets your legs, glutes, and core while also improving balance and coordination.

name: Bodyweight Reverse Lunge
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
2) Step back with one foot and lower your body until both knees are bent at a 90-degree angle.
3) Pause briefly, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
4) Switch legs and repeat.
5) Focus on keeping your torso upright and engaging your core for stability. This exercise targets your legs and glutes.

name: Crab Walks
instructions: 1) Start in a seated position with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Place your hands under shoulders.
2) Lift your hips off the ground, forming a tabletop position with your torso and thighs parallel to the floor.
3) Begin to walk forwards by taking small steps with your hands and feet, moving in one direction.
4) Maintain tension in your core and keep your hips lifted throughout the movement.
5) Continue walking for the desired distance or number of steps.
6) Reverse direction and walk back to your starting point.
7) Focus on controlled movements and keep your core engaged to target your glutes and hip muscles.

name: Duck Walks
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart and toes pointed slightly outwards.
2) Lower into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
3) Take small steps forward, staying low to the ground and maintaining the squat position.
4) Continue walking forward for a set distance or number of steps.
5) Focus on keeping your core engaged and your weight distributed evenly on both feet.
6) Reverse the movement to walk backward if desired.
Duck walks target your quads, glutes, and hip flexors while improving lower body strength and mobility.

3) Pause briefly, then lower your hips back down.

name: Quad Dominant Lunges
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
2) Take a small step forward with one foot, bending both knees to lower your body.
3) As you lunge, focus on driving your front knee forward over the toes.
4) Keep your torso upright and core engaged.
5) As you stand up think about driving the knee back.
6) Repeat for prescribed amount of reps on one side.
7) Repeat on the other leg.
This exercise targets your quads and improves lower body strength.

4) Keep your torso upright and core engaged.
5) As you stand up think about driving the knee back.
6) Repeat for prescribed amount of reps on one side.
7) Repeat on the other leg.
This exercise targets your quads and improves lower body strength.

name: Hip Dominant Lunges
instructions: Start in a split stance keeping your feet roughly hip-width apart.
Lunge down keeping your weight above your leading leg.
As you stand back up from the lunge, aim to shift your weight back more towards your hips, creating a more vertical shin angle and forward lean.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Single Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Hold a dumbbell by your thigh, keeping your other arm free.
Lift one leg off the ground and move the foot behind you whilst leaning forwards slightly.
Keeping your back straight, hinge at the hips to lean further forward, raising your back leg behind you to maintain balance.
Lower until you feel the stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to the starting position.

name: Landmine Deadlift
instructions: Place a barbell in a landmine attachment and load the free end of the bar with weight plates.
If your gym doesn't have a landmine attachment you can wedge the end of the barbell in a corner and place a heavy dumbbell over the bar to help keep it in place.
Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, holding the end of the barbell with both hands supinated.
Keeping your arms extended below you and back straight, keep your core and lats braced and stand up from the lengthened position.
In a controller manner, bring the bar back down keeping your posture tight and chest up.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Landmine Shoulder Press

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell with one hand at shoulder height
Keeping the bar at shoulder height, press the bar up and forwards so that your arm is extended with a slight bend in the elbow at the top of the rep.
In a controller manner, bring the barbell back down to shoulder height to complete the rep.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Landmine Squat
instructions: Place a barbell in a landmine attachment and load the free end of the bar with weight plates.
If your gym doesn't have a landmine attachment you can wedge the end of the barbell in a corner and place a heavy dumbbell over the bar to help keep it in place.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell with both hands at chest height
Keeping the bar at chest height, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive up, keeping your quadriceps and glutes engaged throughout the rep.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Squat Hold
instructions: From standing, place your feet shoulder width apart with your toes pointing slightly outwards. This will be your starting position
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle and hold your position.
Hold this position for the recommended amount of time.
When it's time to return to standing, exhale and drive through your heels to stand up.

name: Heel Elevated Dumbbell Squat
instructions: Place a foam block or two weight plates on the ground, put your heels on the edge of the wedge/plates to create a shoulder width stance.
Once in the correct position, step onto the platform so that your heels are on the edge.
From here, squat down to grab your dumbbells. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive up, engaging your quadriceps and glutes throughout the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Heel Elevated Goblet Squat
instructions: Place a foam block or two weight plates on the ground, put your heels on the edge of the wedge/plates to create a shoulder-width stance.

Squat up, keeping a neutral posture. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly squat down until your upper and lower legs make a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heels to drive up, engaging your quadriceps and glutes in order to do so.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Heel Elevated Barbell Back Squat

name: Battle Rope Alternating Waves (Single Arm)
instructions: For this exercise you will need a heavy rope anchored at its center 15-20 feet away. Standing in front of the rope, take an end one hand with your arm extended at your side. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by rapidly raising one arm to shoulder level as quickly as you can.
As you let that arm drop to the starting position, raise the opposite side.
Continue alternating your left and right arms, whipping the ropes up and down as fast as you can.

Make sure the band will not slip off your foot, slowly pull harder on the band so that the stretch becomes greater until you create the desired amount of tension.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

name: Sandbag - Alternating Static Overhead Side Lunges
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.

Take an exaggerated step out to one side and plant your foot with your toes slightly pointing outwards. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement so that you minimise the risk of injury. Inhale, push your hips backward and bend the knee of the foot you have stepped out with in order to lower your weight over that thigh. Once your thigh is parallel with the floor, pause for a brief moment, exhale and push through the heel and thigh from that planted foot.
Throughout this process the leg that remained in the starting position should be straight.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Leg Press (Wide Stance).

name: Sandbag - Cleans
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a slightly wider than shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bag is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the sandbag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.
Descend the sandbag back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure you keep the bag close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Cleans or Kettlebell Cleans.

name: Sandbag - Decline Lat Pullovers
instructions: Grab the sandbag on each side and lay on a decline bench with the sandbag above your chest. Lift, extending so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows slightly bent. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bag over and beyond your head by bending at your elbows until your upper arms are in line with your torso.
After a brief pause, exhale and bring the bag back into the starting position above your chest.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Pullover, Dumbbell Pullovers, Straight Arm Pulldowns Rope.

name: Sandbag - Decline Narrow Grip Chest Press
instructions: Grab a sandbag with your hands as close to each other as possible (palms inward) and lay on a decline bench. Have your hands by the side of your chest with a neutral grip. This will be your starting position.
Ensuring that your elbows remain as close to your body whilst remaining parallel to each other throughout the movement, press the bag towards the ceiling in a controlled manner.
Squeeze your triceps at the top of the contraction for a second before reversing the movement back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press.

name: Sandbag - Sit Up To Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay down on your back on the floor bringing the dumbbells beside your chest. Keep your elbows at a 90-degree angle and your forearms perpendicular to the floor. Once here, keep your palms facing away from you, exhale and press the dumbbells towards the ceiling. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale lift your shoulders off the floor towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.

Without leaning backward or putting your posture into jeopardy, press the dumbbells above your head so that your arms are fully extended, yet slightly flexed/bent at your elbows. Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

name: Sandbag - V Sit Twists
instructions: Grab a sandbag of the required weight and take a seat on the floor. Hold the bag above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the bag is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V Sit With Plate Rotations or Seated Barbell Twists.

name: Sandbag - Leg Raises
instructions: Grab a sandbag and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the sandbag above your head. Elevate your legs (at full extension) vertically towards the ceiling until you create a 90 degree bend at your hips. Tip: Keep your legs extended throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and start to descend your legs on an arcing motion towards the floor. Continue to descend until your legs are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Reverse Crunches.

name: Sandbag - Lateral Drag With Press Ups
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and get into a push-up position leaning forward so that your weight is now baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. The sandbag should be placed under your chest to either the right or left side. This will be your starting position.
With your right arm, pull the sandbag from your left-hand side and move it to just outside of your right shoulder.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whilst flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Alternate between left and right.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sandbag lateral drags

name: Sandbag - Bent Over Rows
instructions: Grab the sandbag (palms facing each other), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The sandbag should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the sandbag to your abdomen (as you exhale), elevating elbows towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the sandbag). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the bag to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Low Cable Row or a Bent Over Barbell Row

name: Sandbag - Overhead Extensions
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, overhand grip. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders, keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the bag down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the bag along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Tricep Dips.

name: Sandbag - Standing Chest Press
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a sandbag at chest height.
2) Push the sandbag straight out in front of you until your arms are fully extended.
3) Pause briefly, then slowly return the sandbag to the starting position.
4) Keep your core engaged and maintain a stable stance throughout the movement.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
This exercise targets your chest muscles and improves upper body strength.

name: Sandbag - Front Raises
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Stand upright maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with sandbag just below hips. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating both arms on an arcing motion in front of you. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arms are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arms along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Front Plate Raise or Alternating Front Raises.

name: Sandbag - Bicep Curls
instructions: Grab the sandbag with both hands, in a standing position, with your arms at full length, palms facing in. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and bring the sandbag up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Inhale as you lower the sandbag toward the starting position.
Continue for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rotating Bicep Curls or Standing Barbell Curls.

name: Sandbag - Upright Row
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a narrow, overhand grip. Adopt a standing position with the bag resting against your thighs, your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the bag as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the sandbag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Upright Rows or Smith Machine Upright Row

en: shoulders

name: Sandbag - Snatch
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the bag with a neutral grip (palms facing together) and stand upright with the bag resting against your thighs. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time lower the sandbag to the floor and rotate your hands so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the bag so that your palms are facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the sandbag along the same path back in front of your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

en: full body

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time lower the sandbag to the floor and rotate your hands so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the bag so that your palms are facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the sandbag along the same path back in front of your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

name: Sandbag - Sumo Squats
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.
After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.

After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.

name: Sandbag - Sumo Squat To Upright Row
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The sandbag should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.

After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up. Exhale and pull the sandbag up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the sandbag upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bag.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the bag along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.

name: Sandbag - Good Mornings
instructions: Grab a sandbag and place on your upper back taking the weight across your shoulders. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees.
Adopt a shoulder-width stance, maintain a straight back throughout and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to lean forward from your waist. Push your hips and glutes backward as you do so until your torso is just off being parallel to the floor. Your hamstrings should be under tension at this point.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the sandbag to the starting position along the same path, thrusting your hips forward, squeezing your glutes as you do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed. (Remember to keep your back straight throughout the whole movement).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any back injuries, this may not be the most suitable exercise for you.
Variations: Barbell Good Mornings or Stiff Leg Dumbbell Deadlifts.

name: Sandbag - Alternating Swings
instructions: Grab a sandbag with both hands and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the sandbag with your palms facing each other and stand upright with the weight in front of you. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the sandbag to your right knee and push your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.
Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the sandbag to head height with your arm fully extended as you go.
Inhale and return the sandbag along the same path but on the opposite (left) side as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or TRX Squat Jumps.

name: Sandbag - Scissor Lunges (Back)
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your back. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.

As you exhale, you need to direct force through your quadriceps and glutes in order to drive your body upward and into a jumping motion. As you leave the floor you will reverse your stance by bringing your back leg forward and your front leg back.
As you land, you land into the next repetition by bending at your knees and hips to create right angles at the joints of your front leg, inhale as you do so.
Alternate in this manner and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.

name: Sandbag - Squat Jumps
instructions: Whilst in a standing position, grab the sandbag and place across your back. This will be your starting position.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bag to the starting position along the same path, aggressively push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Driving your hips forward is key in this movement as that will help to give you lift from the ground. Just before you reach a standing position, jump as high as you can.
Land softly at your knees by going straight into the next repetition. If you are at the end of your set, remember to allow a bend at the knees to soften the landing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or Kettlebell Squat Jumps.

name: Sandbag - Burpee Press
instructions: Stand upright holding the sandbag shoulder width apart allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and descend into a press up position. Place the bag on the ground and jump backward with your feet so that your legs are fully extended and your body is supported by your toes and hands.

Exhale and aggressively press through your hands by extending your elbow joints and create enough upward motion to be able to jump your legs back underneath your body so that when you lift the sandbag off the floor, you should be in the bottom of a squat.
Drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Sandbag - Woodchops (Right-Left)
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder width apart holding the sandbag on each side, maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Your first movement will be squatting as you rotate your torso to the right and bring the sandbag diagonally across your body until it is below the hip and next to the right knee.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move up and across your body (in a straight diagonal line). Bringing your arms above your left shoulder. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.

name: Sandbag - Shoulder To Shoulder Thruster
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width, holding the bag itself and not the handles. Lift the sandbag above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the sandbag by bending at your elbows until the bag is over your right shoulder.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bag back into the starting position and over to your left shoulder by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Sandbag Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

name: Sandbag - Shoulder To Shoulder
instructions: Grab a sandbag and adopt a shoulder width stance with your feet resting the sandbag on your right shoulder holding it in the middle with both hands. This will be your starting position.
Bend down slightly then as you spring up extending through your knees and ankle joints propel the sandbag upward off of your right shoulder over to the left and slowly lowering.
Maintain a straight back throughout this movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Sandbag - Clean & Press
instructions: Grab a sandbag with a shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bag is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the sandbag. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bag gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the sandbag upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the bag further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bag and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the sandbag above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the sandbag back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure to keep the bag close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Clean & Jerk or Barbell Cleans.

Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Sandbag - Zercher Squats
instructions: Stand up straight and place the sandbag so that it is resting on top of your forearms. This will be your starting position.
Position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.

Begin to lower the sandbag by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the sandbag as you exhale by pushing the floor with the ball of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell. It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form. Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

en: quadriceps
glutes
hamstrings
biceps
Stand up straight and place the sandbag so that it is resting on top of your forearms. This will be your starting position.
Position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.

Begin to lower the sandbag by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the sandbag as you exhale by pushing the floor with the ball of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell. It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form. Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

name: Sandbag - Squat To Overhead Press
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed across your chest. This will be your starting position.
Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return to the starting position where you will then extend the sandbag above your head and hold.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Sandbag - Alternating Overhead Curtsy Squats
instructions: Stand Up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Pick one foot up off the floor and cross it behind your other leg. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heel as you sit back into the movement until your thigh is either parallel or just past to the floor.
Drive up through your standing leg.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Reverse Lunges or Sitting Squats.

name: Sandbag - Alternating Overhead Side Lunges
instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag held above your head. This will be your starting position.

Take an exaggerated step out to one side and plant your foot with your toes slightly pointing outwards. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement so that you minimise the risk of injury. Inhale, push your hips backward and bend the knee of the foot you have stepped out with in order to lower your weight over that thigh. Once your thigh is parallel with the floor, pause for a brief moment, exhale and push through the heel and thigh from that planted foot.
Throughout this process the leg that remained in the starting position should be straight.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Leg Press (Wide Stance).

name: Sandbag - Alternating Reverse Overhead Lunges
instructions: Whilst in a standing position, grab the sandbag and hold above your head. This will be your starting position.
Take a step back (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
As you exhale, use mainly the heel of your front foot and push off the floor towards the starting position again.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Lunges or Barbell Lunges.

instructions: Stand up straight with the sandbag placed on your chest. This will be your starting position.

Take an exaggerated step out to one side and plant your foot with your toes slightly pointing outwards. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement so that you minimise the risk of injury. Inhale, push your hips backward and bend the knee of the foot you have stepped out with in order to lower your weight over that thigh. Once your thigh is parallel with the floor, pause for a brief moment, exhale and push through the heel and thigh from that planted foot.
Throughout this process the leg that remained in the starting position should be straight.
Perform the recommended amount of repetitions and repeat the movement for the opposite leg.
Variation: Leg Press (Wide Stance).

name: Sandbag - Alternating Reverse Lunges (Back)
instructions: Whilst in a standing position, grab the sandbag and place across your back. This will be your starting position.
Take a step back (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
As you exhale, use mainly the heel of your front foot and push off the floor towards the starting position again.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Lunges or Barbell Lunges.

name: Sandbag - Static Lunges
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a sandbag across your shoulders.
2) Step one foot backward into a lunge position, bending both knees to lower your body toward the ground.
3) Keep your front knee aligned with your ankle and your back knee hovering just above the floor.
4) Pause briefly in the lunge position, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
5) Repeat for the desired reps and then switch legs.
6) Focus on maintaining good posture and engaging your leg muscles throughout the movement.

name: Banded Straight Arm Pulldowns
instructions: 1)Attach a resistance band to an overhead anchor.
2) Stand facing the anchor with feet hip-width apart.
3) Grasp the resistance band with both hands, arms extended overhead.
4) Pull the band down towards your hips, keeping your arms straight.
5) Pause briefly at the bottom, then return to the starting position.
6) Focus on engaging your lats and keeping your core stable throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Feet Elevated Inverted Rows
instructions: 1) Grab hold of a bar with an overhand grip, and position yourself underneath with your feet elevated on a bench or box.
2) Have hands just wider than shoulder width apart.
3) Keep your body straight from head to heels, engage your core, and pull your chest towards the bar drawing the elbows down.
4) Lower yourself back down with control.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on maintaining proper form and engaging your back muscles.

name: Tyre - Press Up Plank
instructions: 1) Begin in a plank position with your hands placed wide apart on the ground and your feet elevated on a tyre. Ensure your body forms a straight line from head to heels. This is your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to stabilise your body and maintain proper alignment throughout the exercise. Keep your shoulders down and away from your ears to avoid shrugging.
3) Maintain tension in your core and avoid allowing your hips to sag or lift excessively.
4) Hold the exercise for the desired time, focusing on maintaining proper form.
This press up plank with feet on tyre exercise targets your chest, shoulders, triceps, and core muscles, while also challenging your stability and balance.

This press up plank with feet on tyre exercise targets your chest, shoulders, triceps, and core muscles, while also challenging your stability and balance.

name: Tyre - Dips
instructions: 1) Find a stable, elevated surface such as a sturdy tire or a bench. Sit on the edge of the tire with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips, fingers pointing towards your feet. Extend your legs straight out in front of you, heels on the ground. This is your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to stabilise your torso and maintain a straight posture throughout the exercise. Keep your shoulders down and back to avoid shrugging.

3) Begin the movement by bending your elbows and lowering your body towards the ground. Lower yourself until your elbows are bent at approximately 90 degrees, or slightly less if comfortable, ensuring your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
4) Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch and contraction in your triceps and chest muscles.
5) Press through your palms and engage your triceps to push yourself back up to the starting position. Extend your elbows fully but avoid locking them.
6) Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining proper form throughout.
7) Remember to breathe steadily throughout the exercise, inhaling as you lower yourself and exhaling as you push back up.
This exercise targets your triceps, shoulders, and chest muscles while also engaging your core for stability.

name: Tyre - Incline Press Ups
instructions: 1) Position yourself facing an inclined tyre with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart on the upper edge, fingers pointing forward. Extend your legs behind you, toes on the ground, forming a plank position with your body straight from head to heels. This is your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to stabilise your torso and maintain a straight line from your head to your heels throughout the exercise.
3) Lower your chest towards the tyre by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
4) Lower yourself until your chest is just above the edge of the tyre.
5) Pause briefly, then press through your palms and extend your elbows to push your body back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
6) Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled lowering and pushing phases.
7) Keep your breathing steady throughout the exercise, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.
This exercise targets your chest, shoulders, and triceps while also engaging your core for stability. Adjust the incline of the tyre to increase or decrease the challenge as needed.

name: Tyre - Decline Press Ups
instructions: 1) Begin in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart on the ground and your feet elevated on a sturdy tyre or elevated platform. Your body should form a straight line from head to heels. This is your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to stabilise your body and maintain a neutral spine throughout the exercise.
3) Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body at a 45-degree angle.
4) Pause briefly at the bottom, ensuring a full range of motion and feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
5) Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position, fully extending your elbows without locking them.
6) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining stability on the tyre with your feet.

This variation of push-ups targets your chest, shoulders, and triceps while also engaging your core muscles for stability. Adjust the difficulty by altering the height of the tyre or platform beneath your feet.

name: Tyre - Front Squats
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward, and place a weighted tyre vertically in front of you, gripping it firmly with both hands, elbows bent.
2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and ensure your back remains straight throughout the exercise.
3) Lower your body into a squat position by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your weight on your heels and lowering your hips down toward the tyre.
4) Descend until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below, maintaining control and stability.
5) Pause briefly at the bottom of the squat to ensure proper form and depth.
6) Drive through your heels and straighten your legs to return to the starting position, maintaining tension in your core and keeping your back straight.
7) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining a strong grip on the tire throughout.
This exercise targets your quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, and core muscles, providing a challenging variation of the front squat while also improving grip strength and stability.

name: Tyre - Squat Jumps
instructions: 1) Begin by standing with your feet hip-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward, and your knees slightly bent. Place a large tyre in front of you.
2) Lower yourself into a squat position, bending at the knees and hips while keeping your chest up and your back straight. Your thighs should be parallel to the ground, or as close as comfortable.
3) Once in the squat position, explosively jump upward, driving through your heels and extending your hips, knees, and ankles simultaneously.
4) As you ascend, aim to jump onto the tyre, landing softly with both feet on top.
Upon landing, immediately absorb the impact by bending your knees and hips.
5) From the position on top of the tyre, jump back down to the starting position on the ground, ensuring a soft landing with bent knees to absorb the impact.
6) Repeat the squat jumps onto and off of the tyre for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady and controlled pace.
7) Focus on maintaining proper form throughout the exercise, keeping your core engaged, and landing softly to minimise stress on your joints.
This dynamic plyometric exercise targets your lower body muscles, including the quadriceps, glutes, hamstrings, and calves, while also improving explosive power and agility.

name: Tyre - Drags
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, next to a large tire or similar heavy object that you can drag. Position yourself so that you are facing the tire, with both hands gripping the top edge.
2) Engage your core muscles and maintain a straight back throughout the exercise to stabilise your torso.
3) Lean forward slightly and begin to pull the tire towards you by extending your arms and driving through your legs.
4) Keep a steady pace as you drag the tire across the ground, using short, controlled steps to maintain balance and momentum.
5) Continue pulling the tire until it reaches your feet or as far as your space allows, maintaining proper form throughout.
6) To return the tire to its starting position, carefully reverse the pulling motion, maintaining control and stability.
7) Repeat the dragging motion for the desired number of repetitions or distance, focusing on maintaining a strong core and stable body position throughout the exercise.
The tire drag exercise targets your upper body, core, and legs, helping to improve strength, endurance, and overall fitness.

The tire drag exercise targets your upper body, core, and legs, helping to improve strength, endurance, and overall fitness.

name: Cable Hip Abduction
instructions: 1) Stand beside a low pulley cable machine with the cable attached to your ankle. Keep your core engaged and hold onto the machine for balance. Allow the leg to cross over in front of the other.
2) Lift your leg sideways against the resistance of the cable, keeping it straight.
3) Pause briefly at the top of the movement squeezing the glutes and outer thigh, then slowly lower your leg back down.
4) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
5) Focus on controlled movements and feel the tension in your outer hip muscles.
This exercise targets hip abduction and helps strengthen the muscles around your hips.

name: SkiErg
instructions: 1) Stand in front of the SkiErg machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the handles firmly with an overhand grip, arms extended overhead. This will be your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles and maintain a straight back as you prepare to initiate the movement. Ensure that your knees are slightly bent and your weight is evenly distributed.

3) Initiate the movement by pulling the handles down in a controlled motion. Begin by bending your knees slightly and hinging at your hips, driving your hips backward while maintaining a straight back.
4) As you pull the handles down, engage your lats and arms to bring them towards your thighs. Your hands should travel along the sides of your body in a smooth, fluid motion.
5) At the bottom of the movement, bend your knees further and squat down slightly, allowing your arms to extend fully behind your hips. This position mimics a powerful skiing motion.
6) Reverse the movement by extending your knees and hips to stand back up, simultaneously guiding the handles back to the starting position with your arms extended overhead.
7) Repeat the pulling motion for the desired time, calories, metres, maintaining a steady and controlled rhythm.
8) Focus on engaging your core and legs throughout the exercise. Avoid using only your arms; instead, generate power through your entire body.
The SkiErg exercise is an excellent full-body workout that builds cardiovascular endurance and strengthens your upper and lower body.

name: Weighted Dips
instructions: 1) Position yourself on a sturdy bench or chair, facing away from it, and place your hands shoulder-width apart on the edge of the bench, fingers pointing forward. Have your knees bent and place a weight plate on your thighs. This is your starting position.
2) Lower your body by bending your elbows, allowing them to flare out to the sides, until your upper arms are parallel to the ground or your elbows are at approximately 90-degree angles.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch in your chest and triceps.
3) Press through the palms of your hands and extend your elbows to return to the starting position, straightening your arms fully without locking out your elbows.
4) Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining stability in your core.
Weighted bench dips effectively target your triceps, chest, and shoulders, helping to build strength and muscle mass in the upper body.

name: Single Arm Front Incline Dumbbell Raise
instructions: 1) Sit on an incline bench set to a 45-degree angle, with your feet flat on the floor for stability. Hold a dumbbell in one hand, allowing it to hang straight down in front of you, while your other hand rests on your thigh or grips the bench for support. This will be your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability in your torso. Ensure that your back is straight and your head is in line with your spine.
3) In a controlled motion, lift the dumbbell straight in front of you until your arm is parallel to the ground. Keep your elbow slightly bent to avoid locking the joint.
4) Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, feeling the contraction in your shoulder and upper chest muscles.
5) Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, maintaining control and avoiding any swinging or jerky movements.
6) Repeat the lift for the recommended number of repetitions, focusing on a smooth and controlled motion throughout.
7) Switch to the opposite arm and repeat the exercise, ensuring equal repetitions on both sides.
This exercise targets the anterior deltoid and helps improve shoulder strength and stability.

name: Alternating Front Incline Dumbbell Raise
instructions: 1)Sit on a bench at an incline, holding a dumbbell in each hand.

3) Lower it back down, then repeat with the opposite arm.
4) Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.
5) Focus on controlled movements and keep your core engaged.
This exercise targets your shoulders and helps improve shoulder strength and stability.

name: Chest Supported Single Arm Dumbbell Front Raises
instructions: 1) Position yourself face down on an incline bench, chest supported, with a dumbbell in one hand.
2) Let your arm hang straight down towards the floor, knuckles facing upwards.
3) Raise the dumbbell directly in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arm straight with a slight bend.
4) Lower the dumbbell back down slowly and repeat.
5) Switch arms and repeat the movement.
6) Focus on controlling the motion and engaging your shoulder muscles throughout.

name: BOSU - Glute Bridges (Feet On Dome)
instructions: 1) Lie on your back with your feet placed on the dome of a BOSU ball.
2) Press through your heels to lift your hips towards the ceiling, squeezing your glutes at the top.
3) Lower your hips back down with control whilst bracing the abs.
4) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on engaging your glutes throughout the movement.

name: BOSU - Single Leg Glute Bridges (Foot On Dome)
instructions: 1) Position a BOSU ball with the dome side up.
2) Lie on your back and place one foot on the dome, keeping the other leg extended.
3) Press through your heel on the BOSU to lift your hips, forming a straight line from shoulders to knees.
4) Pause briefly at the top, then lower your hips back down.
5) Repeat for the required repetitions.
6) Switch legs and repeat.
7) Focus on stability and engage your glutes throughout.
This exercise targets your glutes and hamstrings.

name: BOSU - Crunches
instructions: 1) Place a BOSU ball flat-side down on the floor. Lie on your back with your lower back centred on the dome.
2) Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands lightly behind your head.
3) Engage your core muscles and lift your shoulder blades off the BOSU, curling your upper body towards your knees.
4) Think about closing the gab between your ribcage and hips.
5) Pause briefly at the top, then lower back down with control.
6) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining tension in your core throughout.

name: BOSU - V-Sit Ups
instructions: 1) Sit on a BOSU ball with your feet off the ground and knees bent. Lean back slightly to engage your core.
2) Extend your legs straight out while simultaneously leaning back further, forming a V-shape with your body.
3) Return to the starting position by bending your knees and bringing them towards your chest.
4) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and engaging your core throughout.

name: BOSU - V-Sit Ups (Assisted)

4) Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement, then return to the starting position by sitting up and bringing your knees towards your chest.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining balance on the BOSU ball.

name: BOSU - Side Plank With Rotation (Feet On Dome)
instructions: 1) Begin in a side plank position with your feet on the dome of a BOSU ball.
2) Keep hips stacked on top of each other and elbow directly under the shoulder.
3) Engage your core and lift your hips, forming a straight line from head to heels.
4) Rotate your torso, reaching your top arm underneath your body and towards the floor.
5) Return to the starting position and repeat the required repetitions on the same side.
6) Then repeat on the other side.
7) Focus on stability and controlled movements.
This exercise targets your obliques and improves core strength.

name: BOSU - Mountain Climbers
instructions: 1) Start in a plank position with your hands on the flat side of a BOSU ball.
2) Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
3) Drive one knee towards your chest, then quickly switch legs, as if running in place.
4) Continue alternating legs at a moderate pace.
5) Focus on maintaining stability on the BOSU ball and keeping your core engaged throughout.

name: BOSU - Crossover Climbers
instructions: 1) Start in a high plank position with your hands on the BOSU ball and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.

3) Keep abs braced and hips as stable as possible.
4) Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
5) Continue alternating sides in a smooth, controlled motion.
6) Focus on engaging your core and maintaining stability on the BOSU ball throughout the exercise.

name: BOSU - Split Squats (Flat Side Up)
instructions: 1) Stand with one foot on the center of a BOSU ball, flat side up and the other foot in front of you on the ground.
2) Lower your body by bending your knees until your back knee is just above the ground.
3) Keep your front knee aligned with your ankle and your torso upright.
4) Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
6) Focus on stability and balance throughout the movement.

3) Lower your body by bending both knees until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
4) Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
5) Repeat for required repetitions on the same leg.
6) Complete on the other leg.
7) Focus on stability and balance.
This exercise targets your legs and core.

name: BOSU - Static Lunges (Flat Side Up)
instructions: 1) Stand on the flat side of a BOSU ball with one foot positioned in the center and the other foot extended behind you.
2) Lower your body into a lunge position, bending both knees until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
3) Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the movement.
4) Drive through the front heel to return to start position.
5) Repeat for required repetitions.
6) Repeat on the other side.
7) Focus on stability and balance while engaging your leg muscles.

name: BOSU - Squats (Flat Side Up)
instructions: 1) Stand on the flat side of the BOSU with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2) Engage your core and maintain balance as you lower into a squat, keeping your knees aligned with your toes.
3) Pause briefly at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to standing.
4) Repeat for desired reps, focusing on stability and control.
This exercise targets your legs and core.

name: BOSU - Side To Side Press Ups
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and lean forward so that you are now bearing weight on your arms. Have your hands placed where your chest would be on dome side. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your feet and your hands on the BOSU. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.
Place your right hand out to the right of the BOSU.

Pause for a brief moment, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Bring your right hand to the centre of the BOSU to meet the left and then place your left hand out to the left of the BOSU. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles and triceps as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bench Press or Press Ups.

name: BOSU - Power Press Ups
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and lean forward so that you are now bearing weight on your arms. Have your hands placed on each side of the BOSU (flat side). Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your feet and your hands on the BOSU. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whilst keeping your elbows by your side.
Exhale and explosively press hard enough through your palms to return your body not only along the same path but to enable you to lift the BOSU off the floor.
When your body weight is on its descent, land into the next repetition.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bench Press or Press Ups.

name: BOSU - Plank (Feet On)
instructions: 1) Begin in a plank position with your feet on the dome side of the BOSU ball and your forearms on the ground.
2) Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core muscles.
3) Keep shoulders over elbows and hips down.
4) Hold this position for the desired amount of time, focusing on stability and control.
5) Remember to breathe steadily throughout the exercise.
This plank variation targets your core muscles and improves balance and stability.

name: BOSU - Press Up Plank (Feet On)
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and lean forward so that you are now bearing weight on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now lift both feet up onto the BOSU (dome side) so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and your feet on the BOSU (dome side). Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.

Keep your abdominal muscles tight throughout this exercise and keep your pelvis in line with the rest of your body to minimise risk of injury to your lower back (you can elevate your pelvis slightly to reduce lower back pain).
Hold this position for as long as you can or for the recommended amount of time.
Variations: Plank.
