name: BOSU - Plank (Forearms On)
instructions: Firstly, adopt an 'all-fours' position above on the floor. From here you will need to bend your elbows to a 90 degree angle and place your upper body weight onto your forearms on top of the BOSU (dome side). After you have done that, take a step backwards with your feet and place your remaining bodyweight through your toes. At this point your body should be suspended (in a straight line) above the floor with just your forearms and toes as points of contact. This will be your starting position.

Keep your abdominal muscles tight throughout this exercise and keep your pelvis in line with the rest of your body to minimise risk of injury to your lower back (you can elevate your pelvis slightly to reduce lower back pain).
Hold this position for as long as you can or for the recommended amount of time.
Variations: Plank.

name: Double Kettlebell Split Snatch
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at arms length.
2) Swing the kettlebells through the legs and explosively push through your legs and hips to drive the kettlebells overhead, simultaneously splitting your stance so one foot is forward and one back.
3) Lower the kettlebells back down to shoulder height and return to the starting position.
4) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
5) Focus on explosive power and stability throughout the movement.

name: Facepulls
instructions: 1) Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attachment set up at shoulder height.
2) Grab the ends of the rope with an overhand grip.
3) Step back slightly and position your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent.
4) Pull the rope towards your face, keeping elbows up high and spreading it apart as you pull.
5)  Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
6) Pause briefly at the peak contraction, then slowly return to the starting position.
7) Keep your core engaged and maintain a tall posture throughout the movement.
8) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
This exercise targets your rear deltoids and upper back muscles.

name: Cable Squats
instructions: 1) Stand facing a cable machine set up with a low pulley.
2) Grab onto the rope attachment and have feet just wider than shoulder width, toes slightly turned out.
3) Lower into a squat, keeping your back straight and chest up.
4) Pause briefly at the bottom, then push through your heels and squeeze the glutes to return to the starting position.
5) Focus on keeping tension on the cable throughout the movement.
6) Repeat for desired reps.
This exercise targets your legs and glutes while also engaging your core for stability.

name: TRX Curtsy Squats
instructions: 1) Stand facing the TRX straps, holding the handles with your palms facing each other. Position your feet hip-width apart and lean back slightly to create tension in the straps. This will be your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and keep your back straight throughout the movement.
3) Shift your weight to one leg and lift the opposite leg off the ground, crossing it behind your standing leg.
4) Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, allowing the rear leg to cross further behind you. Keep your front knee aligned with your toes and ensure your back remains straight.
5) Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch in your glutes and thighs.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position, bringing your rear leg back to the centre.
6) Repeat the movement on the opposite side, alternating between each leg for the recommended number of repetitions.
This exercise helps improve lower body strength, stability, and coordination.

2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and keep your back straight throughout the movement.
3) Shift your weight to one leg and lift the opposite leg off the ground, crossing it behind your standing leg.
4) Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, allowing the rear leg to cross further behind you. Keep your front knee aligned with your toes and ensure your back remains straight.
5) Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch in your glutes and thighs.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position, bringing your rear leg back to the centre.
6) Repeat the movement on the opposite side, alternating between each leg for the recommended number of repetitions.
This exercise helps improve lower body strength, stability, and coordination.

name: Landmine V Bar Rows
instructions: 1) Stand with feet shoulder-width apart over a landmine facing away from the anchor point.
2) Hold onto a V bar attachment with a bent over position.
3) Keep your back straight, abs braced and chest up.
4) Pull the V-bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together and drawing your elbows towards your hips.
5) Slowly lower the V-bar back to the starting position.
6) Repeat for desired reps.
7) Focus on controlled movements and engage your back muscles throughout.
This exercise targets your upper back and biceps.

name: Smith Machine Reverse Lunges
instructions: 1) Position yourself under the Smith machine bar with your feet hip-width apart. The bar should be resting comfortably across your upper back and shoulders, with your hands gripping it slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.

2) Step back with one foot and lower your body into a reverse lunge, bending both knees to approximately 90-degree angles. Your front knee should be aligned with your ankle, and your back knee should hover slightly above the floor.
3) Maintain an upright torso and engage your core muscles for stability throughout the movement. Keep your chest lifted and your shoulders back.
4) Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position, bringing your back foot forward to meet the other foot.
5) Repeat the movement on the same side for the desired reps before switching to the other leg.

This exercise targets your quadriceps, hamstrings, glutes, and calves, as well as engaging your core for stability. It's a great way to strengthen your lower body and improve balance and coordination.

name: Glute Press Machine
instructions: 1) Stand with one leg straight and the other against the foot pad of the glute machine with your knee bent.
2) Lean forward and grasp the handles or bars for stability.
3) Extend the leg straight back, squeezing your glutes at the top.
4) Pause briefly, then return to the starting position.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on each leg.
Focus on controlled movements and squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.

name: Hack Squat Wide Stance
instructions: 1) Stand on the Hack Squat machine with your back against the pad and your feet nice and wide.
2) Place your feet higher on the platform with toes pointed slightly outward.
3) Grasp the handles and release the safety bars.
4) Lower your body by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
5) Push through your heels to return to the starting position.
6) Focus on maintaining proper form and engaging your leg muscles throughout the movement.

name: Lateral Raise Machine
instructions: 1) Sit on the lateral raise machine with your chest against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
2) Grasp the handles with a neutral grip and position your arms by your sides.
3) Exhale as you raise the handles out to the sides until your arms are parallel to the ground.
4) Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the handles back down to the starting position.
5) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and maintaining proper form throughout.
This exercise targets the deltoid muscles in your shoulders.

name: Walking Incline
instructions: 1) Begin by standing on the treadmill with your feet shoulder-width apart and your body centered on the belt. Make sure the treadmill is set to a low speed to start.
2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability as you start the treadmill and gradually increase the speed to a comfortable walking pace.
3) Once you've established a steady walking pace, increase the incline of the treadmill. Begin with a slight incline, and adjust as needed based on your fitness level and comfort.
4) As you walk, keep your posture upright with your shoulders relaxed and your arms swinging naturally by your sides. Avoid leaning too far forward or backward.
5) Focus on pushing through your heels with each step to engage your glutes and hamstrings. This will help to tone and strengthen your lower body muscles.
6) Continue walking on the inclined treadmill for the desired duration.
Incorporating incline walking into your treadmill routine can boost calorie burn, increase cardiovascular endurance, and target different muscle groups compared to walking on a flat surface.

name: Kettlebell Swings
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell with both hands in front of you.
2) Hinge at your hips and slightly bend your knees while keeping your back straight.
3) Swing the kettlebell between your legs, then explosively thrust your hips forward and squeeze your glutes to swing it up to chest level.
4) Control the descent and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
5) Focus on using your hips to generate momentum and keep your core engaged throughout the exercise.

name: Cable Front Squats
instructions: 1) Stand facing a cable machine with a small bar attachment on a low pulley.
2) Grab the handle with an overhand grip, palms facing the ceiling in a clean positions and step back to create tension in the cable.
3) Keep the bar close to the chest and elbows lifted.
4) Position your feet shoulder-width apart with toes slightly turned out.
5) Lower into a squat, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
6) Pause briefly at the bottom, then push through your heels and squeeze the glutes to return to the starting position.
7) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
8) Focus on maintaining proper form and control throughout the exercise.
Cable front squats target your quads, glutes, and core muscles.

name: alternating barbell curtsy squats
instructions: 1) Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell across your shoulders.
2) Step one foot diagonally behind and across the opposite leg, bending both knees into a curtsy position.
3) Push through your front heel to return to standing, then alternate sides.
4) Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout.
5) Repeat for desired repetitions. This exercise targets your glutes and legs.

name: Barbell Squats (Low Bar)
instructions: 1) Position a barbell across your upper back, resting it below your neck on your traps.
2) Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward.
3) Brace your core and squat down by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight.
4) Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
5) Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement.
6) Repeat for desired reps, focusing on proper form and control.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlifts
instructions: 1) Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding dumbbells in front of thighs.
2) Hinge at hips with a slight bend in the knees, keeping back straight, and lower dumbbells towards shins.
3) Pause when you feel a stretch in hamstrings, then drive through the heels and return to standing position squeezing the glutes.
4) Keep core engaged throughout.
This exercise targets hamstrings and lower back.

name: Chest Supported Alternating Dumbbell Front Raises
instructions: 1) Lie face down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging straight down with an overhand grip.
2) Keeping your chest pressed against the bench, lift one dumbbell up to shoulder level while maintaining a slight bend in your elbow.
3) Lower the dumbbell back down and repeat on the opposite side.
4) Alternate between each arm for the desired number of repetitions.
5) Focus on controlled movements and engage your shoulder muscles throughout.
This exercise targets your front deltoids.

name: Dumbbell Concentration Curls Hammer
instructions: 1) Place one arm against an incline bench with a dumbbell in hand, elbow resting on the bench and palm facing inward.
2) Keep your back straight and core engaged.
3) Curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder while keeping your upper arm stationary squeezing the bicep.
4) Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbell back down.
5) Repeat for required repetitions and then swap arms.
6) Focus on controlled movements and avoid swinging the weight.
This exercise targets your biceps.

name: Press Ups
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and lean forward so that you are now weight baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whist flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bench Press or Dumbbell Chest Press.

name: Seated Hammer Curls
instructions: Grab two dumbbells and take a seat on a bench which is just off vertical (70-80 degrees), and allow your arms to hang by your sides, perpendicular to the floor.
The palms of your hands should be facing each other. This will be your starting position. Tip: Throughout this movement your elbows should stay pinned to the sides of your torso.

Whilst holding the upper arm stationary, exhale as you curl both the dumbbells upward, contracting your biceps. Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your biceps. (Tip: Only the forearms should move.)
Inhale as you slowly begin to bring the dumbbells back along the same path, down to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Hammer Curls or Cable Curls.

name: Kettlebell Jump Squats
instructions: Grab two kettlebells and stand with a shoulder width stance, toes pointing slightly outward (palms facing the side of your legs).
Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a stright back. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, exhale and push hard through your heels to drive your hips forward. Extend at your knee and ankle joints in order to jump as high as you can before landing into your next rep.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always be aware of your posture with this exercise as if performed incorrectly, could cause injury.
Variations: Dumbbell Jump Squats or Barbell Jump Squats.

name: Single Arm Clean & Press (Dumbbell)
instructions: Grab a dumbbell and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arm (with which you are holding the dumbbell) to hang perpendicular to the floor between your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the dumbbell, exhale as you do so. Your back should remain straight and your arm should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the dumbbell gets to the middle of your thighs. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the dumbbell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the dumbbell further. Flare your elbow up and outward until your body is at full extension.
When at full extension, aggressively squat under the dumbbell and rotate your elbow under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders).
Drive upward until you are in a standing position, inhale at this point.

Once you are in a standing position, exhale and bend at your knees to generate power to press the dumbbell above your head until your elbow is locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Inhale and descend the dumbbell back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure to keep the dumbbell close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Barbell Clean & Press or Barbell Cleans.

name: Dumbbell 21's
instructions: Grab two dumbbells and take a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Curl the dumbbells up half way so your elbows are at 90 degrees, adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. Tip: Only your forearms should move and your torso remain rigid. If you are struggling with balance, split your stance so you have one foot in front of the other. This will be your starting position.

This exercise is split up into 3 different elements. The bottom half, top half and a full range bicep curl. Firstly, using your biceps, lower the dumbbells down until your arms are straight (Inhale as you go). Once your arms are straight, use your biceps once more to curl the dumbbells back along the same path into their starting position, exhaling as you do so.

Once you have completed 7 repetitions, curl the dumbbells up (from half way) towards your shoulders. Exhale as you lift the dumbbells and continue until your biceps are squeezed tightly and you cannot elevate your forearms anymore without bringing your elbows away from your body. Once you have reached this point, return the dumbbells along the same path to their starting position whilst inhaling.

Once you have completed 7 repetitions, perform 7 full curls. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells towards your thighs so your arms are hanging perpendicular to the floor. Exhale and use your biceps to curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders. Once your biceps are fully contracted, inhale and lower the dumbbells back along the same path into their starting position.
Variations: Cable 21's or EZ Bar 21's.

forearms

name: Cable 21's
instructions: Grab a low cable with the bar attachment on and take an underhand grip (palms facing forward). Allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor and adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. Tip: Only your forearms should move and your torso remain rigid. If you are struggling with balance, split your stance so you have one foot in front of the other. This will be your starting position.

This exercise is split up into 3 different elements. The bottom half, top half and a full range bicep curl. Firstly, whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the bar upward towards your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so but stop once you get half way. Return the bar along the same path back to the starting position as you inhale.

After 7 repetitions, curl the bar up to your shoulders and perform the top half of your curls. In order to do so, lower the bar whilst inhaling but stop once your elbows are at 90 degrees. Exhale and curl the bar back along the same path to the starting position (shoulders).

After 7 repetitions, Lower the bar all the way until your arms are straight. Once you are here, perform 7 full range repetitions. In order to do so, curl the bar up to your shoulders on an arced path, keeping your elbows pinned to your torso, exhaling as you go. Once you have paused for a brief moment and squeezed your biceps, return the bar along the same path back into the starting position.
Variations: Dumbbell 21's or EZ Bar 21's.

name: Seated Alternating Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a bench that is just of vertical (between 70-80 degrees). Bring the dumbbells up to the side of your shoulders so your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle, your palms should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press one of the dumbbells towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight). Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, return the dumbbell back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Alternating Shoulder Press or Alternating Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Tricep Extension (Rope)
instructions: Place a rope attachment on to a high cable pulley, adopt a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and stand 12 inches away from the machine. Once you have taken hold of the rope, bring your elbows in to the sides of your torso and keep them pinned in place. This is your starting position.
As you exhale, push the rope towards the floor and split your little fingers as much as possible until your elbows are fully extended.
At this position you should squeeze your triceps and pause for a second.
Then, as you inhale, reverse the movement until your elbows are flexed at 90 degrees and you have returned to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Press Down Machine or Dumbbell Tricep Kickbacks.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a bench just off vertical (around 70-80 degrees). Press the dumbbells above your head so that you arms are fully extended, yet slightly flexed/bent at your elbows. Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Slowly and in a controlled fashion, descend the dumbbells until they reach the side of your head (parallel with your ears), creating a 90 degree angle at your elbows. Inhale on the downward motion and flare your elbows out wide.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the dumbbells back along this path to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Plyo Lunges
instructions: Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.

As you exhale, you need to direct force through your quadriceps and glutes in order to drive your body upward and into a jumping motion. As you leave the floor you will reverse your stance by bringing your back leg forward and your front leg back.
As you land, you land into the next repetition by bending at your knees and hips to create right angles at the joints of your front leg, inhale as you do so.
Alternate in this manner and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Barbell Lunges.

name: High Knees
instructions: Stand upright with your arms beside your torso and adopt a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and lift your left leg up infront of you until your knee and hip joints are at 90 degrees. Then have your right arm bent at a 90 degree position at your elbow.
Exhale and swap your left leg for your right and your right arm for your left.
Continue to elevate your knees in alternating fashion whilst swapping your arms over so they are always opposing your legs. Tip: Take shallow breaths whilst you are performing the movement.
Alternate as fast as you can to simulate a running motion but standing still. The higher you can get your knees, the better!
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Ab Bike or Mountain Climbers.

name: Squat Jumps
instructions: Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your toes pointing slightly outward. Allow your arms to hang comfortably by your sides at this point. Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Bring your arms out in front of you so that they are also parallel to the floor at this point. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, exhale and push hard through your heels to drive your hips forward. As you extend at your knee and ankle joints in order to jump as high as you can, pull your arms down towards the sides of your torso at the top of the jump (this will help to generate more height), before landing into your next rep.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always be aware of your posture with this exercise as if performed incorrectly, could cause injury.
Variations: Kettlebell Jump Squats or Dumbbell Jump Squats.

name: Box Jumps
instructions: Set up a box/step/bench in front of you at the desired height. Stand upright with your arms beside your torso and adopt a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend into a squat position by bending at your knees and hips. Once your hips are at 90 degrees, exhale and press through your feet explosively using your thigh and glute muscles in order to generate enough power to jump up and onto the box/step/bench infront of you.
When you land on the platform, bend at your knee and hip joints once more and extend upward into a standing position.
Inhale and step back off the box/step/bench back into your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Step Ups or Dumbbell Step Ups.

name: Burpees
instructions: Stand upright with your arms beside your torso and adopt a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend into a press up position. Place your hands just wider than shoulder width and jump backward with your feet so that your legs are fully extended and your body is supported by your toes and hands.
Continue to drop your torso downward towards the floor by bending at your elbows until your chest is only a few inches away.

Once you get to this point, exhale and aggressively press through your hands by extending your elbow joints and create enough upward motion to be able to jump your legs back underneath your body so that when you take your hands off the floor, you should be in the bottom of a squat.

Now, press through the middle of your feet by using your thighs and glutes, extending at both your knee and hip joints to generate power for a jumping motion (you can use your arms at this point to emphasise lift).
As you land, inhale and go straight into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: TRX Press Ups
instructions: 1) Start by adjusting the TRX suspension mid-length and securing them firmly to a stable anchor point overhead.
2) Stand facing away the anchor point and grasp the handles of the TRX straps with both hands, palms facing down.
3) Position yourself in a plank position, keeping everything in a straight line. This is your starting position.
4) Engage your core muscles to maintain a straight line from your head to your heels throughout the exercise.
5) Bend your elbows and slowly lower your chest towards the ground, maintaining control and stability in your shoulders and core.
6) Pause briefly once you feel the stretch in your chest muscles.
7) Push through your palms and straighten your arms to return to the starting position, exhaling as you push up.
8) Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on maintaining proper form and control throughout.
This exercise targets your chest, shoulders, and triceps while also engaging your core for stability.

name: TRX Plyo One Leg Squats
instructions: 1) Stand facing the TRX suspension trainer anchor point, holding the handles with your arms extended straight in front of you. This is your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and keep your chest lifted throughout the exercise.
3) Bend one knee and lift your foot off the ground.
4) Lower your body into a squat position on the standing leg, keeping your knee in line with your toes and your chest upright.
5) Pause briefly at the bottom of the squat, ensuring your knee does not collapse inward.
6) Push through the heel of your standing foot and explosively jump upwards, extending your hips and knee to propel your body off the ground.
7) Land softly on the same foot and immediately lower yourself back into a squat position upon landing, maintaining control and stability.
8) Repeat the movement for the desired repetitions and then switch sides.
This exercise targets your lower body strength, balance, and coordination while incorporating the instability challenge of the TRX suspension trainer.

name: TRX Pistol Squats
instructions: 1) Stand facing the TRX suspension trainer, holding both handles with an overhand grip. Adjust the straps to hang at mid-length.
2) Balance on one leg, lifting the opposite foot off the ground slightly in front of you. Keep your chest up and shoulders back for proper posture.
3) Lower your body by bending your knee and hip, allowing the suspended foot to extend forward as you descend. Aim to keep your raised foot off the ground throughout the movement.
4) Lower yourself as far as your flexibility allows, ideally reaching a seated position with your thigh parallel to the floor. Maintain a straight back and avoid rounding your shoulders.
5) Pause briefly at the bottom position, then push through your heel to return to the starting position, straightening your standing leg.
6) Maintain control throughout the exercise, engaging your core and focusing on stability. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions on one leg before switching to the other.
The TRX pistol squat challenges balance, strength, and flexibility, particularly targeting your quadriceps, glutes, and core muscles.

name: TRX Atomic Abs
instructions: 1) Place your feet in the TRX straps, adjusting them so they are suspended a few inches off the ground. Begin in a plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line from head to heels. This will be your starting position.
2) Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and ensure your spine is in a neutral position. Keep your head in line with your spine, and avoid letting your hips sag or rise.
3) In a controlled motion, bring your knees towards your chest, keeping your feet together and maintaining a tight core. Your hips will rise slightly as you tuck your knees in.
4) Pause for a moment, feeling the contraction in your abs and hip flexors.
5) Extend your legs back to the starting plank position, straightening your body once again.
6) Continue for the recommended number of repetitions, focusing on maintaining a steady and controlled movement.
7) Remember to breathe consistently and avoid any sudden or jerky movements.
This TRX Atomic Abs exercise effectively targets your core muscles, enhancing strength and stability.

name: Zottman Curl
instructions: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a standing position. Your arms should be fully extended, elbows close to your body with your palms facing away from you. This will be your starting position.

While holding the upper arm still, curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps as you exhale. Tip: Keep your elbows close to your body throughout the movement. Only your forearms should move.
Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your biceps.
Whilst at the top of the movement, you then rotate your wrist so that you have a pronated grip (palms down).
With this grip, descend the dumbbells along the same plane you brought them up on.
Once you return to the starting position, rotate your wrists once again until your palms are facing away from you.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Drag Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Grab two dumbbells with a supinated grip (palms facing forward), elbows close to your torso and your arms at full extension. This will be your starting position.
As you exhale, curl the dumbbells up whilst dragging your elbows backwards until you can contract your biceps no more. Tip: Try to stop your shoulders from lifting.
Pause at this contracted position for a second before returning the dumbbells along the same plane to the starting point. Be sure to inhale as you perform this section of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Curl.

Grab two dumbbells with a supinated grip (palms facing forward), elbows close to your torso and your arms at full extension. This will be your starting position.
As you exhale, curl the dumbbells up whilst dragging your elbows backwards until you can contract your biceps no more. Tip: Try to stop your shoulders from lifting.
Pause at this contracted position for a second before returning the dumbbells along the same plane to the starting point. Be sure to inhale as you perform this section of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Curl.

name: Pull Ups (Neutral Grip)
instructions: Take hold of the pull up bars with a neutral grip (hands should be facing each other).
Both arms should be extended as you create a small curvature in your back and push your chest out. This will be your starting position.

Now, exhale and pull your torso up until your head is at least level with your hands. Concentrate on squeezing your shoulder blades back and down (through your lats) as you reach the fully contracted position.

After a brief pause at the contracted position, inhale as you slowly return your torso to the starting position. Track the same path down as you did up. You are at the starting position when your lats are fully stretched.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Wide Grip Pull Ups.

name: Leverage Iso Row (Single Arm)
instructions: Load up the recommended weight onto the machine and adjust the height of the seat so that the handles are at chest level. Grab one of the handles with either a neutral or pronated (palms facing down) grip. Use your other hand for support (being sure to keep away from any moving parts). This will be your starting position.
Exhale and pull the handle in towards your body by flexing at the elbow. Retract your shoulder blades and engage your back as you go.
Pause at this position, inhale and slowly return the handles to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: One-Arm Seated Row Machine (Neutral Grip).

Variation: One-Arm Seated Row Machine (Neutral Grip).

name: V-Sit Twist (Medicine Ball)
instructions: Grab a medicine ball of the required weight and take a seat on the floor. Hold the medicine ball above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the medicine ball is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: V Sit With Plate Rotations or Seated Barbell Twists.

name: Leg Press (Single Leg)
instructions: Load on the desired amount of weight and take a seat on the machine. Place one of your feet on the platform directly in front of you and adopt a stabilising stance with your other foot (out of the way of any moving equipment). This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly flex at your knee until it is at 90 degrees. Be sure not to allow your knee to collapse in when performing this movement.
As you exhale, Press through your heel to push the foot plate away from you until you return to the starting position with your quads and glutes under tension.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Leg Press or Leg Press (Narrow Stance).

name: Single-Leg Lying Hamstring Curl
instructions: Firstly, adjust the lever where your ankle will be so that it matches your height. When you lay face down on the machine the lever should be a few inches below your calves.

Once you have set the levers, lay face down on the machine and grab the handles positioned on the inside of the machine. Your toes should be straight and pointed towards the floor. This will be your starting position.
While exhaling, curl one of your heels up towards your glutes. Once you are at the top of the contraction, hold for a second before lowering your leg towards the starting position.
Inhale as you do this and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Do not allow your hips to lift as you contract your hamstring as this will put undue stress on the lower back.
Variations: Lying Hamstring Curl or Standing Hamstring Curl Machine.

name: Seated Calf Raise (Toes In)
instructions: Place the recommended amount of weight on the machine and take a seat. Once you are seated, place you feet on the foot plate with your toes pointing together, with your heels apart. Next, adjust the height of the lever pad and place your lower thighs under it. Place your hands on top of the lever pad in order to prevent it from slipping.
Lift the lever slightly by pushing through the balls of your feet and release the safety bar. This will be your starting position.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels towards the floor and continue until your calves are fully stretched.

As you exhale, press through the balls of your feet, contracting the calves until your ankles are as high as possible. Hold and squeeze your calves for a second before repeating the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Calf Raise Machine or Seated Calf Raise Machine (Toes Out).

name: Tricep Press Down Machine
instructions: Place the recommended weight onto the machine and take a seat with your legs firmly secured in position. You should take hold of the handles and start with a 90 degree bend at your elbows. This is your starting position.
As you exhale, press the handles towards the floor until your elbows are fully extended.
At this position you should squeeze your triceps and pause for a second.
Then, as you inhale, reverse the movement until your elbows are flexed at 90 degrees and you have returned to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Tricep Extension or Dumbbell Tricep Kickbacks.

name: Single Leg 45 Degree Leg Press
instructions: Using a 45 degree leg press machine, take a seat and place one of your feet on the foot plate.

Gently press the foot plate away from you as you unhook the lock bars. You will take the weight at this point, ensure your knee is not locked and your joints remain slightly flexed. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly lower the platform until your upper and lower leg makes a 90-degree angle.
As you exhale, press through your heel to push the foot plate away from you, engaging your quadriceps and glutes in order to do so.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and ensure to lock the safety pins properly once you are done.
Caution: Always securely lock the safety bars once you have finished.
Variations: 45 Degree Leg Press (Wide Stance), 45 Degree Leg Press (Shoulder Width Stance) or 45 Degree Leg Press (Narrow Stance)

name: Spin Bike
instructions: To begin, seat yourself on the bike and adjust the seat to your height.
Start pedalling and adjust the resistance to the level you want.
Remember: When you are coming to the end of your cycle, DO NOT stop pedalling without applying the brake or coming down to a gentle stop. Otherwise there is a danger of injury to your joints.

name: Single-Arm Seated Row Machine (Overhand Grip)
instructions: Set the weight on the machine to the recommended resistance and set the height of the seat so that your chest support is on your sternum. You should adopt an overhand grip with one of your hands. Your other hand should be used for support. This will be your starting position.
Pull the handle towards your torso, ensuring your elbow is at shoulder height. Exhale as you retract your elbow until your shoulder blades can retract no more.
Pause at this point for a second and then slowly return the handle to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Plate Side Bends
instructions: Stand with a narrow or shoulder width stance with a weight plate in your right hand. Have your elbow fully extended with the plate by your side. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your back straight and your head up, bend at the waist to the right as far as possible. Be sure to inhale as you bend to the side. Hold at the bottom of the movement for a second and come back up to the starting position as you exhale.
Tip: The rest of your body must remain stationary.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and do the same for the left hand side.
Caution: Exercise caution if you have lower back problems. Stop immediately
Variations: Dumbbell Side Bends or Barbell Side Bends

name: Dumbbell Walking Lunges
instructions: Whilst in a standing position, grab two dumbbells and keep your arms by your sides. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
As you exhale, push off the floor with your rear foot to return your body into a standing position.
Alternate legs as you go (as you walk forwards) and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bodyweight Walking Lunges or Barbell Walking Lunges.

name: Wrist Roller
instructions: To begin, stand straight up grabbing a wrist roller using a pronated grip (palms facing down).  Your feet should be shoulder width apart.

Slowly lift both arms until they are fully extended and parallel to the floor in front of you.  Note: Make sure the rope is not wrapped around the roller.  Your entire body should be stationary except for the forearms.  This is the starting position.
Rotate one wrist at a time in an upward motion to bring the weight up to the bar by rolling the rope around the roller.
Once the weight has reached the bar, slowly begin to lower the weight back down by rotating the wrist in a downward motion until the weight reaches the starting position.
Repeat for the prescribed amount of repetitions in your program.

name: Tricep Extension with Chains
instructions: You will need two cable handle attachments and a flat bench, as well as chains, for this exercise. Clip the middle of the chains to the handles, and position yourself on the flat bench. Your elbows should be pointing straight up.
Begin by extending through the elbow, keeping your upper arm still, with your wrists pronated.
Pause at the lockout, and reverse the motion to return to the starting position.

name: TRX Split Squats
instructions: Suspend your straps so the handles are 18-30 inches from the floor.
Facing away from the setup, place your rear foot into the handle behind you. Keep your head looking forward and your chest up, with your knee slightly bent. This will be your starting position.
Descend by flexing the knee and hips, lowering yourself to the ground. Keep your weight on the heel of your foot and maintain your posture throughout the exercise.
At the bottom of the movement, reverse the motion, extending through the hip and knee to return to the starting position.

Begin by flexing the elbow to initiate the movement. Protract your shoulder blades as you do so.

Begin by flexing the elbow to initiate the movement. Protract your shoulder blades as you do so.
At the completion of the motion pause, and then return to the starting position.

name: Suspended Reverse Crunch
instructions: Secure a set of suspension straps with the handles hanging about a foot off of the ground. Move yourself into a pushup plank position facing away from the rack.
Place your feet into the handles. You should maintain a straight posture, not allowing the hips to sag. This will be your starting position.
Begin the movement by flexing the knees and hips, drawing the knees to your torso. As you do so, anteriorly tilt your pelvis, allowing your spine to flex.
At the top of the controlled motion, return to the starting position.

en: abdominals

name: TRX Press Ups Feet Suspended
instructions: Anchor your suspension straps securely to the top of a rack or other object.

Leaning into the straps, take a handle in each hand and move into a push-up plank position. You should be as close to parallel to the ground as you can manage with your arms fully extended, maintaining good posture.
Maintaining a straight, rigid torso, descend slowly by allowing the elbows to flex.
Continue until your elbows break 90 degrees, pausing before you extend to return to the starting position.

en: chest
shoulders
triceps

name: TRX Good Mornings
instructions: Setup: Position the TRX handles at hip height, then stand facing away from the anchor point. Grip & Stance: Place your hands on the handles, which should be around your quad area, slightly above the knees. Step back to take some tension out of the straps, creating a slight forward angle. Keep your feet about hip-width apart. Hip Hinge: Hinge at your hips, pushing your hips backward as if you're trying to touch the wall behind you. Maintain Position: Keep your legs mostly straight, with just a slight bend in the knees, and maintain weight on your heels. Keep your back straight with a slight arch and your core braced. Descending: Lower your torso forward, letting the TRX assist in providing a gentle stretch in your hamstrings and the posterior chain. Stop when you feel a good stretch or when your upper body is almost parallel to the floor. Ascending: Drive through your heels, squeeze your glutes, and push your hips forward to return to the starting upright position. Repeat: Continue the motion for the desired number of repetitions.

en: hamstrings
abdominals
glutes

name: TRX Fallouts
instructions: Adjust the straps so the handles are at an appropriate height, below waist level.
Begin standing and grasping the handles. Lean into the straps, moving to an incline push-up position. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your arms straight, lean further into the suspension straps, bringing your body closer to the ground, allowing your shoulders to extend, raising your arms up and over your head.
Maintain a neutral spine and keep the rest of your body straight, your shoulders being the only joints allowed to move.
Pause during the peak contraction, and then return to the starting position.

name: Stride Jump Crossover
instructions: Stand to the side of a box with your inside foot on top of it, close to the edge.
Begin by swinging the arms upward as you push through the top leg, jumping upward as high as possible. Attempt to drive the opposite knee upward.
Land in the opposite position that you started, on the opposite side of the box. The foot that was initially on the box will now be on the ground, with the opposite foot now on the box.
Repeat the movement, crossing back over to the other side.

name: Standing Two-Arm Overhead Throw
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder width apart holding a medicine ball in both hands.  To begin, reach the medicine ball deep behind your head as you bend the knees slightly and lean back.
Violently throw the ball forward, flexing at the hip and using your whole body to complete the movement.
The medicine ball can be thrown to a partner or to a wall, receiving it as it bounces back.

name: Chained Barbell Squats
instructions: To set up the chains, begin by looping the leader chain over the sleeves of the bar. The heavy chain should be attached using a snap hook. Adjust the length of the lead chain so that a few links are still on the floor at the top of the movement.

Begin by stepping under the bar and placing it across the back of the shoulders. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and rotate your elbows forward, attempting to bend the bar across your shoulders. Remove the bar from the rack, creating a tight arch in your lower back, and step back into position. Place your feet wide for more emphasis on the back, glutes, adductors, and hamstrings. Keep your head facing forward.

With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit back with your hips as much as possible. Ideally, your shins should be perpendicular to the ground. Lower bar position necessitates a greater torso lean to keep the bar over the heels. Continue until you break parallel, which is defined as the crease of the hip being in line with the top of the knee.

Keeping the weight on your heels and pushing your feet and knees out, drive upward as you lead the movement with your head. Continue upward, maintaining tightness head to toe, until you have returned to the starting position.

en: quadriceps

name: Sled Row
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled connected by a rope or chain. Load the sled to an appropriate weight. Face the sled, backing up until there is some tension in the line.
With a handle in each hand, bend the knees slightly, keep your head and chest up, and begin with your arms extended.
To initiate the movement, flex the elbow as you retract your shoulder blades, pulling the sled towards you.
Take a step or two back to get tension in the line and repeat.

en: middle back
biceps

name: Sled Reverse Flye
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled connected by a rope or chain. Load the sled to a light weight.

Face the sled, backing up until there is some tension in the line. Take both handles at arms length at about waist level. Bend the knees slightly and keep your chest and head up. This will be your starting position.
Without flexing the elbow,  pull the handles upward and apart, performing a reverse fly with some external rotation. Your palms should be facing forward as you do this.
Return to the starting position, taking a couple steps back to take the slack out of the line.

en: shoulders

name: Sled Pull
instructions: To begin, load the sled with the desired weight and attach the pulling strap. You can pull with handles, use a harness, or attach the pulling strap to a weight belt.
Typically, sled drags are performed for a distance as fast as possible, from 30-100 feet. The sled can be pulled backwards to emphasize the quadriceps, or forwards to emphasize the posterior chain.
Whether pulling forwards or backwards, lean in the direction of travel and progress by extending through the hips and knees.

name: Sled Overhead Backward Walk
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled connected by a rope or chain. Load the sled to a light weight.
Face the sled, backing up until there is some tension in the line. Hold your hands directly above your head with your elbows extended. This will be your starting position.
Walk backwards, keeping your arms raised above your head. Avoid jerky movements.
This movement is great for training the posture of your posterial shoulder girdle.

name: Sledgehammer Swings
instructions: You will need a tire and a sledgehammer for this exercise. Stand in front of the tire about two feet away from it with a staggered stance. Grip the sledgehammer.
If you are right handed, your left hand should be at the bottom of the handle, and your right hand should be choking up closer to the head.

As you bring the sledge up, your right hand slides toward the head; as you swing down, your right hand will slide down to join your left hand. Slam it down as hard as you can against the tire. Control the bounce of the hammer off of the tire.
Repeat on the other side.

name: Sled Overhead Tricep Extensions
instructions: Attach a dual handled chain or rope attachment to the sled. You should be facing away from the sled, holding a handle in each hand.

Begin the movement by moving forward for one step. Leaning forward, extend through the legs and hips to move, pausing with each step to extend through the elbows, pressing your hands forward. Step forward until you return to the start position prepared to press.

name: Single-Leg Hop Progression
instructions: Arrange a line of cones in front of you. Assume a relaxed standing position, balanced on one leg. Raise the knee of your opposite leg. This will be your starting position.
Hop forward, jumping and landing with the same leg over the cone.
Use a countermovement jump to hop from cone to cone.
At the end, turn around and go back on the other leg.

name: Single-Cone Sprint Drill
instructions: This drill teaches quick foot action. You need a single cone. Begin standing next to the cone with one arm back and one arm forward.
Chop the feet as quickly as possible, blocking with the arms. Circle the cone, keep your knees up, with violent foot action.
Rest after three trips around the cone.

name: Side to Side Box Shuffle
instructions: Stand to one side of the box with your left foot resting on the middle of it.
To begin, jump up and over to the other side of the box, landing with your right foot on top of the box and your left foot on the floor. Swing your arms to aid your movement.
Continue shuffling back and forth across the box.

name: Seated Head Harness Neck Resistance
instructions: Place a neck strap on the floor at the end of a flat bench.  Once you have selected the weights, sit at the end of the flat bench with your feet wider than shoulder width apart from each other.  Your toes should be pointed out.

Slowly move your torso forward until it is almost parallel with the floor.  Using both hands, securely position the neck strap around your head.  Tip:  Make sure the weights are still lying on the floor to prevent any strain on the neck.  Now grab the weight with both hands while elevating your torso back until it is almost perpendicular to the floor.  Note: Your head and torso needs to be slightly tilted forward to perform this exercise.
Now place both hands on top of your knees.  This is the starting position.
Slowly lower your neck down until your chin touches the upper part of your chest while breathing in.
While exhaling, bring your neck back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise puts a lot of stress on the neck muscles and should be approached with caution.  Any sudden jerk or movement can cause you to strain a neck muscle.  It is best to practice the form at first without any weights to get used to the movements required for this exercise.

en: neck

name: Rope Climb
instructions: Grab the rope with both hands above your head. Pull down on the rope as you take a small jump.
Reach up as high as possible with your arms, gripping the rope tightly.
Pull yourself up with your arms, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Continue until you reach the top of the rope.
To lower yourself, reverse this process using a hand over hand motion.
Caution: Carry out safety check first. Do not attempt unattended and without the neccessary safety precautions/personnel.

name: Ring Dips
instructions: Grip a ring in each hand, and then take a small jump to help you get into the starting position with your arms locked out.
Begin by flexing the elbow, lowering your body until your arms break 90 degrees. Avoid swinging, and maintain good posture throughout the descent.
Reverse the motion by extending the elbow, pushing yourself back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Sled Push
instructions: Place your sled on an appropriate surface, loaded to a suitable weight. The sled should provide enough resistance to require effort, but not so heavy that you are significantly slowed down.
You may use the upright or the low handles for this exercise. Place your hands on the handles with your arms extended, leaning into the implement.
With good posture, drive through the ground with alternating, short steps. Move as fast as you can for a short distance.

name: Heavy Bag Thrust
instructions: Utilize a heavy bag for this exercise. Assume an upright stance next to the bag, with your feet staggered, fairly wide apart. Place your hand on the bag at about chest height. This will be your starting position.
Begin by twisting at the waist, pushing the bag forward as hard as possible. Perform this move quickly, pushing the bag away from your body.
Receive the bag as it swings back by reversing these steps.

name: Box Jump (Multiple Response)

Using the arms to aid in the initial burst, jump upward and forward, landing with feet simultaneously on top of the box or platform.
Immediately drop or jump back down to the original starting place; then repeat the sequence.

name: Bench Hops
instructions: Begin with a box or bench 1-2 feet in front of you. Stand with your feet shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.
Perform a short squat in preparation for the jump; swing your arms behind you.
Rebound out of this position, extending through the hips, knees, and ankles to jump as high as possible. Swing your arms forward and up.
Jump over the bench, landing with the knees bent, absorbing the impact through the legs.
Turn around and face the opposite direction, then jump back over the bench.

name: Battle Rope Alternating Waves
instructions: For this exercise you will need a heavy rope anchored at its center 15-20 feet away. Standing in front of the rope, take an end in each hand with your arms extended at your side. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by rapidly raising one arm to shoulder level as quickly as you can.
As you let that arm drop to the starting position, raise the opposite side.
Continue alternating your left and right arms, whipping the ropes up and down as fast as you can.

name: Bear Crawl Sled Drags
instructions: Wearing either a harness or a loose weight belt, attach the chain to the back so that you will be facing away from the sled. Bend down so that your hands are on the ground. Your back should be flat and knees bent. This is your starting position.
Begin by driving with legs, alternating left and right. Use your hands to maintain balance and to help pull. Try to keep your back flat as you move over a given distance.

The ball can be thrown to a partner or bounced off of a wall.

Catch the ball with both hands as it comes down.

name: Return Push from Stance
instructions: You will need a partner for this drill.
Begin in an athletic 2 or 3 point stance.
At the signal, move into a position to receive the pass from your partner.
Catch the medicine ball with both hands and immediately throw it back to your partner.
You can modify this drill by running different routes.

name: Overhead Slam
instructions: Hold a medicine ball with both hands and stand with your feet at shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the countermovement by raising the ball above your head and fully extending your body.
Reverse the motion, slamming the ball into the ground directly in front of you as hard as you can.
Receive the ball with both hands on the bounce and repeat the movement.

name: One-Arm Medicine Ball Slam
instructions: Start in a standing position with a staggered, athletic stance. Hold a medicine ball in one hand, on the same side as your back leg. This will be your starting position.
Begin by winding the arm, raising the medicine ball above your head. As you do so, extend through the hips, knees, and ankles to load up for the slam.
At peak extension, flex the shoulders, spine, and hips to throw the ball hard into the ground directly in front of you.
Catch the ball on the bounce and continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Medicine Ball Throw with Sprint
instructions: Begin in an athletic stance with the knees bent, hips back, and back flat. Hold the medicine ball near your legs. This will be your starting position.
While taking your first step draw the medicine ball into your chest.
As you take the second step, explosively push the ball forward, immediately sprinting for 10 yards after the release. If you are really fast, you can catch your own pass!

name: Medicine Ball Throw; Medicine Ball Snap
instructions: Begin in a three point stance, squatted down with your back flat and one hand on the ground. Place the medicine ball directly in front of you.
To begin, take your first step as you pull the ball to your chest, positioning both hands to prepare for the throw.
As you execute the second step, explosively release the ball forward as hard as possible.

name: Medicine Ball Throw
instructions: Begin standing while facing a wall or a partner.
Using both hands, position the ball behind your head, stretching as much as possible, and forcefully throw the ball forward.

Ensure that you follow your throw through, being prepared to receive your rebound from your throw. If you are throwing against the wall, make sure that you stand close enough to the wall to receive the rebound, and aim a little higher than you would with a partner.

name: Medicine Ball Scoop Throw
instructions: Assume a semisquat stance with a medicine ball in your hands. Your arms should hang so the ball is near your feet.
Begin by thrusting the hips forward as you extend through the legs, jumping up.
As you do, swing your arms up and over your head, keeping them extended, releasing the ball at the peak of your movement. The goal is to throw the ball the greatest distance behind you.

name: Medicine Ball Full Twist
instructions: For this exercise you will need a medicine ball and a partner. Stand back to back with your partner, spaced 2-3 feet apart. This will be your starting position.
Hold the ball in front of the trunk. Open the hips and turn the shoulders at the same time as your partner.
For full rotation, you and your partner should twist in the same direction, i.e. counter-clockwise.
Pass the ball to your partner, and both of you can now twist in the opposite direction to repeat the procedure.

name: Medicine Ball Chest Pass
instructions: You will need a partner for this exercise. Lacking one, this movement can be performed against a wall.
Begin facing your partner holding the medicine ball at your torso with both hands.
Pull the ball to your chest, and reverse the motion by extending through the elbows. For sports applications, you can take a step as you throw.
Your partner should catch the ball, and throw it back to you.
Receive the throw with both hands at chest height.

name: Kneeling Medicine Ball Throw
instructions: Begin in a kneeling position facing a wall or utilize a partner. Hold the ball with both hands tight into the chest.
Execute the pass by exploding forward and outward with the hips while pushing the ball as hard as possible.
Follow through by falling forward, catching yourself with your hands.
Immediately return to an upright position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Backward Medicine Ball Throw
instructions: This exercise is best done with a partner. If you lack a partner, the ball can be thrown and retrieved or thrown against a wall.
Begin standing a few meters in front of your partner, both facing the same direction. Begin holding the ball between your legs.
Squat down and then forcefully reverse direction, coming to full extension and you toss the ball over your head to your partner.
Your partner can then roll the ball back to you. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Board Press
instructions: Begin by lying on the bench, getting your head beyond the bar if possible. One to five boards, made out of 2x6's, can be screwed together and held in place by a training partner, bands, or just tucked under your shirt.

Tuck your feet underneath you and arch your back. Using the bar to help support your weight, lift your shoulder off the bench and retract them, squeezing the shoulder blades together. Use your feet to drive your traps into the bench. Maintain this tight body position throughout the movement.

You can take a standard bench grip, or shoulder width to focus on the triceps. Pull the bar out of the rack without protracting your shoulders. The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart.
Lower the bar to the boards, and then drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.

name: Bench Press
instructions: Begin by lying on the bench, getting your head beyond the bar if possible. Tuck your feet underneath you and arch your back. Using the bar to help support your weight, lift your shoulder off the bench and retract them, squeezing the shoulder blades together. Use your feet to drive your traps into the bench. Maintain this tight body position throughout the movement. However wide your grip, it should cover the ring on the bar. Pull the bar out of the rack without protracting your shoulders. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart.
Lower the bar to your lower chest or upper stomach. The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times.
Pause when the barbell touches your torso, and then drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.

name: Blast Straps Fallout
instructions: Adjust the straps so the handles are at an appropriate height, below waist level.
Begin standing and grasping the handles. Lean into the straps, moving to an incline push-up position. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your arms straight, lean further into the suspension straps, bringing your body closer to the ground, allowing your shoulders to extend, raising your arms up and over your head.
Maintain a neutral spine and keep the rest of your body straight, your shoulders being the only joints allowed to move.
Pause during the peak contraction, and then return to the starting position.

name: Underhand Cable Pulldowns
instructions: Set the weight to the recommended amount and check that the leg brace is set to the correct height. Take a seat facing the machine (tuck your hips in so they are touching the leg brace) and take a shoulder width, underhand grip with your arms extended towards the ceiling. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the bar down towards your chest using the muscles across your back and your biceps. Lean back ever so slightly as your pull the weight downwards and lift your chest as much as you can. Continue to pull the bar down until either your muscles are optimally contracted or it is in contact with your chest.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Chin Ups or Horizontal Barbell Pull Ups.

name: Lat Pulldown Behind The Head
instructions: Set the weight to the recommended amount and check that the leg brace is set to the correct height. Take a seat facing the machine (tuck your hips in so they are touching the leg brace) and adopt a wide, overhand grip with your arms extended towards the ceiling. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the bar down towards the back of your neck. Lean forwards slightly (from your hips, maintaining a straight back) as you pull the bar downwards. It is paramount that the bar does not touch your neck. Continue to descend the bar until you are a few inches away from your neck.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Pull-Ups or Lat Pulldowns.

name: Barbell Sissy Squats
instructions: Grab a barbell and place it across the back of your shoulders. Next step into the sissy squat machine, making sure that your feet are locked in and the pad reaches at least half way up your thighs. Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. You should lean backwards into the movement so that your weight is supported through the pad that is in contact with your calves. Continue to descend until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads . Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from knee issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Leg Presses.

name: Weighted Pull Ups
instructions: Attach the desired weight around your waist via a belt, adopt a wide, overhand (palms facing away from you) grip of a pull up bar. After you have securely taken hold of the handles, take your feet off the floor so that your body is now hanging from your grip. Pull your heels in towards your glutes so your knees are bent to a 90 degree angle and your hips are pushed forwards. Your arms should be at full extension at this point. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, exhale and pull your torso upward towards your hands as you contract your biceps and the muscles across your back (more so with your back). Continue to pull yourself up until your chest is as high as it can be.
After a brief pause, return your torso back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Lat Pulldowns or T-Bar Rows with a Handle.

After a brief pause, return your torso back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Lat Pulldowns or T-Bar Rows with a Handle.

name: V-Bar Pullup
instructions: Start by placing the middle of the V-bar in the middle of the pull-up bar (assuming that the pull-up station you are using does not have neutral grip handles).  The V-Bar handles will be facing down so that you can hang from the pull-up bar through the use of the handles.

Once you securely place the V-bar, take a hold of the bar from each side and hang from it.  Stick your chest out and lean yourself back slightly in order to better engage the lats.  This will be your starting position.

Using your lats, pull your torso up while leaning your head back slightly so that you do not hit yourself with the chin-up bar.  Continue until your chest nearly touches the V-bar.  Exhale as you execute this motion.
After a second hold on the contracted position, slowly lower your body back to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat for the prescribed number of repetitions.
Variations:
If you are new at this exercise and do not have the strength to perform it, use a pull-up assist machine if available.  These machines use weight to help you push your bodyweight.
Otherwise, a spotter holding your legs can help.
On the other hand, more advanced lifters can add weight to the exercise by using a weight belt that allows the addition of weighted plates.

name: Smith Machine Split Squats
instructions: Place a bench an exaggerated step's distance from the smith machine bar, load up the recommended weight and set its height to shoulder height. Place the un-racked bar on the back of your shoulders and position yourself so that one foot is flat on the floor (directly underneath the bar) and the other is behind you, at knee height supported by the bench. Have your arms gently holding the bar to stop it from falling. This will be your starting position.

From here, start to descend by bending at your knees (maintaining good posture) until you create a 90 degree angle at your front knee. As you descend you should be inhaling and ensuring that your front knee does not extend past your toes.
Pause for a brief moment at the bottom of the movement, before exhaling and driving up through your front heel and thigh, until you return to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Bulgarian Split Squats or Split Squats With Dumbbells.

name: Smith Machine Upright Rows
instructions: Load the smith machine with the desired weight and take a with a narrow, overhand grip. Adopt a standing position with the bar resting against your thighs, your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the bar as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the bar up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull the bar upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the bar.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the bar along the same path towards the starting position.
Variations: Dumbbell Upright Rows or Upright Barbell Rows.

name: Smith Machine Stiff Leg Deadlifts
instructions: Load up the smith machine with the required amount of weight and place the bar at mid thigh height. Adopt a standing position with your feet shoulder width apart so that the bar is gently placed against your thighs. Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs). Next, lift the bar off the rack and slightly bend your knees. Tip: Keep a straight back and legs throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the bar close to your thighs as you go. Keep lowering the bar towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.

After a brief pause bring your torso upright again by pushing your hips forward. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variations: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Dead lifts or Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlifts.

Load up the smith machine with the required amount of weight and place the bar at mid thigh height. Adopt a standing position with your feet shoulder width apart so that the bar is gently placed against your thighs. Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs). Next, lift the bar off the rack and slightly bend your knees. Tip: Keep a straight back and legs throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the bar close to your thighs as you go. Keep lowering the bar towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.

After a brief pause bring your torso upright again by pushing your hips forward. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variations: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Dead lifts or Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlifts.

name: Smith Machine Squats
instructions: Load the smith machine with the desired amount of weight and place the bar at shoulder height. Step underneath the bar and rotate it so that the weight is resting across the back of your shoulders. You should take hold of the bar loosely just to make sure it does not fall. Stand with a wider than shoulder width stance, toes pointing (slightly) outward. Throughout the movement you should keep your head up, chest out and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

Descend slowly by bending at your knees and hips until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, pause for a second before pushing your torso back into the starting position through your heels. As you descend inhale and as you ascend, exhale. You should now be at the starting position, ready to go again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always be aware of your posture with this exercise as if performed incorrectly, could cause injury.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Dumbbell Squats.

name: Smith Machine Shrugs
instructions: Set the machine to the desired weight and set the position of the bar so it is mid thigh. Stand with a shoulder width stance facing the bar. Adopt an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs) and keep your arms extended throughout the movement. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale, shrug your shoulders upwards and pull them in towards your ears at the same time. Keep your arms at full extension (a slight bend at your elbows) as you do so. Continue to shrug until you cannot lift your shoulders anymore.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Shrugs or Dumbbell Shrugs.
