Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into your outer calf as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Middle Back Stretch
instructions: Stand with a narrow stance with your hands placed on your hips. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and rotate through your core until you feel a stretch in your back.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

name: Lying Prone Quadriceps
instructions: Lay face down on the floor with your partner kneeling beside you. Flex one knee and raise that leg off the ground, attempting to touch your glutes with your foot.

Have your partner kneel on your right side and place one hand on your lower back or glutes. His/her other hand should grasp your ankle or knee and push your shin toward your butt. You should feel a stretch in your quadriceps.
After 10 seconds, try to push foot back toward the ground against your partner's resistance and hold for 6 seconds.
Following this 6-second contraction, relax and allow your partner to stretch your quads for another 30 seconds. (If you want, try contracting your hamstrings for greater ROM gains.)
Repeat with other leg.

name: Lying Crossover
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs extended.

Cross one leg over your body with the knee bent, attempting to touch the knee to the ground. Your partner should kneel beside you, holding your shoulder down with one hand and controlling the crossed leg with the other. this will be your starting position.
Attempt to raise the bent knee off of the ground as your partner prevents any actual movement.
After 10-20 seconds, relax the leg as your partner gently presses the knee towards the floor. Repeat with the other side.

name: Seated Bent Leg Groin Stretch
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and the soles of the feet pressed together. Have your partner hold your knees. This will be your starting position.
Attempt to squeeze your knees together, while your partner prevents any movement from occurring.
After 10-20 seconds, relax your muscles as your partner gently pushes your knees towards the floor. Be sure to inform your helper when the stretch is adequate to prevent injury or overstretching.

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch (Elevated Leg)
instructions: Lay down, face up on a mat with your arms by your sides and legs flat on the floor.

Lift your left leg up so that your hip is at a 90 degree angle and your heel is extended directly upward towards the ceiling. Link your fingers together around the back of your upper left leg. This will be your starting position.
Gently exhale and pull your leg in towards you torso, ensure you are keeping your leg as straight as possible, until you feel the desired stretch.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: hamstrings

name: Lat Stretch
instructions: Stand with a shoulder width stance either next to a wall or doorway. Elevate one of your arms so your elbow is at 90 degrees and your forearm is vertical from the floor. Place your forearm against the upright surface. This is your starting position.
Gently lean your bodyweight forward until you feel the desired stretch come through your lats.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: lats

name: Foam Roll Latissimus Dorsi
instructions: Lay on a mat, on your left side, with your arms crossed infront of your body and a foam roller placed just below your arm pit. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, put as much of your bodyweight as you can down through the roller as you gently rock your body upward and back, releasing any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: lats

name: Kneeling Quad Stretch
instructions: Adopt a kneeling position on a mat with your knees and heels together.
Lean back until you feel the desired stretch within your thighs. Tip: to emphasise this stretch, look up at the ceiling, arch your lower back gently and pull your shoulders back.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.

en: quadriceps

Hold this position for the recommended duration.

name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
instructions: To get into the starting position for this stretch, you need to drop to the bottom of a lunge position. So, take a big step to split your stance and descend so that your back knee is now on the floor. Next, take a small step forward with your front foot. This is your starting position.
Lean forward so that your bodyweight is being driven onto your front foot and towards the floor through the extension of your pelvis.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: quadriceps

Lean forward so that your bodyweight is being driven onto your front foot and towards the floor through the extension of your pelvis.

name: Kneeling Forearm Stretch
instructions: Adopt an 'all fours' position on a mat but have your fingers pointing your knees rather than away from your body. This will be your starting position.
Whilst maintaining a connection to the floor with your palms, gently lean your bodyweight backwards towards your heels until you create the desired stretch in your forearms.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.

en: forearms

name: Kneeling Arm Drill
instructions: This drill helps increase arm efficiency during the run. Begin kneeling, left foot in front, right knee down. Apply pressure through the front heel to keep your glutes and hamstrings activated.
Begin by blocking the arms in long, pendulum like swings. Close the arm angle, blocking with the arms as you would when jogging, progressing to a run and finally a sprint.
As soon as your hands pass the hip, accelerate them forward during the sprinting motion to move them as quickly as possible.
Switch knees and repeat.

en: shoulders

name: Knee Circles
instructions: Stand with your legs together and hands by your waist.
Now move your knees in a circular motion as you breathe normally.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Laying Knee Across The Body Stretch
instructions: Lay on a mat, on your back, with your legs extended on the floor and arms stretched out wide. Lift your left leg up to create a 90 degree bend at both your knee and hip. Take hold of your left knee with your right hand and pull it across your body towards the floor on your right hand side. Tip: to get a better stretch, turn your head the opposite way to which your knee is travelling and attempt to keep both shoulders on the mat.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

name: IT Band and Glute Stretch
instructions: Lay on a mat, on your back, with your legs extended on the floor. Loop a band over the arch in your right shoe and bring that leg over your body towards the opposite side (whilst keeping it stright). This will be your starting position.
Gradually, pull harder and harder on that band until you acheive the desired amount of tension so you can feel a stretch through you glute and outer thigh.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: abductors

name: Isometric Neck Exercise - Sides
instructions: With your head and neck in a neutral position (normal position with head erect facing forward), place your left hand on the left side of your head.

Now gently push towards the left as you contract the left neck muscles but resisting any movement of your head.  Start with slow tension and increase slowly.  Keep breathing normally as you execute this contraction.
Hold for the recommended number of seconds.
Now release the tension slowly.
Rest for the recommended amount of time and repeat with your right hand placed on the right side of your head.
Variations: You can also do this exercise by placing your hands to the front and then to the back of your head.  First do one side and then the next.

en: neck
With your head and neck in a neutral position (normal position with head erect facing forward), place your left hand on the left side of your head.

Now gently push towards the left as you contract the left neck muscles but resisting any movement of your head.  Start with slow tension and increase slowly.  Keep breathing normally as you execute this contraction.
Hold for the recommended number of seconds.
Now release the tension slowly.
Rest for the recommended amount of time and repeat with your right hand placed on the right side of your head.
Variations: You can also do this exercise by placing your hands to the front and then to the back of your head.  First do one side and then the next.

name: Isometric Neck Exercise - Front And Back
instructions: With your head and neck in a neutral position (normal position with head erect facing forward), place both of your hands on the front side of your head.

Now gently push forward as you contract the neck muscles but resisting any movement of your head.  Start with slow tension and increase slowly.  Keep breathing normally as you execute this contraction.
Hold for the recommended number of seconds.
Now release the tension slowly.
Rest for the recommended amount of time and repeat with your hands placed on the back side of your head.
Variations: You can also do this exercise by placing your hands on the sides of your head.  First do one side and then the next.

name.en: Isometric Neck Exercise - Front And Back

Now gently push forward as you contract the neck muscles but resisting any movement of your head.  Start with slow tension and increase slowly.  Keep breathing normally as you execute this contraction.
Hold for the recommended number of seconds.
Now release the tension slowly.
Rest for the recommended amount of time and repeat with your hands placed on the back side of your head.
Variations: You can also do this exercise by placing your hands on the sides of your head.  First do one side and then the next.

aliases: Isometric Neck Exercise - Front And Back

name: Isometric Chest Squeezes
instructions: Place your palms together in front of your body with your elbows bent at a 90 degree angle. Your fingers should run along side the corresponding opposite digit, pointing away from your body.
Gently squeeze your palms together by contracting your chest muscles (pectorals).
Gradually squeeze harder and harder until you get to the desired amount of tension.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.

en: chest
shoulders
triceps

Gently squeeze your palms together by contracting your chest muscles (pectorals).
Gradually squeeze harder and harder until you get to the desired amount of tension.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.

name: Reverse Iron Cross Stretch
instructions: Lay on a mat, face down, with your arms extended out towards your sides with your palms facing the floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale, bend your left knee and bring your left leg across the back of your body towards the floor and your right hand.
Pause for a brief moment and return your left leg back along the same path back into the starting position. As soon as you get to the starting position, repeat the movement for your right leg.
Repeat for the recommened amount of repetitions.

en: quadriceps
Lay on a mat, face down, with your arms extended out towards your sides with your palms facing the floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale, bend your left knee and bring your left leg across the back of your body towards the floor and your right hand.
Pause for a brief moment and return your left leg back along the same path back into the starting position. As soon as you get to the starting position, repeat the movement for your right leg.
Repeat for the recommened amount of repetitions.

name: Intermediate Hip Flexor & Quad Stretch
instructions: Lay on a mat, face down, with your legs fully extended and flat on the floor. Take a band and loop it over your toes and gently pull it tight with both hands so that your knee bends and your hips are extended towards the floor, stretching your thigh and hip flexors.
Making sure that the band will not slip off your shoe, slowly pull harder on the band so that the stretch becomes greater until you create the desired amount of tension.
Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: quadriceps

Hold this position for the recommended duration and swap sides.

aliases: Intermediate Hip Flexor and Quad Stretch

name: Intermediate Groin Stretch
instructions: Lay on a mat, on your back, with a band looped around the arch in one off your shoes. Both legs should be fully extended on the floor at this point. This will be your starting position.
Extend the leg with the band on out as wide as possible. From there, pull the band so that there is now the desired tension and hold to create a stretch through your groin and hamstrings.
Hold for the recemmended duration and swap sides.

en: hamstrings
Lay on a mat, on your back, with a band looped around the arch in one off your shoes. Both legs should be fully extended on the floor at this point. This will be your starting position.
Extend the leg with the band on out as wide as possible. From there, pull the band so that there is now the desired tension and hold to create a stretch through your groin and hamstrings.
Hold for the recemmended duration and swap sides.

name: Foam Roll Iliotibial Band
instructions: Lay on the floor on your left side with a foam roller underneath your left thigh. Have your left leg extended out straight with your right leg bent over the top with the foot planted on the floor in front of your left thigh. Have your left hand on the floor for support (you can have both if needed). This will be your starting position.

Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into the side of your left thigh as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: abductors

Lay on the floor on your left side with a foam roller underneath your left thigh. Have your left leg extended out straight with your right leg bent over the top with the foot planted on the floor in front of your left thigh. Have your left hand on the floor for support (you can have both if needed). This will be your starting position.

Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into the side of your left thigh as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Hug Knees To Chest
instructions: Lay on the floor, on your back, with your legs extended and your arms by your sides. This will be your starting position.
Lift both your knees in towards your chest and take hold of them with your hands (allow your knee to bend).
Gently pull your knees in towards your chest so that your glutes and lower back can feel a stretch.
Hold for the recommended duration.

en: lower back
glutes

Lift both your knees in towards your chest and take hold of them with your hands (allow your knee to bend).
Gently pull your knees in towards your chest so that your glutes and lower back can feel a stretch.
Hold for the recommended duration.

name: Hip Crossovers
instructions: Lay on a mat, on your back with your arms extended out to your sides with your palms facing the floor. Lift your feet off the floor by creating a 90 degree bend at your knees and hips. Tip: Keep your knees together throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and allow your knees to descend towards the left hand side until they are a few inches from the floor.
Next, exhale and use your core muscles to bring your lower body back along the same path into the starting position.
Pause for a brief moment and repeat the movement towards the right hand side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: lower back
abductors

Lay on a mat, on your back with your arms extended out to your sides with your palms facing the floor. Lift your feet off the floor by creating a 90 degree bend at your knees and hips. Tip: Keep your knees together throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and allow your knees to descend towards the left hand side until they are a few inches from the floor.
Next, exhale and use your core muscles to bring your lower body back along the same path into the starting position.
Pause for a brief moment and repeat the movement towards the right hand side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Prone Hip Circles
instructions: Adopt an 'all fours' position on a mat. This will be your starting position.

Maintaining the 90 degree bend at your knee, lift your right knee off the mat and attempt to make a circle motion from your hip joint by extending out wide and round. As you stretch your way through the movement, press your heel away so that eventually your leg is fully extended behind your body.
Next, tuck your knee underneath your body in towards your chest and start again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions then swap sides.

en: abductors
adductors

Next, tuck your knee underneath your body in towards your chest and start again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions then swap sides.

name: Hanging Lat Stretch
instructions: Take hold of a chin up bar with an overhand grip, straight arm and allow the majority of your body weight be 'hung' onto this arm.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: lats
biceps

Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

name: Laying Hamstring Stretch
instructions: Lay on the floor, on your back, with your right leg extended directly (or as close as you can) towards the ceiling whilst your left leg is bent at a 90 degree angle with your foot flat on the floor. Place a band over the arch in your right foot and pull gently towards the floor so the band has some tension. This will be your starting position.
Relax your right leg and pull the band tight so that you can feel the stretch in your hamstrings.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

en: hamstrings

Place a band over the arch in your right foot and pull gently towards the floor so the band has some tension. This will be your starting position.
Relax your right leg and pull the band tight so that you can feel the stretch in your hamstrings.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

name: Foam Roll Hamstrings
instructions: Take a seat on the floor with a foam roller placed underneath your left hamstring. Have your left leg entended out straight with your right leg bent at a 90 degree angle. Have your hands placed on the floor behind you for support. This will be your starting position.

Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down through your left hamstring as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: hamstrings

Take a seat on the floor with a foam roller placed underneath your left hamstring. Have your left leg entended out straight with your right leg bent at a 90 degree angle. Have your hands placed on the floor behind you for support. This will be your starting position.

Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down through your left hamstring as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Groiners
instructions: Adopt the top of a press up position with your hands just wider that shoulder width, your feet together and your body suspended above the floor in a straight line (between your heels and shoulders). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and jump forward so that your feet land (open toed) next to your hands. Your knees should be wider than your elbows and your pelvis should drop towards the floor once you reach the end of the movement.
Once you have acheived this position, inhale and jump back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: adductors

Adopt the top of a press up position with your hands just wider that shoulder width, your feet together and your body suspended above the floor in a straight line (between your heels and shoulders). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and jump forward so that your feet land (open toed) next to your hands. Your knees should be wider than your elbows and your pelvis should drop towards the floor once you reach the end of the movement.
Once you have acheived this position, inhale and jump back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Groin PNF Stretch
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet raised towards the ceiling.
Have your partner hold your feet or ankles. Abduct your legs as far as you can. This will be your starting position.
Attempt to squeeze your legs together for 10 or more seconds, while your partner prevents you from doing so.

Now, relax the muscles in your legs as your partner pushes your feet apart, stretching as far as is comfortable for you. Be sure to let your partner know when the stretch is adequate to prevent overstretching or injury.

en: adductors

Now, relax the muscles in your legs as your partner pushes your feet apart, stretching as far as is comfortable for you. Be sure to let your partner know when the stretch is adequate to prevent overstretching or injury.

name: Groin & Back Stretch
instructions: Take a seat on the floor, bend your knees and allow them to extend out wide whilst the soles of your shoes rest flat together. Interlink your fingers around the back of your head and gently pull your head (therefore chin) down towards your chest. This will be your starting position.
Curl your abdominals and pull down gently with your hands so that your elbows are placed against your knees, emphasising the stretch through your groin.
Hold this position for the recommended duration.

en: adductors

name: Glute Stretch - One Knee To Chest
instructions: Lay on the floor, on your back, with your legs extended and your arms by your sides. This will be your starting position.
Lift your right knee in towards your chest and take hold of it with your hands (allow your knee to bend).
Gently pull your knee in towards your chest so that your glutes and lower back can feel a stretch.
Hold for the recommended duration and swap sides.

name.en: Glute Stretch - One Knee To Chest

hamstrings

aliases: Glute Stretch - One Knee To Chest

name: Foam Roll Feet
instructions: Take a seat on a bench/chair and remove your shoes. Place the foam roller underneath the arches in your feet and lean forward so that your weight is putting pressure into the foam roller. This will be your starting position.
Gently roll your feet forward and back so the muscles relieve any tension that they may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: calves

Take a seat on a bench/chair and remove your shoes. Place the foam roller underneath the arches in your feet and lean forward so that your weight is putting pressure into the foam roller. This will be your starting position.
Gently roll your feet forward and back so the muscles relieve any tension that they may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Foam Roll Upper Back
instructions: Take a seat on the floor with a foam roller placed just behind you. Lean backwards onto the foam roller with your upper back the point of contact. Cross your arms in front of your body and roll your shoulders forwards. Keep your knees bent at a 90 degree angle with your feet flat on the floor. This will be your starting position.
Lift your hips off the floor and exert pressure through one side of your back by leaning across. Gently roll the muscles so that you relieve any tensions that you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: middle back
traps

Lift your hips off the floor and exert pressure through one side of your back by leaning across. Gently roll the muscles so that you relieve any tensions that you may have.

name: Foam Roll Quadriceps
instructions: Lay on the floor (face down) with a foam roller underneath your right thigh. Have your left leg bent and out to one side so that your toes are on the floor. This way you can put as much pressure as possible into the right quadricep. Support your upper body by placing your hands on the floor. This will be your starting position.
Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into your thigh as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: quadriceps

Lay on the floor (face down) with a foam roller underneath your right thigh. Have your left leg bent and out to one side so that your toes are on the floor. This way you can put as much pressure as possible into the right quadricep. Support your upper body by placing your hands on the floor. This will be your starting position.
Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into your thigh as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Foam Roll Adductors
instructions: Lay on the floor (face down) with a foam roller underneath your inner right thigh. Have both your legs straight but with a slight bent. Support your upper body by placing your left forearm on the floor and lift your right shoulder by leaning onto your right hand. This will be your starting position.
Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into your inner thigh as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: adductors

Support your upper body by placing your left forearm on the floor and lift your right shoulder by leaning onto your right hand. This will be your starting position.
Lift your hips up and off the floor and put as much pressure down into your inner thigh as you possibly can. Roll your body back and forward on the foam roller to release any tension you may have.

name: Foam Roll Lower Back
instructions: Take a seat on the floor with a foam roller placed just behind you. Lean backwards onto the foam roller with your lower back the point of contact. Cross your arms infront of your body and roll your shoulders forwards. Keep your knees bent at a 90 degree angle with your feet flat on the floor. This will be your starting position.
Lift your hips off the floor and exert pressure through one side of your back by leaning across. Gently roll the muscles so that you relieve any tensions that you may have.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: lower back

name: Dynamic hamstring stretch
instructions: Lay down, face up on a mat with your arms by your sides and legs flat on the floor.

Lift your left leg up so that your hip is at a 90 degree angle. Link your fingers together around the back of your upper left leg (just below your knee). Keep your torso in contact with the floor. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and extend your left heel directly up towards the ceiling until your leg is as straight as possible.
Inhale and return your lower leg back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: hamstrings
calves

Exhale and extend your left heel directly up towards the ceiling until your leg is as straight as possible.
Inhale and return your lower leg back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Dynamic Chest Stretch
instructions: Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your arms at full extension directly out in front of you. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your arms parallel with the floor, open them up so that they are now extended out to your sides. Tip: to emphasise this stretch, try to get the back of your hands together behind your body.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: chest
middle back

Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Dynamic Back Stretch
instructions: Your starting position will be a standing shoulder width stance.
Keeping your arms straight, exhale and swing them up above your head so that they are vertical from the floor.
Once you have acheived full range of motion about your shoulder joints, inhale and return your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

en: lats

Once you have acheived full range of motion about your shoulder joints, inhale and return your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Cross-Body Back Stretch
instructions: Take a seat on the floor with your right leg extended out in front of you with your left leg bent at a 90 degree angle placed over the top of your right leg.
Place your right elbow (arm straight) on the outside of your left knee with your left hand on the floor behind you.
Rotate your torso anti clockwise. Tip: To emphasise this stretch put pressure through your right elbow into your left knee.
Hold for the recommended duration.

en: lower back
abductors
glutes

name: Chin To Chest Stretch
instructions: Take a seat on the floor with your legs extended out in front of you.
Interlink your fingers behind your head and gently start to pull your head (and in turn your chin) down towards your chest.
Once your chin is in contact with your chest, hold the position for the recommended amount of time.

en: neck
traps

Interlink your fingers behind your head and gently start to pull your head (and in turn your chin) down towards your chest.
Once your chin is in contact with your chest, hold the position for the recommended amount of time.

name: Chest Stretch on Stability Ball
instructions: Adopt an 'all fours' position with a swiss ball next to your right elbow. Lift your right elbow up and on top of the swiss ball so that your forearm is supported by the ball too. This is your starting position.
Rotate your right shoulder towards the floor, creating a stretch in your right pectoral muscle.
Hold this position for the recommended amount of time before switching sides.

en: chest

name: Chair Upper Body Stretch
instructions: Take a seat on a chair (or bench) and lean your torso forward so that your chest is as close to your knees as possible.
Take hold of the rear chair legs (or bench) with straight arms and pull your torso towards the floor.
Hold this stretch for the recommended amount of time.

en: shoulders
biceps
chest

name: Chair Lower Back Stretch
instructions: Take a seat in a chair with perfect posture and extend your right arm directly upward towards the ceiling.
Take hold of the chair with your left hand as you lean across to your left hand side.
Hold this stretch for the recommended amount of time and swap sides.

en: lats
lower back

Hold this stretch for the recommended amount of time and swap sides.

name: Chair Leg Extended Stretch
instructions: Take a seat in a chair with perfect posture and grab hold of the sides with your hands.
Raise one leg up straight out in front of you with your toes pulled in towards your shin.
Exhale and slowly move that leg outward until you cannot go any further.
Inhale and return your leg back into the starting position along the same path.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: hamstrings

name: Cat Stretch
instructions: Your starting position will be on your hands and knees in an 'all-fours' position.

Once you are in this position, breathe normally and extend your upper back upward towards the ceiling. Tip: To emphasise the stretch, slowly start to walk your hands in towards you knees. Make sure your back is extended towards the ceiling as you do so, you should also feel a stretch through your forearms.
Hold this position for the recommended amount of time.

en: lower back
middle back

Make sure your back is extended towards the ceiling as you do so, you should also feel a stretch through your forearms.

name: Carioca Quick Step
instructions: The carioca quick step simulates coming out of the lateral power motion.
Begin with your feet a few inches apart and your left arm up in a relaxed, athletic position.
With your right foot, quick step behind and pull the knee up.
Fire your arms back up when you pull the right knee, being sure that your knee goes straight up and down. Avoid turning your feet as you move and continue to look forward as you move to the side.

name: Calf Stretch Hands Against Wall
instructions: Adopt a split stance (one foot in front of the other) a couple of feet away from a wall. Lean forward so that your heels remain flat on the floor with your hands supporting the majority of your weight against the wall. Tip: The further you stand away from the wall, the greater the stretch will be but remember to keep your heels as flat on the floor as possible. Push your hips forward to emphasise this stretch.
Hold for the recommended amount of time and swap sides.
Variations: Standing Calf Stretch or Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall.

en: calves

Adopt a split stance (one foot in front of the other) a couple of feet away from a wall. Lean forward so that your heels remain flat on the floor with your hands supporting the majority of your weight against the wall. Tip: The further you stand away from the wall, the greater the stretch will be but remember to keep your heels as flat on the floor as possible. Push your hips forward to emphasise this stretch.
Hold for the recommended amount of time and swap sides.
Variations: Standing Calf Stretch or Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall.

name: Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall
instructions: Adopt a narrow standing stance a couple of feet away from a wall. Lean forward so that your heels remain flat on the floor with your forearms supporting the majority of your weight against the wall. Tip: The further you stand away from the wall, the greater the stretch will be but remember to keep your heels as flat on the floor as possible.
Hold for the recommended amount of time.
Variation: Standing Calf Stretch.

en: calves

Adopt a narrow standing stance a couple of feet away from a wall. Lean forward so that your heels remain flat on the floor with your forearms supporting the majority of your weight against the wall. Tip: The further you stand away from the wall, the greater the stretch will be but remember to keep your heels as flat on the floor as possible.
Hold for the recommended amount of time.

name: Calf Stretch
instructions: Adopt a standing split stance with your legs a single step apart. Slightly bend both of your knees and whilst keeping your rear heel planted, exert pressure onto the front foot, therefore stretching your rear calf.
Hold this position for the recommended time and swap sides.
Variation: Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall.

en: calves

Adopt a standing split stance with your legs a single step apart. Slightly bend both of your knees and whilst keeping your rear heel planted, exert pressure onto the front foot, therefore stretching your rear calf.
Hold this position for the recommended time and swap sides.
Variation: Calf Stretch Elbows Against Wall.

name: Foam Roll Calves
instructions: Take a seat on the floor with your legs out straight, your calves on top of a foam roller. You can either cross your legs over or have one foot planted on the floor to exert extra force into the calf being rolled. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, place your hands on the floor by the side of your hips and lift your pelvis off the floor. Put as much pressure down through your calves as possible and roll from the very top of the muscle down towards the ankle (make each rolling motion as large as possible to engage as many muscle fibres as possible). Once you reach the bottom of your calf, roll back upwards.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

en: calves

To initiate the movement, place your hands on the floor by the side of your hips and lift your pelvis off the floor. Put as much pressure down through your calves as possible and roll from the very top of the muscle down towards the ankle (make each rolling motion as large as possible to engage as many muscle fibres as possible). Once you reach the bottom of your calf, roll back upwards.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: broomstick stretch
instructions: Adopt a shoulder width, standing stance. Grab hold of a broomstick with both hands, with a wide overhand grip.
Exhale as you bring the broomstick out, up and over your head. Keep your arms extended for as long as you can until your elbows start to bend as you bring the broomstick over your head.
Inhale and return the broomstick back along the same arcing path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Remain aware of your posture and your core.

shoulders

name: Brachialis-SMR
instructions: Lie on your side, with your upper arm against the foam roller. The upper arm should be more or less aligned with your body, with the outside of the bicep pressed against the foam roller.
Raise your hips off of the floor, supporting your weight on your arm and on your feet. Hold for 10-30 seconds, and then switch sides.

en: biceps

Raise your hips off of the floor, supporting your weight on your arm and on your feet. Hold for 10-30 seconds, and then switch sides.

name: Bicep Stretch Against A Wall
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and your partner standing behind you. Extend your arms straight behind you with your palms facing each other. Your partner will hold your wrists for you. This will be the starting position.
Attempt to flex your elbows, while your partner prevents any actual movement.

After 10-20 seconds, relax your arms while your partner gently pulls your wrists up to stretch your biceps. Be sure to let your partner know when the stretch is appropriate to prevent injury or overstretching.

name: Behind Head Chest Stretch
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs comfortably in front of you. Your chest should be up and your back straight. Put your hands on the back of your head and interlace your fingers. This will be your starting position

Have your partner stand behind you and put his/her hands on the inside of your elbows. Allow them to pull back on your elbows until you feel a stretch in your chest and front delts. Hold for 10 seconds.
After 10 seconds, try to bring your elbows in against your partner's resistance. Hold that contraction for 6 seconds.
Now, relax your muscles and have your partner gently pull the elbows back as far as it comfortable for you for 30 seconds. (If you want, try contracting your upper back for greater ROM gains.)

name: Arm Circles
instructions: Adopt a shoulder width standing stance. Elevate your out stretched arms to shoulder height so that they are parallel with the floor. This will be your starting position.
Imagine you are drawing circles with your hands but the movement comes from your shoulders.
The bigger the circle the more rotation your will get from your shoulder joint.
Complete the recommended amount of reptetitions or time set.

name: Foam Roll Tibialis Anterior
instructions: Begin seated on the ground with your legs bent and your feet on the floor.

Using a Muscle Roller or a rolling pin, apply pressure to the muscles on the outside of your shins. Work from just below the knee to above the ankle, pausing at points of tension for 10-30 seconds. Repeat on the other leg.

name: Ankle On The Knee
instructions: Lay on your back with your knees bent to a 90 degre angle.
Once you have done this, lift your right foot up and place your ankle across your left knee.

Reach through the gap you have just created with your legs and pull your left thigh in towards your chest. Tip: Keep your shoulders and neck relaxed. Straighten your left leg if you wish to emphasise the movement or even stretch your left hamstring at the same time.
Hold for the recommended time and swap sides.
Variation: Foam Roll Glutes.

en: glutes

Once you have done this, lift your right foot up and place your ankle across your left knee.

Reach through the gap you have just created with your legs and pull your left thigh in towards your chest. Tip: Keep your shoulders and neck relaxed. Straighten your left leg if you wish to emphasise the movement or even stretch your left hamstring at the same time.
Hold for the recommended time and swap sides.
Variation: Foam Roll Glutes.

name: All Fours Quad Stretch
instructions: Your starting position will be on your hands and knees (in an 'all-fours' position). Lift your left leg up and off the mat and take hold of your ankle behind your body with your left hand.
At this point you should be feeling a stretch through your quadricep and hip flexors.
Push your hips towards the floor to emphasise the stretch.
Hold for the recommended time and switch sides.
Variations: Kneeling Hip Flexor or Kneeling Quad Stretch.

en: quadriceps

name: Adductor Stretch
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet raised towards the ceiling.
Have your partner hold your feet or ankles. Abduct your legs as far as you can. This will be your starting position.
Attempt to squeeze your legs together for 10 or more seconds, while your partner prevents you from doing so.

Now, relax the muscles in your legs as your partner pushes your feet apart, stretching as far as is comfortable for you. Be sure to let your partner know when the stretch is adequate to prevent overstretching or injury.

name: Kettlebell Bent Over Rows
instructions: Place two kettlebells in front of your feet. Bend your knees slightly and then push your butt out as much as possible as you bend over to get in the starting position.
Grab both kettlebells and pull them to your stomach, retracting your shoulder blades and flexing the elbows.  Keep your back straight. Lower and repeat.

name: Kettlebell Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebells up so that your hands are beside your ears, elbows are flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees). Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and press the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells back along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Double Kettlebell Cleans
instructions: Place two kettlebells between your feet. To get in the starting position, push your butt back and look straight ahead.
Clean the kettlebells to your shoulders by extending through the legs and hips as you raise the kettlebells towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrists as you do so.
Lower the kettlebells back to the starting position and repeat.

name: Kettlebell Turkish Get Up
instructions: Grab a mat and a kettlebell in your right hand (with an overhand grip) and lay down on your back. Press your right arm towards the ceiling so that it is vertical from the floor with the kettlebell resting on the back of your forearm. Your left knee should be bent to a 90 degree angle with your foot planted on the floor, your right leg should be extended, flat on the floor. Keep your sight focused on the bottom of the kettlebell. This is your starting position.
To initiate this movement, exhale and bring your torso up and off the floor until you are sitting upright. Tip: Keep your arm pressed towards the ceiling throughout.

Next, inhale and place your left arm out wide on the floor and bring your bodyweight across onto it. As you do so, bring your right leg around and under your body so that you are now at the bottom of a lunge.
Exhale and elevate your body into a standing position. Remember to keep the kettlebell pressed towards the ceiling and maintain good posture throughout.
After a brief pause, return your body back to its starting position along the same path.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Push Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebells up so that your hands are beside your ears, elbows are flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees). Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and gently bend at your knees and hips so that you create a small squat. Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels and thighs to press the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells back along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Plyo Kettlebell Press Ups
instructions: Grab a kettlebell and place it on the floor in front of you. Next, kneel down and lean forward so that you are now weight baring on your arms (one of which is holding onto the kettlebell handle). Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be one of your hands, the kettlebell and your feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whist flaring your elbows out wide. Continue until your elbows are at a 90 degree angle.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms as hard as you can to generate enough force so that you 'jump' your torso across to the opposite side of the kettlebell whilst exchanging the hand that holds the handle.

Land straight into your next repetition so bend at your elbows and inhale on your descent until your elbows are at 90 degrees, then power up as hard as you can whilst exhaling and 'jump' your torso across to the opposite side of the kettlebell and exchange your grip once more.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Press Ups or Dumbbell Bench Press.

name: Single Arm Overhead Kettlebell Squats
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your right hand and extend it above your head vertically from the floor with an overhand grip so the kettlebell is resting on the back of your forearm. Adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance with your left arm behind your back. This will be your starting position. Tip: Your arm will remain vertical from the floor throughout!
To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips to descend your pelvis either in line or just past your knees. Tip: Keep a straight back throughout and maintain good posture.
Once you get to the bottom of the movement, exhale and press through your heels to extend your hip and knee joints, elevating your torso back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Overhead Squats.

name: One-Arm Kettlebell Split Snatch
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your right hand with a neutral grip (palm facing your thigh) and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor beside your torso. This will be your starting position.

To Initiate the movement, inhale and gently squat by bending at your knees and hips. As you do so bring the kettlebell between your legs and push it towards the floor. Once you are half way down into a squatting position, pull the kettlebell up and towards the ceiling until your arm is vertical from the floor and the kettlebell is resting on the back of your forearm. Simultaneously exhale and immediately press through your thighs and extend your hips forward so much so that you jump off the floor slightly. This will allow you to split your stance and descend into a lunge position (90 degrees at both knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell down towards your shoulder before returning it into its starting position as you ascend your body into your original standing position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Double Kettlebell Jerk or Kettlebell Snatch.

en: shoulders
hamstrings
quadriceps

Grab a kettlebell in your right hand with a neutral grip (palm facing your thigh) and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor beside your torso. This will be your starting position.

To Initiate the movement, inhale and gently squat by bending at your knees and hips. As you do so bring the kettlebell between your legs and push it towards the floor. Once you are half way down into a squatting position, pull the kettlebell up and towards the ceiling until your arm is vertical from the floor and the kettlebell is resting on the back of your forearm. Simultaneously exhale and immediately press through your thighs and extend your hips forward so much so that you jump off the floor slightly. This will allow you to split your stance and descend into a lunge position (90 degrees at both knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell down towards your shoulder before returning it into its starting position as you ascend your body into your original standing position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Double Kettlebell Jerk or Kettlebell Snatch.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Split Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebell up to your shoulder and allow it to rest against the back of your forearm. At this point, your elbow should be bent to 90 degrees and flared out wide. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement is to descend into a squatting motion by bending at your knees and hips. Once you get half way down, drive upward through your heels and quads in order to start elevating your torso and kettlebell, exhale as you do so.

Generate enough force so that you jump off the floor and land in a split stance to continue descending into a lunge. Simultaneously, press the kettlebell above your head until your elbow is locked out. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout.
Return your body to a standing position with your arm and kettlebell extended towards the ceiling. After a brief pause, return the kettlebell back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Clean and Jerk or Kettlebell Jerk.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Rows
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your left hand with a neutral grip (palm facing towards the right). Place this kettlebell just inside of your right foot whilst taking a big step backwards with your left foot. Bend your right knee and support your bodyweight on your thigh through your right forearm. Your back should be straight and your left arm should be hanging perpendicular to the floor as the kettlebell is now directly under your chest. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your left elbow up towards the ceiling whilst brushing your torso with it as you go. Continue to pull the kettlebell upward until the muscles in your back are as tight as they can be. Tip: Your arms should remain as passive as possible throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Low Cable Row or Single Arm Row.

After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Shoulder Press
instructions: Clean a kettlebell to your shoulder. Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebell towards your shoulder. Rotate your wrist as you do so, so that the palm faces inward. This will be your starting position.
Look at the kettlebell and press it up and out until it is locked out overhead.
Lower the kettlebell back to your shoulder under control and repeat. Make sure to contract your lat, butt, and stomach forcefully for added stability and strength.

name: One-Arm Kettlebell Floor Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your left hand with an overhand grip (palm facing away from you) and lay on your back. Have your arm bent at a 90 degree angle with your elbow flared out wide (the kettlebell should be resting on the back of your forearm at this point). Finally have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle whilst keeping your feet on the floor. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by exhaling and pressing the kettlebell towards the ceiling until your elbow joint is nearly at full extension.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly return the kettlebell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Kettlebell Floor Press or Chest Press.

name: Kettlebell Lunge Pass Through
instructions: To begin this movement, stand with a kettlebell in your left hand (neutral grip), arms by your sides with your feet shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, inhale and stride forward with your right foot, descend your left knee until it nearly touches the floor creating a 90 degree angle at both knee joints. At this point your right knee should stay over the top of your right foot, with your torso upright. As you drop your bodyweight down into this position you are going to pass the kettlebell through the gap created underneath your right thigh and hand it over to your right hand.
After a brief pause, exhale and push through the heel of your front foot to bring your torso back along the same path into the starting position again.
Repeat the movement with the opposite leg and repeat until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Lunges or Dumbbell Walking Lunges.

To begin this movement, stand with a kettlebell in your left hand (neutral grip), arms by your sides with your feet shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, inhale and stride forward with your right foot, descend your left knee until it nearly touches the floor creating a 90 degree angle at both knee joints. At this point your right knee should stay over the top of your right foot, with your torso upright. As you drop your bodyweight down into this position you are going to pass the kettlebell through the gap created underneath your right thigh and hand it over to your right hand.
After a brief pause, exhale and push through the heel of your front foot to bring your torso back along the same path into the starting position again.
Repeat the movement with the opposite leg and repeat until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Lunges or Dumbbell Walking Lunges.

name: Leg-Over Floor Press
instructions: Lie on the floor with a kettlebell in your left hand. Bend your left leg to a 90-degree angle and bring this over your body so your knee is close to the floor.
Whilst in this position, exhale and extend your left-hand (and kettlebell) towards the ceiling. Continue with this movement until your elbow is nearly at full extension.
Once at the top of the movement, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning the kettlebell along the same path, back to it's starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Windmill
instructions: Place a kettlebell in front of your lead foot and clean and press it overhead with your opposite arm. Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebell towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrist as you do so, so that the palm faces forward. Press it overhead by extending the elbow.

Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the locked out kettlebell. Bending at the hip to one side, sticking your butt out, slowly lean until you can touch the floor with your free hand. Keep your eyes on the kettlebell that you hold over your head at all times.
Pause for a second after reaching the ground and reverse the motion back to the starting position.

name: Kettlebell Turkish Get-Up (Squat style)
instructions: Lie on your back on the  floor and press a kettlebell to the top position by extending the elbow. Bend the knee on the same side as the kettlebell.
Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, pivot to the opposite side and use your non- working arm to assist you in driving forward to the lunge position.

Using your free hand, push yourself to a seated position, then progressing to your feet. While looking up at the kettlebell, slowly stand up. Reverse the motion back to the starting position and repeat.

name: Kettlebell Thruster
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebells up so that your hands are beside your ears, elbows are flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees). Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement to minimise the risk of an injury. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that you descend into a squat. Once your thighs are either parallel to the floor, or just past, immediately exhale and use the power you have generated in your legs to drive through your heels and thighs to press the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells back along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Single Arm Kettlebell Push Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Swings
instructions: Grab a kettlebell with your right hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with an overhand grip (palm facing behind you) and stand upright with the weight in front of you. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebell between your legs and push your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.
Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebell to head height with your arm full extended as you go.
Inhale and return the kettlebell along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Squat Jumps or TRX Squat Jumps.

name: Kettlebell Sumo High Pulls
instructions: Take hold of a kettlebell by its handle in both hands (overhand grip) and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and allow your arms to hang straight towards the floor in front of your torso. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards, this will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees and hips. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. Continue in this descent until your thighs are either parallel to the floor or just past.

After a brief pause, exhale and simultaneously push through your heels, thighs and hips to drive your torso back along the same path into the starting position whilst pulling the kettlebell up towards your chin. Keep your elbows higher than the kettlebell at all times and keep it close to your body.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path in to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Barbell Sumo Squats or Dumbbell Sumo Squats.

name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
instructions: Take hold of a kettlebell by its handle in both hands (overhand grip) and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and keep your arms as close to your chest as possible. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards, this will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. Continue in this descent until your thighs are either parallel to the floor or just past.
After a brief pause, exhale and push through your heels, thighs and hips to bring your torso back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Sumo Dumbbell Squats or Barbell Side Lunges.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Grab a kettlebell with your right hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with a neutral grip (palm facing your pocket) and stand upright with the weight by your side. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebell between your legs and rotate it so you have an overhand grip (palm facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebell above your head until your arm is nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the kettlebell so that your palm is facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearm.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Double Kettlebell Snatch or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

name: Kettlebell Seesaw Press
instructions: Clean two kettlebells two your shoulders.
Press one kettlebell.

instructions: Sit on the floor and spread your legs out comfortably.

name: Kettlebell Pistol Squat
instructions: Pick up a kettlebell with two hands and hold it by the horns. Hold one leg off of the floor and squat down on the other.
Squat down by flexing the knee and sitting back with the hips, holding the kettlebell up in front of you.
Hold the bottom position for a second and then reverse the motion, driving through the heel and keeping your head and chest up.
Lower yourself again and repeat.

name: Kettlebell Pirate Ships

Allow the bell to drop as you rotate to the opposite side, again raising the kettlebell to head height.
Repeat for the desired amount of repetitions.

name: Single Leg Kettlebell Deadlifts
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your left hand with a neutral grip (palm facing inwards) and adopt a single leg stance on your right leg. This will be your starting position.
To initiate this movement, inhale and slowly lean your torso forward and push the kettlebell down towards the floor, maintaining a straight back.
As your descend your torso towards the floor, lift your left leg up behind you and keep your right leg straight.
Once the kettlebell is an inch off the floor, exhale and return your torso along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Dumbbell Stiff Leg Dead Lifts or Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlifts.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebell up to your shoulder and allow it to rest against the back of your forearm. At this point, your elbow should be bent to 90 degrees and flared out wide. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement is to descend into a squatting motion by bending at your knees and hips. Once you get half way down, drive upward through your heels and quads in order to start elevating your torso and kettlebell, exhale as you do so.

Generate enough force so that you jump off the floor and press the kettlebell above your head until your elbow is locked out. Simultaneously, land into a deep squatting position to cushion the downward force. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Return your body to a standing position with your arm and kettlebell extended towards the ceiling. After a brief pause, return the kettlebell back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Clean and Jerk or Kettlebell Clean and Jerk.

name: Kettlebell Front Squats
instructions: Take hold of a kettlebell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) so that they are running down the back of each forearm. Keep them close to your body throughout the movement.
Adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance with your toes gently pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the kettlebells to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and if you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variation: Barbell Front Squats.

name: Kettlebell Figure Of 8
instructions: Grab and place a kettlebell of the desired weight between your legs. Bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. At this point, the kettlebell should be directly under your chest as your right arm takes hold of the handle and is perpendicular to the floor. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, gently lift the kettlebell off the floor and swing it between your legs whilst receiving the handle in your left hand behind your left leg. Once you have done this, bring the kettlebell around the outside of your left leg. Continue to wrap around your leg until you bring the kettlebell between your legs where you then pass the handle over to your right hand (reaching from being your right leg).

Similarly, bring the weight around the outside of your right leg until you find yourself between your legs once more. Pass the kettlebell over to your left hand which is reaching from behind your left leg.
This will form a 'Figure of 8' pattern around your legs. Keep your breathing consistent throughout the movement and keep your back as straight as possible to reduce the risk of injury.
Continue in this manner until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Kettlebell Swings.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Dead Cleans
instructions: Grab a kettlebell and place it on the floor between your wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with an overhand grip. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, bend at your knees and hips (more so with your knees than hips) descending into a squat position whilst maintaining a straight back. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point.
Next, drive through your heels and quads and start elevating the kettlebell towards your shoulder.
Next you need to drive your pelvis forward when the kettlebell gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the kettlebell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the kettlebell further.
Once you are in a standing position, flip the kettlebell over your wrist so that it is resting on the back of your forearm.
Whilst maintaining good posture, bring the kettlebell back down along the same path to its starting position, reset, and go again!
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cleans or Dumbbell Cleans.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Clean & Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in one hand, with a slightly wider than shoulder width overhand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arm to hang perpendicular to the floor until the kettlebell is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the kettlebell. Your back should remain straight and your arm should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the kettlebell gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the kettlebell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the kettlebell further. Flare your elbow up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the kettlebell and rotate your elbow under and in so that the kettlebell flips up and over your wrist to rest on the back of your forearm. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to jump into a split stance (top of a lunge). At the same time as powering the body into a jump, aggressively press the kettlebell above your head until your elbow is locked out.
Step forward with your rear leg so that you are now positioned under the kettlebell. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the kettlebell back into the starting position by reversing the upward motion, making sure you keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Clean & Jerk or Dumbbell Clean.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the kettlebell gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the kettlebell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the kettlebell further. Flare your elbow up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the kettlebell and rotate your elbow under and in so that the kettlebell flips up and over your wrist to rest on the back of your forearm. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to jump into a split stance (top of a lunge). At the same time as powering the body into a jump, aggressively press the kettlebell above your head until your elbow is locked out.
Step forward with your rear leg so that you are now positioned under the kettlebell. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the kettlebell back into the starting position by reversing the upward motion, making sure you keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Clean & Jerk or Dumbbell Clean.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Cleans
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your right hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs) and stand upright with your arm hanging perpendicular to the floor in front of your body. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebell between your legs, you should feel as though you are pushing your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebell up towards shoulder height (your elbow should be flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees)). As you do so, flip the kettlebell so that your palm is now facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearm.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Double Kettlebell Snatch or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

name: Seated Kettlebell Arnold Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with the back support (just off) vertical. Hold two kettlebells with an overhand grip (palms facing forward) allowing them to rest on the back of your forearms.

To perform the movement, bring the kettlebells down towards your torso until you reach 90 degrees at the elbows. Rotate your wrists as you bring your elbows in towards each other, in front of your body. At this point your palms should be facing your torso. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.

After a brief pause, reverse the movement and press the kettlebells towards the ceiling, whilst rotating your palms back to the original position (facing forward). Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Seated Cable Shoulder Press.

en: shoulders

To perform the movement, bring the kettlebells down towards your torso until you reach 90 degrees at the elbows. Rotate your wrists as you bring your elbows in towards each other, in front of your body. At this point your palms should be facing your torso. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.

After a brief pause, reverse the movement and press the kettlebells towards the ceiling, whilst rotating your palms back to the original position (facing forward). Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Seated Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Extended Range One-Arm Kettlebell Floor Press
instructions: Lie on the floor and position a kettlebell for one arm to press. The kettlebell should be held by the handle. The leg on the same side that you are pressing should be bent, with the knee crossing over the midline of the body.
Press the kettlebell by extending the elbow and adducting the arm, pressing it above your body. Return to the starting position.

name: Double Kettlebell Windmill
instructions: Place a kettlebell in front of your front foot and clean and press a kettlebell overhead with your opposite arm. Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebell towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrist as you do so, so that the palm faces forward.

Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the locked out kettlebell.
Bending at the hip to one side, sticking your butt out, slowly lean until you can retrieve the kettlebell from the floor. Keep your eyes on the kettlebell that you hold over your head at all times.
Pause for a second after retrieving the kettlebell from the ground and reverse the motion back to the starting position.

name: Double Kettlebell Split Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in both hands with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and bring them up to your shoulders (the kettlebells should be resting on the back of your forearms at this point). Adopt a shoulder width standing stance with your elbows flared out wide. This will be your starting position.

To Initiate the movement, inhale and gently squat by bending at your knees and hips. Once you are half way down into a squatting position exhale and immediately press through your thighs and shoulders to elevate not only your arms (and therefore the kettlebells) into full extension but to jump off the floor slightly. This will allow you to split your stance and descend into a lunge position (90 degrees at both knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells along the same path, back into their starting position as you ascend your body into your original standing position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Kettlebell Jerk or Clean and Jerk.
