name: Double Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebells with a neutral grip (palms facing together) and stand upright with the weights by your side. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebells between your legs and rotate them so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the kettlebells so that your palms are facing forward with the weights running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

name: Double Kettlebell Push Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebells up so that your hands are beside your ears, elbows are flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees). Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and gently bend at your knees and hips so that you create a small squat. Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels and thighs to press the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells back along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Bottoms-Up Clean From The Hang Position
instructions: Initiate the exercise by standing upright with a kettlebell in one hand.
Swing the kettlebell back forcefully and then reverse the motion forcefully.
Crush the kettlebell handle as hard as possible and raise the kettlebell to your shoulder.

name: Bent Press
instructions: Clean a kettlebell to your shoulder.  Clean the kettlebell to your shoulders by extending through the legs and hips as you raise the kettlebell towards your shoulder. The wrist should rotate as you do so. This will be your starting position.

Begin my leaning to the side opposite the kettlebell, continuing until you are able to touch the ground with your free hand, keeping your eyes on the kettlebell.  As you do so, press the weight vertically be extending through the elbow, keeping your arm perpendicular to the ground.
Return to an upright position, with the kettlebell above your head. Return the kettlebell to the shoulder and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Balance Board
instructions: Note: This exercise is designed to increase balance.
Place a balance board in front of you.
Stand up on it and try to balance yourself.
Hold the balance for as long as desired.
Caution: If your balance is poor, start out with one of the less challenging boards.
Variations: You can perform this exercise using various types of balance boards.  Some are more challenging than others.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Renegade Rows
instructions: Place two kettlebells on the floor about shoulder width apart. Adopt an 'all-fours' position but instead of having your hands on the floor, take hold of the kettlebells with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip. At this point you should extend your legs out behind you so that your body is suspended above the floor in a straight line with your toes and the kettlebells being the only points of contact you have with the floor. The kettlebells should be directly below your chest so that your arms are perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Whilst exhaling, row one of the kettlebells towards the side of your torso by squeezing the muscles in your back. Continue to pull your elbow as high as you can until your body wants to rotate. Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Continue in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Single Arm Rows or Alternating Dumbbell Renegade Rows.

Continue in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance (standing). Bring the kettlebells up to the side of your shoulders so your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle, your palms should be facing forward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press one of the kettlebells towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight). Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Continue in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Shoulder Press or Alternating Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Floor Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and lay on your back. Have your arms extended towards the ceiling directly above your chest (the kettlebells should be resting on the back of your forearms at this point). Finally have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle whilst keeping your feet on the floor. This will be your starting position.
To Initiate the movement, inhale and descend one of the kettlebells down towards the floor. Allow your elbow to flare out wide until it gently touches the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the kettlebell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat this movement in an alternating fashion between your left and right side until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Floor Press or Chest Press.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Hang Clean
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebells with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs) and stand upright with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor in front of your body. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring one of the kettlebells up to shoulder height whilst relaxing your grip to allow the kettlebell to swing up and over to the back of your wrist. To do this, you will need to pull your elbow up toward the ceiling as fast as you can.

Immediately after doing this, take shallow breaths and return the kettlebell back along the same path back to its starting position. Whilst you are returning the first kettlebell back to its original position, pull your other kettlebell up to your shoulder. Once again, pull your elbow upwards as fast as you can and relax your grip to allow the kettlebell to flip over to the reverse side of your wrist.
Continue to alternate as you go and repeat this movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cleans or Dumbbell Cleans.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Bent Over Rows
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand (palms facing your torso), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The weights should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift one of the kettlebells to your side (as you exhale), elevating your elbow towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the weights). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the weight to the starting position as you inhale.
Once you have completed this movement, repeat for the opposite side.
Continue in this manner until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Dumbbell Bent Over Rows or Barbell Bent Over Rows.

name: Weighted Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a weight plate and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue to roll your shoulders towards your knees until your abdominals are fully contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your torso back along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rope Crunches or Crunches.

en: abdominals

Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a weight plate and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue to roll your shoulders towards your knees until your abdominals are fully contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your torso back along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rope Crunches or Crunches.

name: V Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down on your back. Have your arms extended above your head so they are just above the floor. Have your legs extended too, again just above the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, exhale and simultaneously elevate your legs up towards your torso and lift your torso and arms up towards your legs. At the top of the movement your abdominals should be fully contracted and you should be reaching for your toes.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your legs and torso along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be careful of your lower back throughout this movement.
Variations: Cocoons or Jackknife Sit-Ups.

en: abdominals

Grab a mat and lay down on your back. Have your arms extended above your head so they are just above the floor. Have your legs extended too, again just above the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, exhale and simultaneously elevate your legs up towards your torso and lift your torso and arms up towards your legs. At the top of the movement your abdominals should be fully contracted and you should be reaching for your toes.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your legs and torso along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be careful of your lower back throughout this movement.
Variations: Cocoons or Jackknife Sit-Ups.

name: Tuck Crunches
instructions: To begin, lie down on the floor or an exercise mat with your back pressed against the floor.  Your arms should be lying across your sides with the palms facing down.

Your legs should be crossed by wrapping one ankle around the other.  Slowly elevate your legs up in the air until your thighs are perpendicular to the floor with a slight bend at the knees.  Note: Your knees and toes should be parallel to the floor as opposed to the thighs.
Move your arms from the floor and cross them so they are resting on your chest.  This is the starting position.
While keeping your lower back pressed against the floor, slowly lift your torso.  Remember to exhale while perform this part of the exercise.
Slowly begin to lower your torso back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Variations: This exercise can be performed with a weighted plate resting across your chest with your arms holding it.  This can be a bit challenging for those who have never performed this exercise with weights. Always try the exercise without weights before moving to weighted plates.
You can also perform this exercise with your hands clasped behind your head.  This is a great way to add variety to your routine.

en: abdominals
To begin, lie down on the floor or an exercise mat with your back pressed against the floor.  Your arms should be lying across your sides with the palms facing down.

Your legs should be crossed by wrapping one ankle around the other.  Slowly elevate your legs up in the air until your thighs are perpendicular to the floor with a slight bend at the knees.  Note: Your knees and toes should be parallel to the floor as opposed to the thighs.
Move your arms from the floor and cross them so they are resting on your chest.  This is the starting position.
While keeping your lower back pressed against the floor, slowly lift your torso.  Remember to exhale while perform this part of the exercise.
Slowly begin to lower your torso back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Variations: This exercise can be performed with a weighted plate resting across your chest with your arms holding it.  This can be a bit challenging for those who have never performed this exercise with weights. Always try the exercise without weights before moving to weighted plates.
You can also perform this exercise with your hands clasped behind your head.  This is a great way to add variety to your routine.

name: Toe Touches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms above your head with your legs straight, pointing directly towards the ceiling (you can cross your legs if you wish). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst reaching towards your toes. Continue with this movement until your fingers touch your toes or your abdominal muscles are as tight as possible.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Rope Crunches.

en: abdominals

name: Sit Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

en: abdominals

name: Side Bridges
instructions: Grab a mat and place it on the floor, lay on your side with your shoulders elevated from the floor by leaning on your forearm. Have your other arm placed gently across your chest for stability (you can also place it on the floor in front of you if youy need further support). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and lift your pelvis directly upward towards the ceiling. Your body should now be in one straight line from your head to toes. The two points of contact with the floor should now be your bottom foot and your forearm (you can also perform this movement from your knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Plank With A Twist.

name: Flat Bench Leg Pull In
instructions: Grab a bench and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the bench by the side of your head. Your knees should be directly above your pelvis with both your hips and knees bent at a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and extend your knee and hip joints so that your legs are just above parallel to the floor. Continue to extend until your legs are out straight, making sure your lower back is not under any stress or strain.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Cable Crunches.

en: abdominals

name: Scissor Kicks
instructions: Grab a mat and lay flat on your back, your arms should run by the side of your torso with your forearms and hands placed just so, under your hips and glutes to support your lower back. At this point you should inhale and lift your feet around 6 inches off of the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, simultaneously take shallow breaths whilst kicking your feet up and down between a vertical position and a few inches from the floor (as if you were treading water). When you do this, use the whole of your legs rather than just from your knees down. Therefore the movement should be coming from your hips. Tip: If you need to adjust your hands, drop your feet to the floor and start the movement again in order to preserve your lower back.
Continue in the fashion until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back issues, this may not be the best exercise to perform.
Variations: Hip Raises or Flutter Kicks.

name: Reverse Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your hands gently placed on your temples with your knees and hips bent at a 90 degree angle. Your thighs should be vertical from the floor and lower legs parallel to the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your knees in towards your torso by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue with this movement until your knees are as close to your shoulders as they can be.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Flat Bench Leg Pull-In or Cocoons.

en: abdominals

name: Push Up to Side Plank
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and lean forward so that you are now weight baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whist flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.

Next, Whilst still exhaling, lift one of your hands off the floor whilst roating your torso. You should continue to rotate until your chest is perpendicular to the floor (facing the wall rather than the floor) creating a side on 'T' position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your torso back along the same path into the starting position.
You can either complete all repetitions on one side first or alternate as you go.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Plank With Twist or Plank.

name: V Sit With Plate Rotation
instructions: Grab a weight plate and take a seat on the floor. Hold the plate above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the plate is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back pain, this may not be the most suitable exercise.
Variations: Cable Russian Twists or V-Sit Twist With Med Ball.

name: Plank
instructions: Firstly, place a mat on the floor underneath your torso as you adopt an 'all-fours' position above it. From here you will need to bend your elbows to a 90 degree angle and place your upper body weight onto your forearms. After you have done that, take a step backwards with your feet and place your remaining bodyweight through your toes. At this point your body should be suspended (in a straight line) above the floor with just your forearms and toes as points of contact. This will be your starting position.

Keep your abdominal muscles tight throughout this exercise and keep your pelvis in line with the rest of your body to minimise risk of injury to your lower back (you can elevate your pelvis slightly to reduce lower back pain).
Hold this position for as long as you can or for the recommended amount of time.

name: Otis Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a weight plate and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst pressing the weight plate directly towards the ceiling at the same time. Continue to sit up as you push the weight as high towards the ceiling as possible.
Once your arms are vertical and your abdominal muscles are as tight as they can be, pause briefly, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rope Crunches or Cocoons.

name: Oblique Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst rotating your torso at the same time, aiming one shoulder to the opposite knee.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path to the original position.
Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cross Body Crunches or Rope Oblique Crunches.

name: Leg Pull Ins
instructions: Grab a flat bench, take a seat on it and grip the bench with your hands behind your back. At this point you should bend at your knees and hips in order to tuck your knees in as close to your chest as possible. Lean your torso forward into your knees to create optimal contraction within your abdominal muscles. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and simultaneously extend your knee and hip joints so that your legs are just above parallel to the floor whilst leaning your torso backwards to lengthen your abdominals. Your arms should be taking as little weight as possible so that your core is working at it's maximum, this is at your discretion.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your torso and legs back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

en: abdominals

At this point you should bend at your knees and hips in order to tuck your knees in as close to your chest as possible. Lean your torso forward into your knees to create optimal contraction within your abdominal muscles. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and simultaneously extend your knee and hip joints so that your legs are just above parallel to the floor whilst leaning your torso backwards to lengthen your abdominals. Your arms should be taking as little weight as possible so that your core is working at it's maximum, this is at your discretion.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your torso and legs back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

name: Janda Sit-Up
instructions: Note: This exercise was created by Czech exercise physiologist, Dr. Vladimir Janda and is one of the most challenging sit-up variations as it completely isolates the rectus abdominals by eliminating the hip flexors.

Position your body on the floor in the basic sit-up position; knees to a ninety degree angle with feet flat on the floor and arms either crossed over your chest or to the sides.  This will be your starting position.

As you strongly tighten your glutes and hamstrings, fill your lungs with air and in a slow (three to six second count) ascent, slowly exhale.  Tip: It is important to tighten the glutes and hamstrings as this will cause the hip flexors to be inactivated in a process called reciprocal inhibition, which basically means that opposite muscles to the contracted ones will relax.
As you inhale, slowly go back in a controlled manner to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Variations: The exercise can be done using a dedicated abdominal bench apparatus, having a workout partner exert pulling pressure on your calves (while you maintain your feet on the ground) or wrapping your legs over barbell and pulling back.

name: Hanging Pike
instructions: Grab a pull-up bar with a shoulder width, neutral grip (palms facing each other). As you have both arms extended above your head, maintain a straight back as you lift your feet to allow your body to hang from the bar. Bend at your knees in front of your body so they and your hips are at 90 degrees. This is your starting position. Tip: For those of you that have a stronger core, try straightening your legs and maintain that extension throughout.

To initiate the movement, exhale and activate your abdominals to bring your knees up towards your shoulders in a semi-circular motion as your hips tilt underneath your body. Continue to bring your legs up towards the ceiling until they between your hands. Be sure to keep your arms at full extension throughout the movement.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your legs and torso along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Cable Reverse Crunches.

en: abdominals

Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Cable Reverse Crunches.

name: Hanging Leg Raises
instructions: Take hold of a chin up bar/handles and allow your body to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and elevate your legs in front of your body by contracting your abdominal muscles (either keeping them as straight as possible, or bending at the knees) until they create a 90 degree angle with your torso (if you have bent your knees, you should have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position. Tip: This movement should be slow and controlled so that you do not swing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hip Raises or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

en: abdominals

To initiate the movement, exhale and elevate your legs in front of your body by contracting your abdominal muscles (either keeping them as straight as possible, or bending at the knees) until they create a 90 degree angle with your torso (if you have bent your knees, you should have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position. Tip: This movement should be slow and controlled so that you do not swing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hip Raises or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

name: Gorilla Pull Ups
instructions: Grab a pull-up bar with a slightly wider than shoulder width underhand grip (palms facing your torso). As you have both arms extended above your head, maintain a straight back as you lift your feet to allow your body to hang from the bar. Bend at your knees (90 degrees) so that your ankles are behind your body. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your torso up towards the ceiling through the muscles in your back and biceps. As you are ascending, activate your abdominals to bring your knees up towards your shoulders as your hips tilt underneath your body. Continue to ascend in this fashion until your chest is as close to your hands as possible and your knees close to your chest.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your body along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Hanging Pikes.

name: Frog Sit Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. Next, place the soles of your shoes together and allow your knees to relax out wide. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your feet by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your abdominals are as tight as possible.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sit-Ups or Otis-Ups.

name: Flat Bench Lying Leg Raises
instructions: Grab a bench and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the bench by the side of your head. Elevate your legs (at full extension) vertically towards the ceiling until you create a 90 degree bend at your hips. Tip: Keep your legs extended throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and start to descend your legs on an arcing motion towards the floor. Continue to descend until your legs are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Reverse Crunches.

en: abdominals

name: Decline Oblique Crunches
instructions: Find yourself a decline bench (around 30 degrees) and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and secure your legs so you don't slip down the bench (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the bench towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Twist your torso as you go so that your right elbow is aiming at your left knee. Continue in this fashion until you cannot contract your abdominals or obliques any more.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Alternate sides as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Kneeling Cable Crunches With Alternating Oblique Twists or Oblique Crunches.

name: Decline Crunches
instructions: Find yourself a decline bench (around 30 degrees) and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and secure your legs so you don't slip down the bench (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the bench towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

en: abdominals

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the bench towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms directly above your chest towards the ceiling and maintain their position throughout. For your lower body, create a 90 degree bend at both your hips and knee joints so your lower legs are parallel with the floor, feet in mid air. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and extend one of your legs out and lower towards the floor as you go. Continue until your heel is an inch off the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your leg back along the same path into the starting position.
Alternate as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Reverse Crunches.

en: abdominals

After a brief pause, exhale and return your leg back along the same path into the starting position.
Alternate as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Reverse Crunches.

name: Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your abdominals cannot be any tighter.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Cable Crunches.

en: abdominals

name: Crunches Hands Overhead
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend and place your arms on the floor above your head and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your abdominals cannot be any tighter. Keep your arms at full extension behind your head adding resistance to your crunches.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Rope Crunches.

name: Cross Body Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, you need to simultaneously, bring your right knee up and in towards your body whilst crunching your left shoulder over towards it (exhale as you do this).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path to the original position.
Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side. So exhale and bring your left knee up and in towards your torso whilst crunching your right shoulder over towards it.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Ab Bike.

en: abdominals

Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Ab Bike.

name: Cocoons
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms above your head with your legs laying flat on the floor. Your body should be at full extension. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and simultaneously pull your knees (bending them as you go) in towards your torso and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles and bringing your arms towards your toes. Continue with this movement until your knees are as close to your shoulders as they can be.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Flat Bench Leg Pull-In or Jacknives.

en: abdominals
Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms above your head with your legs laying flat on the floor. Your body should be at full extension. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and simultaneously pull your knees (bending them as you go) in towards your torso and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles and bringing your arms towards your toes. Continue with this movement until your knees are as close to your shoulders as they can be.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Flat Bench Leg Pull-In or Jacknives.

name: Bottoms Up
instructions: Grab a mat and lay flat on your back, your arms should be by your side with your legs extended. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring your knees in towards your chest by bending at both the knees and hips. Once they reach your chest you should extend your legs towards the ceiling so that they lift your hips and glutes up and off the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs along the same path back to the starting position.
Variation: Reverse Crunches or Cocoons.

en: abdominals
Grab a mat and lay flat on your back, your arms should be by your side with your legs extended. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring your knees in towards your chest by bending at both the knees and hips. Once they reach your chest you should extend your legs towards the ceiling so that they lift your hips and glutes up and off the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs along the same path back to the starting position.
Variation: Reverse Crunches or Cocoons.

name: Alternating Heel Touches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your chest and lift your legs so that you have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees. Crunch your abs so that you lift your shoulders up from the mat and you are ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and reach around the side of your body with your right hand towards your right heel, your legs should remain stationary. Tip: To acheive a peak contraction, crunch upward slightly as you go.
After a brief pause inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Now repeat the movement for your left hand side. Exhale and reach around your body with your left arm towards your left heel. Inhale and return to the starting position.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Weighted Side Bends.

en: abdominals

To initiate the movement, exhale and reach around the side of your body with your right hand towards your right heel, your legs should remain stationary. Tip: To acheive a peak contraction, crunch upward slightly as you go.
After a brief pause inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.

name: Air Bike
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples and lift your legs so that you have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees. Crunch your abs so that you lift your shoulders up from the mat and you are ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, you need to simultaneously, bring your right knee up and in towards your body whilst crunching your left shoulder over towards it (exhale as you do this). As you do this extend your left leg to create space for your left elbow.

Gently inhale and return to the original position. Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side. So exhale and bring your left knee up and in towards your torso whilst crunching your right shoulder over towards it, extending your right leg as you go.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunches .

en: abdominals

To initiate the movement, you need to simultaneously, bring your right knee up and in towards your body whilst crunching your left shoulder over towards it (exhale as you do this). As you do this extend your left leg to create space for your left elbow.

Gently inhale and return to the original position. Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side. So exhale and bring your left knee up and in towards your torso whilst crunching your right shoulder over towards it, extending your right leg as you go.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunches .

name: Ab Wheels Rollout
instructions: Grab an ab-wheel and place it on the floor at the end of a mat. Kneel down on the mat and adopt an overhand grip on the wheel. With a bend at your knees and hips, lean your bodyweight forward so that your (straight) arms are holding you upright. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and slowly roll the wheel forward, stretching your arms out as far as you can whilst dropping your pelvis down at the same time.
After a brief pause, exhale and use your abdominal muscles to pull the wheel back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any lower back pain or hernias, this exercise is not suitable.
Variation: Barbell Roll Out.

name: 3:4 Sit Up
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is 3/4 of the way towards being vertical to the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Sit-Ups.

en: abdominals

Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

name: Banded Upright Rows
instructions: To begin, stand on an exercise band so that tension begins at arm's length. Grasp the handles using a pronated (palms facing your thighs) grip that is slightly less than shoulder width. The handles should be resting on top of your thighs. Your arms should be extended with a slight bend at the elbows and your back should be straight. This will be your starting position.Use your side shoulders to lift the handles as you exhale. The handles should be close to the body as you move them up. Continue to lift the handles until they nearly touches your chin. Tip: Your elbows should drive the motion. As you lift the handles, your elbows should always be higher than your forearms. Also, keep your torso stationary and pause for a second at the top of the movement.Lower the handles back down slowly to the starting position. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.Variations: This exercise can also be performed using a straight or e-z bar. Another variation is to use dumbbells, though this later exercise should be reserved by the most advanced people that are well familiarized with correct execution.

en: traps
shoulders

name: Speed Band Pushdown
instructions: Choke a band onto a pull up bar.
Grasp the band with a pronated grip, hands close together.
Beginning with the elbow flexed, extend the elbow to straighten your arms, ensuring you keep your upper arm in place.
Pause briefly, and return to the starting position.

en: triceps
Choke a band onto a pull up bar.
Grasp the band with a pronated grip, hands close together.
Beginning with the elbow flexed, extend the elbow to straighten your arms, ensuring you keep your upper arm in place.
Pause briefly, and return to the starting position.

name: Dynaband Overhead Tricep Extensions
instructions: For this exercise anchor a band to the ground. We used an incline bench and anchored the band to the base, standing over the bench. Alternatively, this could be performed standing on the band.
To begin, pull the band behind your head, holding it with a pronated grip and your elbows up. This will be your starting position.
To perform the movement, extend through the elbow to to straighten your arms, ensuring that you keep your upper arm in place.
Pause, and then return to the starting position.

name: Single-Leg High Box Squat
instructions: Position a box in a rack. Secure a band or rope in place above the box.
Standing in front of it, step onto the box to a full standing position, letting your other leg remain unsupported. Hold onto the band for balance
. Continue stepping up and down on the same leg before switching to the opposite side.

name: Dynaband Shoulder Press
instructions: To begin, stand on an exercise band so that tension begins at arm's length.  Grasp the handles and lift them so that the hands are at shoulder height at each side.
Rotate the wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward.  Your elbows should be bent, with the upper arms and forearms in line to the torso.  This is your starting position.
As you exhale, lift the handles up until your arms are fully extended overhead.

name: Seated Banded Hamstring Curls
instructions: Secure a band close to the ground and place a bench a couple feet away from it.
Seat yourself on the bench and secure the band behind your ankles, beginning with your legs straight. This will be your starting position.
Flex the knees, bringing your feet towards the bench. You may need to lean back slightly to keep your feet from striking the floor.
Pause at the completion of the movement, and then slowly return to the starting position.

en: hamstrings

name: Reverse Band Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Begin with a bar loaded on the floor inside of a power rack. Attach bands to the top of the rack, using either pegs or the frame itself. Attach the other end to the barbell.

Approach the bar so that the bar intersects the middle of the feet. The feet should be set very wide, near the collars. Bend at the hips to grip the bar. The arms should be directly below the shoulders, inside the legs, and you can use a pronated grip, a mixed grip, or hook grip. Relax the shoulders, which in effect lengthens your arms.

Take a breath, and then lower your hips, looking forward with your head with your chest up. Drive through the floor, spreading your feet apart, with your weight on the back half of your feet. Extend through the hips and knees.
As the bar passes through the knees, lean back and drive the hips into the bar, pulling your shoulder blades together.
Return the weight to the ground by bending at the hips and controlling the weight on the way down.

name: Reverse Band Squat
instructions: Begin in a power rack with the pins and bar set at the appropriate height. After loading the bar, attach bands to the top of the rack, using either pegs or the frame itself. Attach the other end of the bands to the bar.

Begin by stepping under the bar and placing it across the back of the shoulders. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and rotate your elbows forward, attempting to bend the bar across your shoulders. Remove the bar from the rack, creating a tight arch in your lower back, and step back into position. Place your feet wide for more emphasis on the back, glutes, adductors, and hamstrings.

Keep your head facing forward. With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit back with your hips as much as possible. Ideally, your shins should be perpendicular to the ground. Lower bar position necessitates a greater torso lean to keep the bar over the heels. Continue until you break parallel, which is defined as the crease of the hip being in line with the top of the knee.

Keeping the weight on your heels and pushing your feet and knees out, drive upward as you lead the movement with your head. Continue upward, maintaining tightness head to toe, until you have returned to the starting position.

Begin in a power rack with the pins and bar set at the appropriate height. After loading the bar, attach bands to the top of the rack, using either pegs or the frame itself. Attach the other end of the bands to the bar.

Begin by stepping under the bar and placing it across the back of the shoulders. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and rotate your elbows forward, attempting to bend the bar across your shoulders. Remove the bar from the rack, creating a tight arch in your lower back, and step back into position. Place your feet wide for more emphasis on the back, glutes, adductors, and hamstrings.

Keep your head facing forward. With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit back with your hips as much as possible. Ideally, your shins should be perpendicular to the ground. Lower bar position necessitates a greater torso lean to keep the bar over the heels. Continue until you break parallel, which is defined as the crease of the hip being in line with the top of the knee.

Keeping the weight on your heels and pushing your feet and knees out, drive upward as you lead the movement with your head. Continue upward, maintaining tightness head to toe, until you have returned to the starting position.

name: Reverse Band Deadlift
instructions: Set the bar up in a power rack. Attach bands to the top of the rack, using either bands pegs or the frame itself. Attach the other end of the bands to the bar.

Approach the bar so that it is centered over your feet. You feet should be about hip width apart. Bend at the hip to grip the bar at shoulder width, allowing your shoulder blades to protract. Typically, you would use an overhand grip or an over/under grip on heavier sets.

With your feet, and your grip set, take a big breath and then lower your hips and bend the knees until your shins contact the bar. Look forward with your head, keep your chest up and your back arched, and begin driving through the heels to move the weight upward.
After the bar passes the knees, aggressively pull the bar back, pulling your shoulder blades together as you drive your hips forward into the bar.
Lower the bar by bending at the hips and guiding it to the floor.

name: Reverse Band Box Squat
instructions: Begin in a power rack with a box at the appropriate height behind you.  Set up the bands either on band pegs or attached to the top of the rack, ensuring they will be directly above the bar during the squat. Attach the other end to the bar.

Begin by stepping under the bar and placing it across the back of the shoulders. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and rotate your elbows forward, attempting to bend the bar across your shoulders. Remove the bar from the rack, creating a tight arch in your lower back, and step back into position. Place your feet wider for more emphasis on the back, glutes, adductors, and hamstrings, or closer together for more quad development. Keep your head facing forward.

With your back, shoulders, and core tight, push your knees and butt out and you begin your descent. Sit back with your hips until you are seated on the box. Ideally, your shins should be perpendicular to the ground. Pause when you reach the box, and relax the hip flexors. Never bounce off of a box.

Keeping the weight on your heels and pushing your feet and knees out, drive upward off of the box as you lead the movement with your head. Continue upward, maintaining tightness head to toe. Use care to return the barbell to the rack.

name: Reverse Band Bench Press
instructions: Position a bench inside a power rack, with the bar set to the correct height. Begin by anchoring bands either to band pegs or to the top of the rack. Ensure that you will be position properly under the bands. Attach the other end to the barbell.

Lie on the bench,  tuck your feet underneath you and arch your back. Using the bar to help support your weight, lift your shoulder off the bench and retract them, squeezing the shoulder blades together. Use your feet to drive your traps into the bench. Maintain this tight body position throughout the movement. However wide your grip, it should cover the ring on the bar.

Pull the bar out of the rack without protracting your shoulders. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart. Lower the bar to your lower chest or upper stomach. The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times.
Pause when the barbell touches your torso, and then drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.

Pull the bar out of the rack without protracting your shoulders. Focus on squeezing the bar and trying to pull it apart. Lower the bar to your lower chest or upper stomach. The bar, wrist, and elbow should stay in line at all times.
Pause when the barbell touches your torso, and then drive the bar up with as much force as possible. The elbows should be tucked in until lockout.

name: Rear Delt Flyes (Dynaband)
instructions: Grab a dynaband and step onto the middle of the it with a shoulder width stance (the width of your stance will increase/decrease the resistance). Cross the handles over and take hold of one in each hand. At this point the band should form an 'X' shape in front of you. Once you have done this, lean forward from your waist until your torso is parallel with the floor and have a slight bend at your knees. Let your arms hang infront of you, perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Whilst exhaling and maintaining a stationary torso, lift the handles out to your side, taking them as wide as you can. Continue to lift them until your arms are parallel to the floor, squeeze your shoulder blades together to acheive this.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rear Delt Cable Flyes or Face Pulls.

name: Banded Lat Raises
instructions: Grab a dynaband and take a handle in each hand (neutral grip, palms facing each other). Step onto the middle of the band and adopt a shoulder width stance (the width of your stance will increase/decrease the resistance). Once you have done this, make sure you are in an upright position with a slight bend at your knees and elbows. Let your arms hang infront of you, perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling and maintaining a stationary torso, lift the handles out to your side, taking them as wide as you can. Continue to lift them until your arms are parallel to the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Lateral Raises or Seated Lateral Raises.

en: shoulders

name: Dynaband Internal Rotations
instructions: Secure a band to a rigid structure around belly button height. Stand side on to the structure and take a step away. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position and take hold of the band with the hand nearest the structure. Once you have hold of the band, tuck your elbow in close to your torso (your elbow should be at a 90 degree angle). This will be your starting position.

Exhale and rotate your hand on an imaginary semicricle in front of you, pivoting from your elbow which, remains tight against the side of your torso. Rotate your shoulder fully so that your forearm is as close to a right angle across your body as possible.
After a brief pause, return the band along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Caution: Be cautious with the weight as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variations: Dumbbell Internal Rotations or Cable Internal Rotations.

en: shoulders

name: Banded Hip Flexions
instructions: Securely attach a band around a rigid structure at around ankle height, with the other end of the band around your ankle. Whilst facing away from the structure, take one step forwards (you can hold on to a support for balance if you need to). Remember to maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

To inititate the movement, exhale and pull your knee up towards your chest. This promotes a semicircular movement with your ankle, infront of your body. Continue to lift your knee upward until you cannot lift it any higher.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the ankle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap legs.

en: quadriceps

name: Banded Hip Extensions
instructions: Securely attach a band around a rigid structure at around ankle height, with the other end of the band around your ankle. Whilst facing the structure, take one step backwards and hold on for support. Tip: You should lean forwards from your hips and maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping a gentle bend at your knee and hip, exhale and squeeze the glute of the leg you have the band attached to. This promotes a semicircular movement with your ankle, behind your body.
Once you have achieved a peak contraction through your glute, pause for a brief moment, inhale and return the ankle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap legs.
Variation: Cable Hip Extensions

name: Banded Glute Bridges
instructions: Securely attach a dynaband around two rigid structures very close to, if not on the floor. Once you have done this, lay down (face up) so that your hips are under the band. Have your torso flat on the floor with your arms by your sides. Your feet should be planted on the floor with your knees bent at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees). This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling by squeezing your glutes. As you do so, lean back onto your shoulders and keep driving upward until your glutes are fully contracted.

After a brief pause, inhale and gently relax your glutes to drop your pelvis towards the floor (the resistance should be placed through your heels). Continue to drop your pelvis until your glutes touch the floor.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Glute Bridge or Barbell Hip Thrust.

After a brief pause, return the band along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Caution: Be cautious with the weight as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variations: Dumbbell External Rotations or Cable External Rotations.

name: Dynaband Deadlifts
instructions: Grab a band (with the correct resistance) and place it flat on the floor. Stand on the band with a shoulder width stance, taking hold the ends with an overhand grip (palms facing behind you).

Whilst maintaining good posture (a straight back), bend at your knees and hips (until your hips are just above knee height) which will lower your torso. This will be the starting position of the exercise.

Initiate the movement by pressing through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously using the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. Maintain strict form (straight back) and exhale. Once you are in a standing position you should push your chest out by pulling your shoulder blades together.
After a brief pause, descend the band back along the same path and inhale until you are back in the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be very careful when performing this exercise and if you suffer from any back pain, this may not be suitable movement for you.
Variations: Hex Bar Deadlifts or Barbell Deadlifts.

name: Banded Calf Raises
instructions: Grab a band (with the correct resistance) and place it flat on the floor. Stand on the band with a shoulder width stance, taking hold of the ends with an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). Stand up straight and pull as hard as you wish on the band. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and raise your heels as high as possible by pushing off of the balls of your feet, flexing your calves throughout the contraction. Your knees should remain extended. Hold the contracted position for a second before you start to go back down.
Inhale and return slowly to the starting position as you lower your heels towards the floor.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Calf Raises or Smith Machine Calf Raises.

en: calves

name: Banded Box Squats
instructions: Place a box/bench just behind where you will be performing this movement. Now, attach either end of a band to a barbell. Place the barbell across the back of your shoulders and have a partner pull the middle of the band down so that you can stand on it.

Adopt a shoulder width stance on the band with your toes slightly pointing outward just in front of the box/bench. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your glutes gently touch the box.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a partner. If you do not have a partner, be cautious. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Barbell Box Squats.

name: Banded Monster Walks
instructions: Place a band around both ankles and another around both knees. There should be enough tension that they are tight when your feet are shoulder width apart.
To begin, take short steps forward alternating your left and right foot.
After several steps, do just the opposite and walk backward to where you started.

en: abductors

name: Banded Skull Crushers
instructions: Grab a band (with the correct resistance) and secure it around the end of a bench. Lay face up on that bench with your head located the same end as the band. Reach behind you to take hold of the handles and extend them upwards towards the ceiling. Continue until your arms are at full extension and your palms are facing away from you. Ensure that your elbows are tucked in and remain as close together throughout the movement as possible. This is your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the handles towards the back of your head. Be sure to keep your elbows tucked in and stretch your triceps as you go. When you get to within an inch of your forehead, pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the band along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cable Skull Crushers or EZ-Bar Skullcrushers.

en: triceps

name: Band Pull Aparts
instructions: Grab a band and take hold of it with both hands (adjust the width of your hands for more or less resistance). Keeping your arms straight (slight bend at your elbows), lift the band up to shoulder height in front of you.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your arms back out wide beside you, stretching the band as you go. Your body should now be in a 'T' shape with the band gently touching your chest. Tip: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the movement and try not to rock or sway in order to stretch the band.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the band back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Banded Hip Adductions
instructions: Grab a dynaband and securely attach it to a rigid structure around ankle height. Stand side on to the structure you have attached the band to and place the other end around the ankle closest to the structure.
Stand upright (you can take hold of a rigid frame for balance if needed) and lift the leg off the floor the band is attached to. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring the working leg across your body and towards the outside of your standing leg. Keep your leg straight (knees slightly bent) and maintain a straight back throughout.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the ankle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Adduction Machine.

Grab a dynaband and securely attach it to a rigid structure around ankle height. Stand side on to the structure you have attached the band to and place the other end around the ankle closest to the structure.
Stand upright (you can take hold of a rigid frame for balance if needed) and lift the leg off the floor the band is attached to. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring the working leg across your body and towards the outside of your standing leg. Keep your leg straight (knees slightly bent) and maintain a straight back throughout.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the ankle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Adduction Machine.

name: Banded Good Mornings
instructions: Using a 41 inch band, stand on one end, spreading your feet a small amount. Bend at the hips to loop the end of the band behind your neck. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your legs straight, extend through the hips to come to a near vertical position.
Ensure that you do not round your back as you go down back to the starting position.

lower back

name: Dynaband Flyes
instructions: Attach a dynaband around a rigid structure (around shoulder height). Take a handle in each hand and face away from where you have tied the band. Press the handles in front of you until your arms are (nearly) straight. Adopt a shoulder width standing stance (you can split your stance if you wish to gain extra stability), whilst maintaining a straight back troughout the movement. Your palms should be facing each other. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, bring your arms back on a wide arc until your arms are out wide to your sides. Have a slight bend at your elbows and allow the resistance to stretch your pectoral muscles.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Flye Machine.

en: chest

Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Flye Machine.

name: Band Assisted Pull Ups
instructions: Tie a band around the middle of the Pull-Up bar and secure tightly! Once you have done this place one foot within the band and adopt an overhand, wide grip. Keep your arms extended and your back straight, in fact, from your head to your feet should be one straight line. This will be your starting position.To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your body upward by contracting your lats (back muscles) and bending your elbows. The best way to do this is to pull your elbows towards the floor. Continue to pull yourself upwards until your head becomes in line with your hands. Tip: Avoid swinging, twisting or jerking motions as you could cause yourself an injury.After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back into the starting position.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.Variations: Horizontal Barbell Pull Up or Chin Ups.

name: Band Assisted Step Ups
instructions: Place a dynaband around a rigid structure (around shoulder height) and place a step infront of it. The height of the step should be realistic to the ability of the user. Take hold of the dynaband so that it is at shoulder height and stretched enough to assist when your perform the movement. Your elbows should be flared out to the side and bent at a 90 degree angle. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement so that you minimise the risk of an injury. Now place your left foot on top of the step, your knee should roughly be at a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.
As you exhale, step up onto the step by pushing through your left heel and thigh. As you do so, your hip and knee joints should straighten out. Lift your right leg onto the step at this point.
Inhale and return back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Step Ups or Barbell Step Ups.

hamstrings

Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Step Ups or Barbell Step Ups.

name: Dynaband Rear Delt Flyes
instructions: Run a band around a stationary post like that of a squat rack.
Grab the band by the handles and stand back so that the tension in the band rises.

Extend and lift the arms straight in front of you. Tip: Your arms should be straight and parallel to the floor while perpendicular to your torso.  Your feet should be firmly planted on the floor spread at shoulder width.  This will be your starting position.
As you exhale, move your arms to the sides and back.  Keep your arms extended and parallel to the floor. Continue the movement until the arms are extended to your sides.
After a pause, go back to the original position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations:  You can perform this exercise on a rear delt machine as well or using a pulley machine.

name: Hyper-Extension Over An Exercise Ball
instructions: Grab a Swiss Ball and lay over the top of it (face down) with your hips supported. Have your feet shoulder width and placed against a wall or fixed in place by a support. Relax your torso over the ball with your hands placed gently on your temples. This is the starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and slowly pull your shoulders back and engage the muscles in your back (specifically, your lower back) until you cannot contract any more.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your torso along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be VERY careful if you suffer from lower back pain. This may not be the best suited exercise if so.
Variation: Skydivers.

name: Exercise Ball Torso Rotation
instructions: Grab an exercise ball and adopt a shoulder width standing stance. Hold the ball out in front of you at full arms reach, at shoulder height. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, twist your torso to your left as far as you possibly can (whilst keeping your arms out in front of you and at shoulder height).
Pause for a brief movement, inhale and use your core muscles to return the exercise ball back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Cable Russian Twists or V-Sit Twists (Medicine Ball).

name: Stability Ball Abdominal Twist
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is 2/3 of the way up its runner and set the weight accordingly. Next, attach a 'V' bar to the cable and place a swiss ball adjacent to the runner (around 2 feet away) and lay on top of it. To get into position, take a seat on top of it and walk your body down the ball until your shoulders and neck are supported by it, whilst your feet are shoulder width apart and supporting the rest of your bodyweight. Your knees should be at a 90 degree angle, hips pushed up towards the ceiling through your glutes and your back should be straight. The handle should now be in line with your chest, fully extend your arms and take hold of it. Pull the handle slightly so it is now above your chest, your arms aiming vertically towards the ceiling. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and rotate through your core to turn your shoulders away from the cable machine. Keep your arms extended as you rotate and continue until your arms become parallel to the floor. The only point of contact with the swiss ball should be through your shoulder.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Russian Twists or Hip Crossovers.

en: abdominals

name: Press Ups Feet On An Exercise Ball
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor with an exercise ball close by, and lean forward so that you are now weight baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Next you will need to place your feet on top of the exercise ball so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and the exercise ball. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms vertical from the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whist flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out. Remember to keep your body straight throughout.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Decline Push-ups or Machine Bench Press.

name: Oblique Crunches On An Exercise Ball
instructions: Sit upright on an exercise ball with a straight back and a 90 degree bend at your hips and knees. Once in this position, slowly and in a controlled manner roll onto your right hip. At this point your legs will straighten (slight bend at your knees) and they will become crossed for extra stability. Have your torso relaxed over the top of the ball and place your left hand gently onto your temple. Your right arm can either come across your body onto the left side of your rib cage or placed on the exercise ball for greater stability. This is the starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and contract the left side of your torso, bringing your left elbow as close to your hip as possible.
Once you cannot contract your torso anymore, pause for a brief moment, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Oblique Crunches or Cable Oblique Crunches.

name: Hug A Ball
instructions: Seat yourself on the floor.
Straddle an exercise ball between both legs and lower your hips  down toward the floor.

Hug your arms around the ball to support your body. Adjust your legs so that your feet are flat on the floor and your knees line up over your ankles. Keep a good grip on the ball so it doesn't roll away from you and send you back onto your buttocks.

name: Jackknife
instructions: To get into position for this exercise you will need to place your hands shoulder width apart on the floor (as if you were in the starting position for a press up) and place your shins on top of a swiss ball. Keep your abdominals tight throughout this movement in order to keep alignment from your shoulders to your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement you will be pulling the swiss ball in towards your torso by lifting your hips slightly to allow your knees to be driven in towards your chest. Your shins will be replaced on the swiss ball by your feet and toes. Tip: This entire movement is directed by your abdominal muscles with your lower back playing no part at all. If you find it is hurting, either lift your pelvis or use an alternative exercise.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your knees back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cocoons or Cable Crunches.
