name: Crunches On An Exercise Ball
instructions: Sit on a swiss ball with an upright posture and a 90 degree bend at your hips and knees. Slowly walk your feet forward allowing your body to move down the ball until your lower back is supported. Maintain the 90 degree bend at your knees and allow your torso to be laying flat on top of the ball. There should be a straight line between your knees and your head, your lower back being supported by the swiss ball. Have your feet about shoulder width apart and your hands gently placed on your temples. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and contract your abdominal muscles in order to lift your shoulders up and toward your knees. Your lower back should remain in contact with the swiss ball throughout the movement. Continue to curl your shoulders towards your knees until your abdominals cannot contract anymore.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your shoulders along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Crunches or Cocoons.

en: abdominals

name: Crunches Legs On Exercise Ball
instructions: Lay down on your back with your lower legs resting on top of a swiss ball, creating a 90 degree bend at your hips and knees. Have your hands on your temples with your elbows out wide. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and contract your abdominal muscles in order to lift your shoulders up and off the floor toward your knees. Your lower back should remain in contact with the floor throughout the movement. Continue to curl your shoulders towards your knees until your abdominals cannot contract anymore.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your shoulders along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cocoons.

name: Hamstring Curls On Exercise Ball
instructions: Lay on your back on a mat with your feet propped up on top of a swiss ball. Your arms should be laying flat alongside your torso. Have your heels so that they are in the centre of the swiss ball so that you can apply pressure directly down through the ball and into the floor. Squeeze your glutes to lift your pelvis and elevate your body off the floor, your body should now be in one straight line. Your arms and shoulders should be taking the weight as well as your hamstrings from your heel position. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your heels in towards your hips. Bend at your knees to do so but keep your pelvis pushed forward so that you still have a straight line from your shoulders to knees.
Pause for a brief moment, inhale and return your ankles along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Lying Leg Curls or Seated Leg Curls.

name: Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
instructions: Set the weight to the recommended amount and check that the leg brace is set to the correct height. Take a seat facing the machine (tuck your hips in so they are touching the leg brace) and take a wide, overhand grip with your arms extended towards the ceiling. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the bar down towards your chest using the muscles across your back and your biceps. Lean back ever so slightly as your pull the weight downwards and lift your chest as much as you can. Continue to pull the bar down until either your muscles are optimally contracted or it is in contact with your chest.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar back along the same path into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Pull-Ups or Cable Pullovers.

name: Incline Cable Flyes
instructions: To get yourself into the starting position, set the pulleys at the floor level (lowest level possible on the machine that is below your torso).
Place an incline bench (set at 45 degrees) in between the pulleys, select a weight on each one and grab a pulley on each hand.
With a handle on each hand, lie on the incline bench and bring your hands together at arms length in front of your face.  This will be your starting position.

With a slight bend of your elbows (in order to prevent stress at the biceps tendon), lower your arms out at both sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch on your chest.  Breathe in as you perform this portion of the movement.  Tip: Keep in mind that throughout the movement, the arms should remain stationary.  The movement should only occur at the shoulder joint.

Return your arms back to the starting position as you squeeze your chest muscles and exhale.  Hold the contracted position for a second.  Tip: Make sure to use the same arc of motion used to lower the weights.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variation: You can vary the angle of the bench in order to target the upper chest at slightly different angles.

name: Incline Cable Chest Press
instructions: Adjust the weight to an appropriate amount and be seated, grasping the handles. Your upper arms should be about 45 degrees to the body, with your head and chest up. The elbows should be bent to about 90 degrees. This will be your starting position.
Begin by extending through the elbow, pressing the handles together straight in front of you. Keep your shoulder blades retracted as you execute the movement.
After pausing at full extension, return to the starting position, keeping tension on the cables.

name: Cable Upright Rows
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low cable pulley and adopt a standing, shoulder width close to the pulley. Face the machine and take hold of the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs). Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor creating a straight line from the cable to your shoulders. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the bar upwards towards your chin. Flare your elbows out, high and wide and you do so, this will activate your shoulder muscles. Tip: Keep your elbows above your hands and maintain a straight back throughout this movement. Do not rock your bodyweight forward and backwards in order to generate momentum.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Upright Row Barbell or Smith Machine Upright Row.

name: Cable Tricep Push Down V Bar Attachment
instructions: Attach a V-bar to a high cable pulley. Adopt a shoulder width, upright stance (about 18 inches away from the machine) and take hold of the bar with an overhand grip (palm facing the floor).

Once you have hold of the bar, take the weight by tucking your elbow into the side of your torso. Your forearms and upper arms should create an acute angle (less than 90 degrees) when ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and push the bar down towards your thighs on an arcing motion. Use your triceps to do this and contract fully when your arms are by your thighs. At this point, your arms should be perpendicular to the floor. Tip: Keep your elbows tucked in to your sides as only the forearms should move.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Overhead Tricep Extensions.

name: Standing Cable Crunches
instructions: Adopt a shoulder width stance underneath a high cable with a rope attached. Face away from the machine but be as close to it as possible, keep your legs straight throughout (slight bend at the knees). When setting the weight for this exercise, be cautious as if it is too heavy, your abs will not be taking the load and your lower back will play a part.
Take hold of the ropes and bring them down so they are next to your head on your upper chest. Push your hips back and lean forward from your hips so that your torso is taking the weight.

From here you should try to arch your back by taking a deep breath, pulling your shoulders back and pushing your chest out. This will be your starting position. Tip: Throughout this movement your hips and your lower limbs should remain stationary.

Exhale and flex your abs to move your shoulders (and rope) downward towards your thighs. As you flex your abs, the arch in your back reverses so that your back is now rounding over. Your elbows should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return along the same path to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back pain, be cautious of the resistance.
Variations: Kneeling Cable Crunches or Cocoons.

name: Single Arm Standing Cable Curls
instructions: Attach a handle to a low cable pulley and take hold of it with an underhand grip (palm facing away from you). Stand upright around 12 inches away from the cable, maintaining a straight back throughout.

Whilst holding the upper arm stationary, exhale as you curl the handle up toward your shoulder by contracting your bicep. Continue the movement until your bicep is fully contracted and the handle is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your bicep.
(Tip: Only the forearm should move.)
Inhale as you slowly begin to return your arm along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Concentration Curls or Barbell Bicep Curls.

name: Single Arm Low Cable Overhead Tricep Extensions
instructions: Attach a handle to a low cable pulley, take hold of it and stand with an upright, shoulder width stance facing away from the machine, arm straight above your head vertical from the floor. Keep your elbow tucked in close to your ear and have your palm facing away from you. Allow your other arm to relax across your torso so it does not influence the movement. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbow pinned in close to your ear. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the handle down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbow is at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment, exhale and return the handle along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Tricep Extension (Rope) or Standing Overhead Barbell Triceps Extension.

name: Single Arm Cable Lat Raises Behind The Back
instructions: Place a handle attachment onto a low cable pulley. Stand side on to the machine with your right side being closest. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position, maintaining a straight back throughout. Reach across and behind your body and take hold of the handle with your left hand. Return to your original, standing position. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating your left hand on a wide arc out to your side. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arm is parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Dumbbell Lateral Raises or Cable Lat Raises.

name: Cable Woodchops
instructions: Attach a handle to a high pulley cable and adopt wide stance next to the machine around 12 inches away. Reach across your body and take hold of the handle in both hands. Both your arms should be straight (slight bend at the elbow) and this is how they must remain throughout. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and rotate your core so that your hands move down and across your body (in a straight diagonal line) aiming at the hip furthest from the machine. Keep your back and arms straight as you do so. Try to minimise the rotation of your hips so that your core is taking the load.
Once you have turned as far as your torso will allow, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arms back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Reverse Cable Woodchops.

name: Cable Crossovers
instructions: Attach handles to two high cables. Take one in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles down to your shoulders and press them forward in front of you until your arms are (nearly) straight. Adopt a standing, split stance, maintaining a straight back throughout the movement. Your palms should be facing each other. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, bring your arms back on a wide arc until your arms are out wide to sides. Have a slight bend at your elbows and allow the weight to stretch your pectoral muscles.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Flye Machine

name: Cable Curls
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low cable pulley and take hold of it with an underhand grip (palms facing upwards). Stand upright around 12 inches away from the cable, maintaining a straight back throughout.

Whilst holding the upper arm stationary, exhale as you curl the bar up towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your biceps.
(Tip: Only the forearms should move.)
Inhale as you slowly begin to return the bar along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Hammer Curls or Barbell Bicep Curls.

name: Single Arm Cable Crossover
instructions: Attach two handles to high cable pulleys and adopt a shoulder width standing standing between them. Take a cable in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles down to your waist until your arms are (nearly) straight and vertical from the floor. Your palms should be facing each other. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back through out the movement.

Whilst inhaling, bring one of your arms up and out (to your side) on a wide arcing motion until your arm is parallel with the floor. Have a slight bend at your elbow and allow the weight to stretch your pectoral muscles.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Alternate as you go and repeat until you complete for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Pec Deck or Press Ups.

name: Cable Shotgun Rows
instructions: Attach a handle to a low cable pulley and adjust the weight accordingly. Take hold of the handle with a neutral grip and take a big step backwards. Split your stance, bend your front knee and lean forwards from your hips (maintaining a straight back however) allowing the muscles across your upper back and shoulder to stretch. Your arm should be at full extension at this point. This is your starting position.

To initiate this movement, exhale and pull your elbow backwards in to the side of your torso whilst retracting your shoulder joint and contracting the muscles across your mid-upper back. Continue to squeeze these muscles and draw the handle in closer to your body until they are optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle back along the same path to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Dumbbell Row or Seated One-Arm Cable Pulley Rows.

name: Seated Two-Arm Palms-Up Low-Pulley Wrist Curl
instructions: Put a bench in front of a low pulley machine that has a barbell or EZ Curl attachment on it.
Move the bench far enough away so that when you bring the handle to the top of your thighs tension is created on the cable due to the weight stack being moved up.
Now hold the handle with both hands, palms up, using a shoulder-width grip.
Step back and sit on the bench with your feet about shoulder width apart, firmly on the floor.
Lean forward and place the forearms on your thighs with the back of your wrists over your knees.  This will be your starting position.
Lower the bar as far as possible, while inhaling and keeping a tight grip.
Now curl the bar up as high as possible while contracting the forearms. Tip: Only the wrist should move; not the forearms.
After a second contraction at the top go back to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: This exercise can be performed also with a single handle attachment (one arm at a time) or with free weights (barbells or dumbbells) using one arm and two arm variations.

name: Seated Single Arm Cable Rows
instructions: Attach a handle to a low cable pulley, take hold of it with a nuetral grip and take a seat about 2 feet away from the pulley system. Once seated, you should have your working arm and legs out infront of you (slightly bent) with your posture held in an upright position (this is paramount to maintain throughout the movement) with your chest pushed out. This is your starting position.

Exhale and pull the handle in towards your lower abdominals by contracting your back muscles. Your posture should remain upright throughout the movement and your arm should remain as passive as possible.
Once the handle touches your abdominals, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning your arm along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back pain, this may not be a suitable movement for you.
Variations: Seated Cable Rows or Landmine Single arm Rows.

name: Seated Cable Shoulder Press
instructions: Attach handles to two low opposing cables and place an incline bench (around 70-80 degrees) between them facing forward. Take a seat and take hold a handle in each hand. Bring the handles up to shoulder height with your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle. Your palms should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press both handles towards the ceiling until your elbows become fully extended (straight).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Seated Shoulder Press.

name: Rear Delt Cable Fly
instructions: Remove the handles from two parallel cables. Adjust the height of the pulleys so they are above your head and adopt a standing, shoulder width stance between them.

Once you have done this take hold of the left cable with your right hand and the right cable with your left hand. Your arms should be at full extension with a slight bend at your elbows. This will be your starting position.

Now exhale and pull the cables back (crossing the cables over and keeping them around shoulder height) on a wide arcing motion. Tip: You should be leaning backward to help lift the weights nor should your back come out of a straight position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rear Delt Flyes or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine).

name: Cable Pull Through
instructions: Begin standing a few feet in front of a low pulley with a rope or handle attached. Face away from the machine, straddling the cable, with your feet set wide apart.

Begin the movement by reaching through your legs as far as possible, bending at the hips. Keep your knees slightly bent. Keeping your arms straight, extend through the hip to stand straight up. Avoid pulling upward through the shoulders; all of the motion should originate through the hips.

name: Pallof Press With Rotation
instructions: Attach a handle to a cable pulley that is at chest height and adopt a shoulder width stance adjacent to the cable machine (on your right side) about a foot away.
Take hold of the handle with an both hands, bend your elbows and keep your hands tucked in to your chest at this point. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain good posture throughout.
Exhale and press your hands out in front of your torso, keeping them parallel to the floor as you go. Continue to press forwards until your arms are at full extension.

Once at full extension, you then need to twist your torso (and the cable) away from the machine as far as you can without moving your hips. Your arms should remain parallel to the floor throughout and your head following your shoulders.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle back along the same path to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Cable Russian Twists or Plate Twists.

name: Overhead Cable Curl
instructions: To begin, set a weight that is comfortable on each side of the pulley machine.  Note: Make sure that the amount of weight selected is the same on each side.
Now adjust the height of the pulleys on each side and make sure that they are positioned at a height higher than that of your shoulders.

Stand in the middle of both sides and use an underhand grip (palms facing towards the ceiling) to grab each handle.  Your arms should be fully extended and parallel to the floor with your feet positioned shoulder width apart from each other.  Your body should be evenly aligned the handles.  This is the starting position.
While exhaling, slowly squeeze the biceps on each side until your forearms and biceps touch.
While inhaling, move your forearms back to the starting position.  Note: Your entire body is stationary during this exercise except for the forearms.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions prescribed in your program.
Variations: This exercise can also be performed using one handle at a time.

name: Cable Kickbacks
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to both a low cable pulley and your ankle. Whilst facing the machine, take one step backwards and hold onto the metal structure for support. Lift your ankle so that you have a 90 degree bend at your hip. Tip: You should lean forwards from your hips and maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and kick backwards whilst maintaining a stationary torso. Squeeze the glute of the leg you have the cuff attached to and keep going until you have a peak contraction within the glute.
Once you have achieved a peak contraction, pause for a brief moment, inhale and return the ankle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap legs.
Variation: Hip Thrusts.

name: Single Arm High Pulley Cable Side Bends
instructions: Attach a handle to a high cable pulley and adopt a narrow stance adjacent to the cable machine (on your right side) about a foot away.

Take hold of the handle with an underhand grip, bend your elbow as you take hold and tuck it in to the side of your torso. Allow your left hand to rest across your torso so it does not interfere with the movement. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and crunch your torso laterally (sideways) towards your right side, gently pulling the weight down as you go. Tip: Only bend laterally, not forward or back.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path to its starting position.
Variations: Plate Side Bends or V-Sit Twists (Medicine Ball).

name: Cable Skull Crushers
instructions: Place a flat bench in front of a single low cable pulley (around 12 inches away) with the straight bar attachment connected. Lay down on your back with your head the same end as the cable and have your torso flat with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle and your feet firmly placed on the floor.

Grab the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and extend your arms above your chest so they are vertical from the floor. Tip: From now on in your elbows should remain as close as possible and your upper arm should be stationary throughout. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your elbows creating an arcing motion with the bar so that your hands are heading towards your forehead. The weight should be stretching your triceps. Remember to keep your elbows as close together as possible.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar and your hands along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Rope Extensions or Skull Crushers.

name: Lying High Cable Curls
instructions: Place a flat bench in front of a high pulley or lat pulldown machine.
Hold the straight bar attachment using an underhand grip (palms up) that is about shoulder width.
Lie on your back with your head over the end of the bench.
Now extend your arms straight above your shoulders. Your torso and your arms should make a 90-degree angle and the elbows should be in.  This will be your starting position.

As you breathe out, curl the bar down in a semicircular motion until it touches your chin. Squeeze the biceps for a second at the top contracted position. Tip: As you execute this motion only the forearms should move.  At no time should the upper arms be moving at all.  They are to remain perpendicular throughout the movement.
Return to starting position slowly.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can also curl down to above your head, rather than to your chin or use an E-Z Bar Attachment instead of a straight one.

name: Lying Cable Curls
instructions: Grab a straight bar or E-Z bar attachment that is attached to the low pulley with both hands, using an underhand (palms facing up) shoulder-width grip.
Lie flat on your back on top of an exercise mat in front of the weight stack with your feet flat against the frame of the pulley machine and your legs straight.
With your arms extended and your elbows close to your body slightly bend your arms.  This will be your starting position.
While keeping your upper arms stationary and the elbows close to your body, curl the bar up slowly toward your chest as you breathe out and you squeeze the biceps.
After a second squeeze at the top of the movement, slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can also perform this exercise with exercise bands.

name: Low Cable Row To Neck
instructions: Attach a rope to a low cable pulley and adopt a standing, shoulder width stance a couple of feet away from the pulley. Face the machine and take hold of the rope with an overhand grip (little fingers on the rubber ends of the attachment). From the cable pulley to your shoulders, should be a straight line in what will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the rope in towards your chin. Seperate the rope as much as possible by pulling your little fingers apart and flaring your elbows out, high and wide. Tip: Keep your hands above your elbows and maintain a straight back throughout this movement. Do not rock your bodyweight forward and backwards in order to generate momentum.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the rope along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Rear Delt Rows Rope or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine).

name: Laying Cable Tricep Extensions
instructions: Place a mat in front of a single low cable pulley (around 18 inches away) with the rope attachment connected. Lay down on your back with your head the same end as the cable and have your torso flat with your knees bent at a confortable angle, this will support your lower back.

Grab the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and extend above your chest so your arms are vertical from the floor. Tip: From now on in your elbows should remain as close as possible and your upper arm should be stationary throughout. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your elbows creating an arcing motion with the rope so that your hands are heading towards your forehead. The weight should be stretching your triceps. Remember to keep your elbows as close together as possible.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the rope and your hands along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Rope Extensions or Skull Crushers.

name: Low Cable Crossovers
instructions: Attach handles to two opposing low cable pulleys. Take a standing, shoulder width stance between the cables facing forward. Take hold of both handles with an underhand grip (palms facing forward) and take a small step forward (around 18 inches). Allow your arms to hang by your sides, torso straight with your chest out. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, exhale and lift the handles on an arcing motion in front of your body until the handles meet in the middle and cross over at shoulder height. Make sure that your elbows are slightly bent throughout and that your arms remain as passive as possible, the movement should be directed through your pectoral muscles (chest).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles along the same path back in to the starting positions.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Pec Deck or Flat Dumbbell Flyes.

name: Kneeling Single Arm High Cable Rows
instructions: Attach a handle to a high cable pulley. Adopt a kneeling position (about 18 inches away from the machine) and take hold of the handle with an overhand grip (palm facing the floor). Once you have hold of the handle, your arm should be at full extension aiming at the high cable pulley (creating a straight line from your shoulder to the pulley). Have a straight back and a 90 degree angle at your knees. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the handle down towards the side of your torso. Use your back muscles in order to activate the movement and keep your arm as passive as possible. Continue to pull the handle until your elbow is either beside or just past your torso (without twisting your body). Tip: Twist your hand as you pull the handle down and in order to get the best contraction.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Single Arm Row or Single Arm Lat Pulldown.

name.en: Kneeling Single Arm High Cable Rows

name: Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunches
instructions: Adopt a kneeling position underneath a high cable with a rope attached. When setting the weight for this exercise, be cautious as if it is too heavy, your abs will not be taking the load and your lower back will play a part.
Take hold of the ropes and bring them down so they are beside your head on your upper chest. Push your hips back and lean forward to create a 90 degree angle from your lower back and thighs.

From here you should try to arch your back by taking a deep breath, pulling your shoulders back and pushing your chest out. This will be your starting position. Tip: Throughout this movement your hips and your lower limbs should remain stationary.

Exhale and flex your abs and obliques to move your shoulders (and rope) downward towards your right thigh. As you flex your abs and obliques, the arch in your back reverses so that your back is now rounding over. Your elbows should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return along the same path to your starting position.
Repeat the movement but this time as you crunch downward, aim towards your left thigh. Keep alternating in this fashion until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back pain, be cautious of the resistance.
Variations: Oblique Crunches or Cross Body Crunches.

name: Kneeling High Cable Rows Rope
instructions: Attach a rope to a high cable pulley and adopt a kneeling stance facing the cable machine about a 2 feet away.

Take hold of the rope with an overhand grip and keep your arms pointing towards the cable pulley itself. Lean back slightly from your knees allowing you to keep a straight back and looking in the same direction your arms are pointing. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the rope in towards your face. Separate the rope as much as possible by pulling your little fingers apart and flaring your elbows out, high and wide. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and do not rock your bodyweight forward and backwards in order to generate momentum.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the rope along the same path to the starting position.
Variations: Lat Pulldown or T-Bar Rows With Handle.

name: Kneeling Cable Tricep Extensions
instructions: Attach a bar to a high cable pulley and adopt a kneeling stance facing away from the machine about a foot away.

Grasping the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing forward), lean forward with your bodyweight so that you create a slight bend at your hips. Your knees should be at 90 degrees located under the bar. Your elbows should also be at 90 degrees, tucked in close to your ears where they should stay throughout the movement. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout, only your forearms should move. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and press the bar forwards on an arcing path whilst squeezing your triceps. Continue with this movement until your arms are fully extended.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bench Dips or Tricep Extension (Rope).

name: High Cable Curls
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so it is at the top of its runner and set the weight accordingly. Attach a flat bar to the cable. Next take hold of the bar with an underhand grip (palms facing you) and stand away from the cable machine and pull the bar so that there is tension in the cable, elbows close together, above your chest. This will be the starting position of the exercise.To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the bar towards your forehead in an arcing motion. Tip: Only your forearms should move, your upper arms should remain stable. Continue to curl the bar until either your biceps are optimally contracted or you reach your forehead.After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.Variations: Cable Curls or Preacher Curls.

name: Cable Front Raises
instructions: Place a handle attachment on to a low cable pulley. Facing away, stand about a foot away from the machine. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position, maintaining a straight back throughout. Take hold of the handle in your left hand and return to your original position with your hand hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating your left arm on an arcing motion in front of you. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arm is parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Front Plate Raise or Alternating Front Raises.

en: shoulders

Place a handle attachment on to a low cable pulley. Facing away, stand about a foot away from the machine. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position, maintaining a straight back throughout. Take hold of the handle in your left hand and return to your original position with your hand hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating your left arm on an arcing motion in front of you. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arm is parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variation: Front Plate Raise or Alternating Front Raises.

name: Flat Cable Flyes
instructions: Set two opposing cable pulleys to the bottom of the machine and attach a handle to each. Position a bench in between the two cables and lay on the bench with your chest between the cables. Take hold of the handles and press the weights directly up towards the ceiling until your arms are fully extended (slight bend at your elbows) and the handles are above your chest. This will be your starting position.
Maintaining the slight bend at your elbows, lower your arms in a wide arcing motion until you feel your chest stretching, inhale as you do so.
Following the same path as you went down, squeeze your pectoral muscles (chest) and bring your arms back to the starting position, remembering to exhale as you go.
Pause for a second and then repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Pec Deck.

en: chest

Set two opposing cable pulleys to the bottom of the machine and attach a handle to each. Position a bench in between the two cables and lay on the bench with your chest between the cables. Take hold of the handles and press the weights directly up towards the ceiling until your arms are fully extended (slight bend at your elbows) and the handles are above your chest. This will be your starting position.
Maintaining the slight bend at your elbows, lower your arms in a wide arcing motion until you feel your chest stretching, inhale as you do so.
Following the same path as you went down, squeeze your pectoral muscles (chest) and bring your arms back to the starting position, remembering to exhale as you go.
Pause for a second and then repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Pec Deck.

name: Cable External Rotation
instructions: Attach a handle to the cable and adjust the height of the pulley so that it sits at belly button height. Stand side on to the machine and take a step away. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position and take hold of the handle with the hand furthest from the machine. Once you have hold of the handle, tuck your elbow in close to your torso (your elbow should be at a 90 degree angle). This will be your starting position.

Exhale and rotate your hand on an imaginary semicricle in front of you, pivoting from your elbow which, remains tight against the side of your torso. Rotate your shoulder fully so that your hand is as close to a right angle (pointing away from your body) as possible.
After a brief pause, return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Caution: Be cautious with the weight as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variations: Dumbbell External Rotations or Dynaband External Rotations.

en: shoulders

After a brief pause, return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Caution: Be cautious with the weight as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variations: Dumbbell External Rotations or Dynaband External Rotations.

name: Elevated Cable Rows
instructions: Get a platform of some sort (it can be an aerobics or calf raise platform) that is around 4-6 inches in height.
Place it on the seat of the cable row machine.
Sit down on the machine and place your feet on the front platform or crossbar provided making sure that your knees are slightly bent and not locked.
Lean over as you keep the natural alignment of your back and grab the V-bar handles.

With your arms extended pull back until your torso is at a 90-degree angle from your legs.  Your back should be slightly arched and your chest should be sticking out. You should be feeling a nice stretch on your lats as you hold the bar in front of you.  This is the starting position of the exercise.

Keeping the torso stationary, pull the handles back towards your torso while keeping the arms close to it until you touch the abdominals.  Breathe out as you perform that movement.  At that point you should be squeezing your back muscles hard.  Hold that contraction for a second and slowly go back to the original position while breathing in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Avoid swinging your torso back and forth as you can cause lower back injury by doing so.
Variations: You can use a straight bar instead of a V-Bar and perform with a pronated grip (palms facing down-forward) or a supinated grip (palms facing up-reverse grip).

name: Cable Wrist Curl
instructions: Start out by placing a flat bench in front of a low pulley cable that has a straight bar attachment.

Use your arms to grab the cable bar with a narrow to shoulder width supinated grip (palms up) and bring them up so that your forearms are resting against the top of your thighs.  Your wrists should be hanging just beyond your knees.
Start out by curling your wrist upwards and exhaling.  Keep the contraction for a second.
Slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Your forearms should be stationary as your wrist is the only movement needed to perform this exercise.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations:

This exercise can also be performed sitting down by kneeling and using the bench as a resting position for your forearms.  Your wrist can hang over the bench and the same movements as mentioned above can be performed.
You can also use a dumbbell instead of a barbell.

name: Cable Tuck Reverse Crunch
instructions: Use a twin cable tower. Connect a rope attachment to one cable and an ankle attachment to the other. Both should be positioned at the lowest pulley setting.
Place a mat in between the cables and sit down; attach the ankle strap to your feet.
Lie back and grab the rope with both hands; position the rope behind your head with your hands by your ears.
Make sure you are positioned in the middle with your legs completely outstretched.
In one motion, pull the rope forward in a crunching movement and bring your knees inward to your torso.
As your elbows and knees come together, elevate your shoulders and hips off the floor.

Pause for a moment and in a slow and controlled manner reset to the starting position. You should hold your legs off the ground after the beginning repetition to keep tension on the cable in the resting position.
Repeat the same movement to failure.

Tip: For maximum challenge and effectiveness, lower your legs as close to the floor as possible without touching before going into the crunch. In the lowered position, your legs should also be completely straight. Don’t cheat yourself by bending your knees.

en: abdominals
Use a twin cable tower. Connect a rope attachment to one cable and an ankle attachment to the other. Both should be positioned at the lowest pulley setting.
Place a mat in between the cables and sit down; attach the ankle strap to your feet.
Lie back and grab the rope with both hands; position the rope behind your head with your hands by your ears.
Make sure you are positioned in the middle with your legs completely outstretched.
In one motion, pull the rope forward in a crunching movement and bring your knees inward to your torso.
As your elbows and knees come together, elevate your shoulders and hips off the floor.

Pause for a moment and in a slow and controlled manner reset to the starting position. You should hold your legs off the ground after the beginning repetition to keep tension on the cable in the resting position.
Repeat the same movement to failure.

Tip: For maximum challenge and effectiveness, lower your legs as close to the floor as possible without touching before going into the crunch. In the lowered position, your legs should also be completely straight. Don’t cheat yourself by bending your knees.

name: Cable Shrugs
instructions: Adjust two opposing cable pulleys so they are at the bottom of their runners and set the weights accordingly. Position yourself in between these runners with a shoulder width stance. Attach a handle to each cable and (whilst maintaining good posture (a straight back)), bend at your knees and hips to lower your torso, enabling you to take hold of each. Press through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously using the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. This will be the starting position of the exercise.
To initiate the movement, exhale and elevate your shoulders by pulling them in towards your ears. Continue to elevate until the muscles around your neck (trapezius) are optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Shrugs or Smith Machine Shrugs.

en: traps

After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Shrugs or Smith Machine Shrugs.

name: Standing Cable Shoulder Press
instructions: Attach handles to two low cables. Take one in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles up to shoulder height with your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance (you can split your stance for more stability), maintaining a straight back throughout the movement. Your palms should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press both handles towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight).
After a brief pause, return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Seated Shoulder Press.

en: shoulders
triceps

Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance (you can split your stance for more stability), maintaining a straight back throughout the movement. Your palms should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press both handles towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight).
After a brief pause, return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Seated Shoulder Press.

name: Seated Cable Rear Delt Flyes
instructions: Adjust two opposing cable pulleys so they are at the bottom of their runners and set the weights accordingly. Next, attach handles to each cable and place a flat bench length ways between the cables. Take a seat on the bench facing away from the machines with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle and your torso leaning forwards whilst maintaining a straight back. Next you will need to cross the cables over underneath the bench so your left hand takes hold of the right cable and vice versa. Adopt underhand grips and allow your elbows to flare out wide. This is your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the back of your hands out and away from one another on a wide arcing path by flexing the muscles in the rear of your shoulders or across your upper back.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handles back along the same path to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Lateral Raises or Seated Side Lateral Raises.

name: Seated Cable Crunch
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the top of its runner and set the weight accordingly. Next, attach a rope to the cable and place a flat bench adjacent to the runner (around a foot away). Take a seat on the bench facing away from the machine with your knees and hips at 90 degree angles and your torso upright. Reach up and grab the rope with both hands, pulling it down to sit either side of your head on your upper chest. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and flex your abs to move your shoulders (and rope) downward towards your thighs. As you flex your abs, the arch in your back reverses so that your back is now rounding over. Your elbows should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the rope back along the same path to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Crunch or Cocoons.

en: abdominals

name: Cable Russian Twists
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is 2/3 of the way up its runner and set the weight accordingly. Take a large step away from the cable and set your stance at shoulder width. Take hold of the cable in both hands and extend your arms in front of you. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and rotate through your core to turn your shoulders away from the cable machine. Keep your arms extended as you rotate and continue until your arms are pointing away from the cable machine.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Russian Twists or Hip Crossovers.

en: abdominals

To initiate the movement, exhale and rotate through your core to turn your shoulders away from the cable machine. Keep your arms extended as you rotate and continue until your arms are pointing away from the cable machine.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back into its starting position.

name: Seated Cable Rows
instructions: Attach two handles to a low cable pulley, take hold of them with a neutral grip and sit about 2 feet away from the pulley system. Once seated, you should have your arms and legs out in front of you (slightly bent) with your posture held in an upright position (this is paramount to maintain throughout the movement) with your chest pushed out. This is your starting position.

Exhale and pull the handles in towards your lower abdominals by contracting your back muscles. Your posture should remain upright throughout the movement and your arms should remain as passive as possible.
Once the handles touch your abdominals, pause for a brief moment before inhaling and returning them along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from back pain, this may not be a suitable movement for you.
Variations: Landmine T Bar Rows or Underhand Cable Pulldowns.

name: Cable Rear Delt Rows Rope
instructions: Attach a rope to a high cable pulley and adopt a standing, shoulder width stance a couple of feet away from the pulley. Face the machine and take hold of the rope with an overhand grip (little fingers on the rubber ends of the attachment).
Elevate your arms so that they are just above parallel with the floor. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the rope in towards your upper chest. Seperate the rope as much as possible by pulling your little fingers apart and flaring your elbows out high and wide. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and do not rock your bodyweight forward and backwards in order to generate momentum.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the rope along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Facepulls or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine)

name: Overhead Tricep Extensions Rope
instructions: Attach a rope to a low cable pulley, take hold of the rope and stand with an upright, shoulder width stance facing away from the machine, arms straight above your head with your little fingers on the rubber ends to the rope. Keep your elbows tucked in close to your ears and have your knuckles facing the ceiling. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout this movement and keep your elbows pinned in close to your ears. This will be your starting position.
Whilst your upper arms remain stationary, inhale and lower the rope down on an arcing motion behind your head until your elbows are at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees).
Pause for a brief moment then exhale and return the rope along the same path to the starting position whilst contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Skullcrushers or Bench Dips.

name: Cable Reverse Crunch
instructions: Attach two handles to a low cable pulley and place a mat in front of it. Lay down on your back and securely put the handles over your toes.

Lift your feet off the ground so that your knees and hips are both at 90 degrees and place your hands on your temples. Tip: Try not to pull your neck or head in to the movement with your hands. If your neck starts to ache, put one hand behind your head for support and to take some weight.
Now exhale and whilst crunching your up torso upward, bring your knees in towards your chest.
Once your abs are as tight as they can be pause for a brief moment, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cocoons or Jack-Knives.

en: abdominals

name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Remove the handles from two parallel cables. Adjust the height of the pulleys so they are above your head and adopt a standing, shoulder width stance between them.

Once you have done this take hold of the left cable with your right hand and the right cable with your left hand. Your arms should be at full extension with a slight bend at your elbows. This will be your starting position.

Now exhale and pull the cables back (crossing the cables over and keeping them around shoulder height) on a wide arcing motion. Tip: You should be leaning backward to help lift the weights nor should your back come out of a straight position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rear Delt Flyes or Rear Delt Flyes (Machine).

en: shoulders

name: Straight Arm Pulldowns Rope
instructions: Attach a bar to a high cable pulley, adopt a shoulder width, upright stance and take hold of the bar with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Take a step backwards (around 2 feet or so) and bend slightly forward from your waist whilst maintaining a straight back. Pull the weight down with straight arms so that they are now parallel with the floor. Tip: Keep a slight bend at your elbows throughout. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and pull the bar downwards towards your thighs whilst maintaining both your posture and straight arms. Contract your lats to make this movement happen. Continue to pull the bar downwards towards your thighs until the bar is just a few centimetres away from them.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Dumbbell Pull Overs or Bent Arm Barbell Pullover.

en: lats

name: Cable Preacher Curls
instructions: Place the preacher curl machine in front of a low cable pulley (with the bar attachment on) and take a seat. Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine and grab hold of the bar with your palms facing forward. Set the height of the seat so that your arm pits are snug on top of the pad and have your arms extended. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the bar up towards your shoulders. Keep your triceps on the pad and squeeze your biceps until they are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Curls or Barbell Preacher Curl.

en: biceps
forearms

Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine and grab hold of the bar with your palms facing forward. Set the height of the seat so that your arm pits are snug on top of the pad and have your arms extended. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the bar up towards your shoulders. Keep your triceps on the pad and squeeze your biceps until they are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

name: Single Arm Cable Tricep Extensions
instructions: Attach a handle to a high cable pulley. Adopt a shoulder width, upright stance (about 18 inches away from the machine) and take hold of the handle with an overhand grip (palm facing the floor).

Once you have hold of the handle, take the weight by tucking your elbow into the side of your torso. Your forearm and upper arm should create an acute angle (less than 90 degrees) when ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and push the handle down towards your side with an arcing motion. Use your tricep to do this and contract fully when your arm is by your side. At this point, your arm should be perpendicular to the floor. Tip: Keep your elbow tucked in to your side as only the forearm should move.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extensions.

name: Cable Skull Crushers Rope Attachment
instructions: Place a bar attachment onto a low cable pulley and place a flat bench facing away from it. Lay down on the bench with your head at the cable end. Adopt an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and press the bar straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your elbows as close together as you can to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.
Inhale as you descend the bar towards your forehead. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forehead pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extenions.

en: triceps

Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Kickbacks or Tricep Rope Extenions.

name: Cable Lateral Raises
instructions: Place a handle attachment onto a low cable pulley. Stand side on to the machine with your right side being closest. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position, maintaining a straight back throughout. Reach across your body and take hold of the handle with your left hand. Return to your original, standing position. This will be your starting position.

Initiate the movement by exhaling and elevating your left hand on a wide arc out to your side. Keep a slight bend at your elbow and continue to elevate upward until your arm is parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Dumbbell Lateral Raises or Dynaband Lateral Raises.

en: shoulders
forearms

After a brief pause, inhale and return your arm along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Dumbbell Lateral Raises or Dynaband Lateral Raises.

name: Cable Judo Flip
instructions: Connect a rope attachment to a tower, and move the cable to the lowest pulley position. Stand with your side to the cable with a wide stance, and grab the rope with both hands.
Twist your body away from the pulley as you bring the rope over your shoulder like you’re performing a judo flip.
Shift your weight between your feet as you twist and crunch forward, pulling the cable downward.
Return to the starting position and repeat until failure.
Then, reposition and repeat the same series of movements on the opposite side.

Tip: Although this should be one continuous movement, think of it as two separate ab movements: the twist for obliques and the crunch for abs. This will help with the mind-muscle connection. Also, over exaggerate the end of the crunch by pausing for a moment and clinching your abs in an isometric hold before releasing.

en: abdominals

Shift your weight between your feet as you twist and crunch forward, pulling the cable downward.
Return to the starting position and repeat until failure.

name: Cable Iron Cross
instructions: Place handle attachments on two high parallel cable pulleys. Once you have done this, stand inbetween the cables (facing forward) and grab one in a each hand. Have a shoulder width, upright stance with your back straight and elbows slightly bent. Your head should be up and your chest pushed out. Your position should now resemble a 'T'. This will be your starting position.

Keeping your arms out wide, exhale and bring your hands down towards the middle of your hips on two wide arcing paths. Tip: Your pectoral muscles should be initiating this movement but only your arms should be moving.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands back along the same paths towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Flyes or Pec Deck.

en: chest

name: Cable Internal Rotation
instructions: Attach a handle to the cable and adjust the height of the pulley so that it sits at belly button height. Stand side on to the machine and take a step away. Adopt a shoulder width, upright standing position and take hold of the handle with the hand nearest the machine. Once you have hold of the handle, tuck your elbow in close to your torso (your elbow should be at a 90 degree angle). This will be your starting position.

Exhale and rotate your hand on an imaginary semicricle in front of you, pivoting from your elbow which, remains tight against the side of your torso. Rotate your shoulder fully so that your hand is close to the opposite side of your body.
After a brief pause, return the handle along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Caution: Be cautious with the weight as you could damage your rotator cuff.
Variations: Dumbbell Internal Rotations or Dynaband Internal Rotations

en: shoulders

name: Incline Cable Tricep Extensions V Bar Attachment
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the top of its runner and set the weight accordingly. Place an incline bench in front of the cable but facing away from the machine. Next, attach a 'V' bar to the cable and lay on the bench (chest facing the ceiling) that is set to an incline angle of around 30-45 degrees. Extend your arms vertically towards the ceiling and take an overhand grip of the 'V' bar. Your palms should now be facing forwards. Bend your elbows so that they are tucked in and are at a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and extend your arms out and away from your body on a wide arcing motion. Continue to press the weight away from your body until your arms are fully extended and your triceps are fully contracted. Tip: Keep your elbows as close together as possible throughout the movement.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Kickbacks or Tricep Extension (Rope).

name.en: Incline Cable Tricep Extensions V Bar Attachment

name: Cable Incline Pushdown
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the top of its runner and set the weight accordingly. Place an incline bench in front of the cable but facing away from the machine. Next, attach a 'V' bar to the cable and lay on the bench (chest facing the ceiling) that is set to an incline angle of around 30 degrees. Extend your arms vertically towards the ceiling and take an overhand grip of the 'V' bar. Your palms should now be facing forwards. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your arms down towards your thighs (whilst keeping them extended throughout) on a wide arcing motion. Continue to pull the weight down towards your thighs until your arms are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Pullover or Bent-Arm Barbell Pullover.

name: Cable Hip Adduction
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low pulley. Stand side on to the pulley system and attach the cuff to the ankle nearest the cable.

Once you have done this take a large step away from the cable pulley. Now have your weight on the leg that the cuff is not attached to (the furthest foot away from the machine). If you need to hold onto something for stability, you can do so.
Allow your ankle (which the cable is attached to) to be pulled towards the machine. This will be your starting position.
Exhale and move the weight loaded ankle across your body (away from the machine) and in front of your standing foot using your inner thigh muscles.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the ankle along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap ankles.
Variation: Adductor Machine or Leg Press (Wide Stance).

name: Cable Hammer Curls Rope
instructions: Attach a rope to a low cable pulley. Take hold of the ropes with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) making sure that the plastic/rubber part on the end is touching your thumbs and index fingers. Stand upright around 12 inches away from the cable, maintaining a straight back throughout.

Whilst holding the upper arm stationary, exhale as you curl the rope up towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the the rope is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your biceps.
(Tip: Only the forearms should move.)
Inhale as you slowly begin to return the rope along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Hammer Curls or Seated Hammer Curls.

en: biceps

name: Cable Deadlifts
instructions: Adjust two opposing cable pulleys so they are at the bottom of their runners and set the weights accordingly. Position yourself in between these runners with a shoulder width stance. Attach a bar to each cable and (whilst maintaining good posture (a straight back)), bend at your knees and hips to lower your torso, enabling you to take hold of the bar. This will be the starting position of the exercise.

To initiate the movement, exhale and press through your heels and quads whilst simultaneously using the muscles in your back to bring your torso into a vertical position. Once you are in a standing position you should push your chest out by pulling your shoulder blades together.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the bar along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hex Bar Deadlifts or Barbell Deadlifts.

name: Cable Crunches
instructions: Adopt a kneeling position underneath a high cable with a rope attached. When setting the weight for this exercise, be cautious as if it is too heavy, your abs will not be taking the load and your lower back will play a part.
Take hold of the ropes and bring them down so they are beside your head on your upper chest. Push your hips back and lean forward to create a 90 degree angle from your lower back and thighs.

From here you should try to arch your back by taking a deep breath, pulling your shoulders back and pushing your chest out. This will be your starting position. Tip: Throughout this movement your hips and your lower limbs should remain stationary.

Exhale and flex your abs to move your shoulders (and rope) downward towards your thighs. As you flex your abs, the arch in your back reverses so that your back is now rounding over. Your elbows should remain as close to your torso as possible throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return along the same path to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back pain, be cautious of the resistance.
Variations: Crunches or Cocoons.

en: abdominals

name: Cable Chest Press
instructions: Attach handles to two cables (placed at shoulder height). Take one in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles up to shoulder height with your elbows parallel with the floor, flared out wide at a 90 degree angle. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance, maintaining a straight back troughout the movement. Your palms should be facing the floor. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press the handles in front of you until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight) and bring your hands together.
After a brief pause and squeeze of your pectoral muscles, return the handles back along the same path to the starting position.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Chest Press or Chest Press Machine

name: Bosu Ball Cable Crunch With Side Bends
instructions: Connect a standard handle to each arm of a cable machine, and position them in the most downward position.
Grab a Bosu Ball and position it in front and center of the cable machine.
Lie down on the Bosu Ball with the small of your back arched around the ball. Your rear end should be close to the floor without touching it.
With both hands, reach back and grab the handle of each cable.
With your feet positioned in a wide stance, extend your arms straight out in front of you and in between your knees. Your hands should be at knee level.
Keep your arms straight and in-line with the upward angle of the cable. Elevate your torso in a crunching motion without dropping or bending your arms.
Maintain the rigid position with your arms. Slowly descend back to the starting position with your back arched around the Bosu Ball and your abdominals elongated.
Repeat the same series of movements to failure.
Once you reach failure, keep your abs tight and raise your torso into plank position so your back is elevated off the Bosu Ball.
Lower your arms down to your side; keep them straight. Start doing alternating side bends; reach for your heels! This finishing movement will focus on your obliques.

Tip: Arch your back around the Bosu Ball before each rep to fully elongate your abdominals. This will provide a full range of motion and lengthen your muscle fibers, giving them a much fuller appearance.

name: Bent Over Single Arm Low Cable Rear Delt Flyes
instructions: Adjust a cable pulley so that it is at the bottom of its runner with a handle attached. Stand with your right side adjacent to the runner and take hold of the handle with an underhand grip in your left hand. Bend at your knees and waist (more so at your waist) whilst maintaining a straight back so that your torso is just off parallel with the floor. Your right arm should be in a neutral position so that it does not interfere with the movement. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the back of your left hand up and out, on a wide arcing motion, away from the pulley and towards the ceiling. Continue to pull until your arm is parallel to the floor. To do so, use the muscles in the back of your shoulder joint and across the top of your back. Tip: Maintain your posture throughout.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the handle along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Rear Delt Machine Flyes or Face Pulls.

name: Standing Alternating Cable Shoulder Press
instructions: Attach handles to two low cables. Take one in each hand and face away from the machine. Bring the handles up to shoulder height with your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance, maintaining a straight back troughout the movement. Your palms should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press one of the handles towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight). Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, return the handle back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Alternating Shoulder Press or See-Saw Press.

name: Bodyweight Flyes
instructions: Position two equally loaded barbells on the ground next to each other. Ensure they are able to roll.
Assume a push-up position over the bars, supporting your weight on your toes and hands with your arms extended and body straight.
Place your hands on the bars. This will be your starting position.
Using a slow and controlled motion, move your hands away from the midline of your body, rolling the bars apart. Inhale during this portion of the motion.
After moving the bars as far apart as you can, return to the starting position by pulling them back together. Exhale as you perform this movement.

name: Wrist Rotations with Straight Bar
instructions: Hold a barbell with both hands and your palms facing down; hands spaced about shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
Alternating between each of your hands, perform the movement by extending the wrist as though you were rolling up a newspaper. Continue alternating back and forth until failure.
Reverse the motion by flexing the wrist, rolling the opposite direction. Continue the alternating motion until failure.

en: forearms
Hold a barbell with both hands and your palms facing down; hands spaced about shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
Alternating between each of your hands, perform the movement by extending the wrist as though you were rolling up a newspaper. Continue alternating back and forth until failure.
Reverse the motion by flexing the wrist, rolling the opposite direction. Continue the alternating motion until failure.

name: Sumo Stiff Legged Deadlifts
instructions: Place a loaded barbell in front of you and adopt a wide stance. Have your shins gently touching the barbell, lean forward from your hips to take an overhand, narrow grip (index fingers 6-12 inches apart). Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor, a slight bend at your knees and remember to maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, exhale as you drive through your heels to elevate the barbell. You should be pulling through your hamstrings and driving your hips forward at the same time to lift the bar up into a standing position.

After a brief pause in this standing position, lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the barbell close to you as you go. Push your glutes back to help lower the bar towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Inhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Deadlifts.

name: Wide-Stance Barbell Squat
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across your shoulders whilst maintaining a straight back and a small bend at your knees. Press through your legs to lift the bar and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads and glutes to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Dumbbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Wide-Grip Decline Barbell Pullover
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with both legs securely locked in position.  Reach for the barbell behind the head using a pronated grip (palms facing out).  Make sure to grab the barbell wider than shoulder width apart for this exercise.  Slowly lift the barbell up from the floor by using your arms.
When positioned properly, your arms should be fully extended and perpendicular to the floor.  This is the starting position.

Begin by moving the barbell back down in a semicircular motion as if you were going to place it on the floor, but instead, stop when the arms are parallel to the floor.  Tip: Keep the arms fully extended at all times.  The movement should only happen at the shoulder joint.  Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Now bring the barbell up while exhaling until you are back at the starting position.  Remember to keep full control of the barbell at all times.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions of your training program.
When finished with your set, slowly lower the barbell back down until it is level with your head and release it.
Caution: It is a good idea to have a spotter for this exercise.
Variations: You can also use an EZ bar or dumbbells to perform this movement.
